Then He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were focused on Him.
He began speaking to them <sup>[saying]</sup>, “Today this <sup>[passage of]</sup> Scripture has been fulfilled as you listened to it.”
So, everyone spoke well of Him and marveled at the gracious words coming from His mouth. They said, “Is he not Joseph’s son?”
And He replied to them, “No doubt you will tell me this proverb, ‘Doctor, heal yourself,’ and ‘Perform <sup>[the miracles]</sup> here in your own home town also that we heard you did in Capernaum.’”
And He said, “Truly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his own home town.

But it is true when I tell you, <sup>[while]</sup> there were many widows <sup>[living]</sup> in Israel during the time of Elijah, when it did not rain for three and a half years, causing a great famine over the entire land,
Elijah was not sent to any of them, except to a widow in Zarephath <sup>[i.e., a Gentile town]</sup> in the region of Sidon.

And <sup>[while]</sup> there were many people with infectious skin diseases <sup>[living]</sup> in Israel during the time of Elisha the prophet, none of them was healed except Naaman, the Syrian <sup>[who was also a Gentile]</sup>.”
Then everyone in the synagogue became extremely angry when they heard these things.
They rose up and threw Jesus out of the city, leading Him to the top of the hill on which the city was built, in order to throw Him down <sup>[from a cliff]</sup>.
But He <sup>[just]</sup> walked right through them <sup>[miraculously (?)]</sup> and went on His way.
Then He went down to the Galilean city of Capernaum and taught the Jews on the Sabbath day.
They were amazed at His teaching for He spoke with authority.

Now there was a man in the synagogue who was dominated by the spirit of an evil demon <sup>[Note: These “evil spirits” or “demons” were powerful beings sent by Satan to inhabit people, causing physical, mental and spiritual harm to them]</sup>. He was shouting with a loud voice,
“Ha! What business do we have with you, Jesus from Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are; <sup>[you are]</sup> God’s Holy One.”

Then Jesus spoke sternly to the evil spirit <sup>[in the man]</sup>, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him.” And when the evil spirit had thrown the man down in front of them, it came out of him, without causing any harm.
And everyone became amazed and began saying to one another, “What is he saying? For with authority and power he orders the evil spirits to come out <sup>[of people]</sup>, and they do.”
Then a report about Him spread to the entire surrounding region.

So, He left the synagogue and entered Simon’s <sup>[i.e., Peter’s]</sup> house. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was <sup>[in bed]</sup> stricken with a high fever. And they <sup>[i.e., Andrew, James and John, besides Peter. See Mark 1:29]</sup> begged Him <sup>[to do something]</sup> for her.
So, He stood over her and spoke sternly to the fever. It left her and she got up <sup>[out of bed]</sup> and began serving them.
And when the sun was setting, everyone who had friends or relatives who were sick with various diseases brought them to Jesus and He placed His hands on each one of them and healed them.

And evil spirits also came out of many people, shouting <sup>[at Him]</sup>, “You are the Son of God.” And Jesus spoke sternly to them and would not allow them to speak <sup>[anymore]</sup> because they knew He was the Christ <sup>[Note: Jesus would not accept the endorsement of these Satanic spirits]</sup>.

And when it became daylight He left <sup>[Simon’s house]</sup> and went to a deserted place <sup>[i.e., to pray. See Mark 1:35]</sup>. The crowds were looking for Him and <sup>[upon finding Him]</sup> went to Him and tried to discourage Him from leaving them.
But He told them, “I must go to other towns also to preach the good news of God’s <sup>[coming]</sup> kingdom, for this is why I was sent <sup>[by God]</sup>.”
So, He <sup>[went and]</sup> preached in the <sup>[Jewish]</sup> synagogues of Judea <sup>[also]</sup>.
Now it happened while Jesus was standing on the shore of Lake Genneseret <sup>[i.e., Lake Galilee]</sup> that the crowd was pushing closer to Him to hear God’s message.
He saw two boats <sup>[tied up]</sup> along the shore of the lake, for the fishermen had left them <sup>[there]</sup> and were washing their nets <sup>[i.e., on the beach]</sup>.
So, He entered one of the boats that belonged to Simon <sup>[i.e., Peter]</sup>, and asked him to launch out a short distance from shore. Then He sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
And when He had finished talking, He said to Simon, “Launch out into deeper water and lower your nets for a catch <sup>[of fish]</sup>.”
Simon replied, “Master, we worked all night, but did not catch anything. But I will lower the nets if you say so.”
And when they had done this, they gathered in a large number of fish <sup>[until]</sup> their nets began to break.
So, they motioned to their partners in the other boat to come and help them. When they came, they filled both boats until they began to sink.
But when Simon Peter saw this, he fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Go away from me, Lord, because I am a sinful man.”
For he and those who were with him were amazed at the <sup>[huge]</sup> quantity of fish they had caught.
Simon’s partners James and John, sons of Zebedee, were also amazed. So, Jesus said to Simon, “Do not be afraid; from now on you will catch people.”
And when they had brought their boats to shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.

And it happened while Jesus was in one of the towns <sup>[i.e., surrounding Lake Galilee]</sup>, that a man covered with an infectious skin disease appeared and, when he saw Jesus, he fell on his face <sup>[to the ground]</sup> and begged Him, saying, “If you want to, you can heal me.” <sup>[Note: Throughout this narrative the terms “heal/healing” are originally “clean/cleansing” because the Jews viewed a person with such a disease as ceremonially “unclean.” See Lev. 13:1-3]</sup>.
Then He reached out His hand and touched the man, saying, “I <sup>[do]</sup> want to; be healed.” And immediately the infectious skin disease left him.

Then He ordered the man not to tell anyone <sup>[about the healing]</sup>, saying, “Go on your way and show yourself to the priest and offer the proper sacrifice for your healing that Moses required <sup>[Lev. 13:49; 14:2ff]</sup> as evidence to people <sup>[that you were healed]</sup>.”
But the news about what Jesus had done spread all the more widely, so that large crowds assembled to listen to Him and to be healed of their sicknesses.
So, He went away to deserted places and prayed.

And it happened on one of those days <sup>[in Capernaum. See Mark 2:1]</sup>, as Jesus was teaching, that some Pharisees <sup>[i.e., a strict sect of the Jewish religion]</sup> and teachers of the Law of Moses, who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem, were sitting around <sup>[i.e., listening to Him]</sup>. And the power of the Lord was with Him, enabling Him to heal people.
Just then <sup>[four]</sup> men <sup>[See Mark 2:3]</sup> brought a paralyzed man on a cot and attempted to place him in front of Jesus.

But because they could not find a <sup>[suitable]</sup> way to get him in <sup>[to the house]</sup> because of the people crowded <sup>[around]</sup>, they went up to the housetop <sup>[Note: This was a flat area, where people retired for rest, prayer, etc., with a stairway accessible from the outside]</sup> and lowered the man on his cot through the roof tiles into the presence of Jesus.
And when He saw the evidence of their faith, He said, “Man, your sins are forgiven.”

Then the experts in the Law of Moses and the Pharisees began to reason <sup>[in their minds]</sup>, saying, “Who is this man who speaks things against God <sup>[i.e., by claiming the ability to forgive sins]</sup>? Who can forgive sins except God only?”
But Jesus perceived their reasonings and answered them, “Why are you reasoning in your hearts?
Which is easier, to say <sup>[to the paralyzed man]</sup>, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
But so you will know that the Son of man has authority on earth to forgive sins, (He then said to the paralyzed man), ‘I say to you, get up, pick up your cot and go home.’”
So, immediately he stood up in front of them and picked up what he had been lying on <sup>[i.e., his cot]</sup>, and went home, giving honor to God.
And everyone was filled with amazement; they gave honor to God and were overwhelmed with reverence, saying, “We have seen incredible things today.”

Then after these things happened, Jesus went out <sup>[i.e., probably walking along a street]</sup> and saw a publican <sup>[Note: This was a person with a bad reputation for dishonest tax collecting activities]</sup> named Matthew <sup>[i.e., the same person as Levi. See Mark 2:14]</sup> sitting at the toll booth <sup>[collecting taxes]</sup> and said, “Become my follower.”
So, Matthew gave up everything, got up and followed Jesus.
Then Levi prepared a lavish dinner for Jesus at his house. A large number of <sup>[other]</sup> tax collectors were <sup>[also]</sup> having dinner with them.
Now the Pharisees and their experts in the Law of Moses complained to Jesus’ disciples, saying, “Why do you men eat and drink with tax collectors and worldly people?”
And Jesus answered them, “People who are healthy do not need a doctor, but <sup>[only]</sup> sick people do.
I did not come to call those who are doing right to repent <sup>[i.e., to change their hearts and lives]</sup>, but <sup>[only those who are]</sup> sinners.”

Then they said to Jesus, “John’s <sup>[the Immerser’s]</sup> disciples fast frequently <sup>[i.e., going without food and/or drink for religious reasons]</sup> and they offer earnest prayer requests, and the disciples of the Pharisees do the same things. But your disciples eat and drink.”
Jesus answered them, “Can you force the companions of the groom to fast while the groom is <sup>[still]</sup> with them?
But the time will come when the groom will be taken away from them <sup>[Note: Jesus here refers to Himself]</sup>. Then they will fast at that time.”

And He told them a parable <sup>[i.e., a brief story to illustrate His teaching]</sup>: “No one tears a piece <sup>[of cloth]</sup> from a new item of clothing and sews it on an old one. If he does, it will tear the new one. And besides, the piece <sup>[of cloth]</sup> from the new clothing will not match the old one.

And no one puts freshly squeezed grape juice into previously used bottles made of animal skins. <sup>[If he does]</sup>, the newly squeezed juice will <sup>[crack and]</sup> burst the <sup>[dried out]</sup> animal skins and <sup>[all the juice will]</sup> spill out, and the animal skin bottles will <sup>[also]</sup> become useless.
But freshly squeezed grape juice must be put into newly made animal skins.
And no one who has drunk aged wine wants newly squeezed <sup>[juice]</sup>, for he says, ‘The older <sup>[grape juice]</sup> is better.’”

Now it happened on a Sabbath day that Jesus was walking through some grain fields. His disciples picked off some heads of grain, rubbed them in their hands and ate <sup>[the kernels]</sup>. <sup>[Note: It was an accepted practice for people in that day to be permitted to do this as they walked along paths bordering a grain field]</sup>.
But certain Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is against the Law of Moses on the Sabbath day?”
And Jesus answered them, “Have you never read about what King David did when he and his men were hungry <sup>[I Sam. 21:6]</sup>;

how he entered the house of God <sup>[i.e., the Temple]</sup> and took some ‘Bread of Presence’ and ate it, and also gave some to his men to eat? <sup>[This practice]</sup> was not permissible according to the Law of Moses, <sup>[since the bread was reserved]</sup> only for the priests.”
Then He said to them, “The Son of man is lord <sup>[i.e., has authority over]</sup> the Sabbath day.”
And it happened on another Sabbath day, when Jesus entered the synagogue to teach, that a man was there whose right hand was deformed.
And the experts in the Law of Moses were watching Him to see if He would heal <sup>[someone]</sup> on the Sabbath day, so they could figure out how to accuse Him.
But He knew what they were thinking. Then He said to the man with the deformed hand, “Get up, step forward in front of everyone.” So, he got up and stepped forward.
Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, is it permitted by the Law of Moses to do something good or to do something harmful on the Sabbath day? To save a life or to destroy it?”
Then He looked around at all of them <sup>[there]</sup> and said to the man, “Reach out your hand.” And <sup>[when]</sup> he did this, his hand was restored <sup>[to normal use]</sup>.
But the experts in the Law of Moses and the Pharisees became furious and began discussing among themselves what they could do to Jesus.
And it happened during that time that Jesus went out to the mountain <sup>[i.e., probably a hillside near Capernaum]</sup> to pray, and continued praying to God all night.
And when it was daylight, He called His disciples and chose from among them twelve, whom He also called apostles.
<sup>[They were]</sup> Simon, also called Peter, and his brother Andrew, James and John <sup>[also brothers]</sup>, Philip, Bartholomew <sup>[i.e., the person same as Nathaniel. See John 1:45]</sup>,

Matthew <sup>[i.e., the same person as Levi. See Mark 2:14]</sup>, Thomas <sup>[i.e., the same person as Didymus. See John 11:16]</sup>, James, the son of Alphaeus, Simon, called the Zealot <sup>[i.e., the same person as the Cananaean. See Matt. 10:4]</sup>,
Judas, the son of James <sup>[i.e., the same person as Thaddaeus. See Matt. 10:3]</sup> and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor <sup>[to Jesus]</sup>.

Then He came down <sup>[from the mountain]</sup> with them and stood on a level place <sup>[where]</sup> a large crowd of His disciples and a large group of people had gathered to hear Him and be healed from their diseases.
And <sup>[also]</sup> those who were tormented by evil spirits were healed.
Then the whole crowd tried to touch Him because <sup>[supernatural]</sup> power was coming from His body, and He healed all of them.
Then He looked at His disciples and said, “Those of you who have a <sup>[sense of personal]</sup> need are blessed, because the kingdom of God belongs to you.

Those of you who now have an appetite <sup>[i.e., for doing what is right]</sup> are blessed, because you will be satisfied <sup>[eventually]</sup>. Those of you who cry now <sup>[i.e., over your sins]</sup> are blessed, because you will laugh <sup>[i.e., when you are forgiven later]</sup>.
You are blessed when men hate you and withdraw their fellowship from you and speak abusively about you and reject your name as evil, <sup>[all]</sup> for being loyal to the Son of man.
Be happy at that time and jump for joy and realize that your reward in heaven will be great, for the forefathers <sup>[i.e., of your corrupt Jewish leaders]</sup> treated the prophets the same way.
But it is too bad for you rich people because you have <sup>[already]</sup> received <sup>[all]</sup> the comforts <sup>[you are going to get]</sup>.

It is too bad for you people who have plenty to eat now, because you will be hungry <sup>[later on]</sup>. It is too bad for you people who are laughing now, because you will grieve and cry <sup>[later on]</sup>.
It is too bad for you people when everyone speaks well about you, because the forefathers said the same thing about the false prophets.
“But I tell you people who are listening <sup>[to me]</sup>, love your enemies and do good things to those who hate you.
<sup>[Ask God’s]</sup> blessing on those who curse you; pray for those who treat you spitefully.

Whoever hits you on one side of the face, allow him <sup>[to hit you on]</sup> the other side also; and when someone takes away your coat, do not stop him from also taking away your shirt <sup>[i.e., if he insists on it]</sup>.
Give to everyone who asks you <sup>[for something]</sup> and do not demand that he bring back your possessions that he takes away.
And whatever you would like people to do to you, do the same thing to them.
And if you love <sup>[only]</sup> those who love you, what thanks do you deserve for doing that? For even worldly people love those who love them.
And if you do good things <sup>[only]</sup> to those who do good things to you, what thanks do you deserve for doing that? For even worldly people do that much.

And if you lend something <sup>[only]</sup> to those people from whom you expect to have it returned, what thanks do you deserve for doing that? Even worldly people lend to worldly people expecting to get the same things back.

But love your enemies and do good things to them; and lend to them without expecting it to be returned, and your reward <sup>[i.e., from God]</sup> will be great and you will be <sup>[considered]</sup> sons of the Highest <sup>[i.e., God]</sup>. For He is kind to unthankful and evil people <sup>[as well]</sup>.
You people should be merciful just like your Father is merciful.

And do not pass <sup>[hypocritical]</sup> judgment <sup>[on other people. See Matt. 7:1-5]</sup> and you will not receive such judgment <sup>[on yourselves]</sup>. And do not condemn <sup>[others]</sup> and you will not be condemned <sup>[yourselves]</sup>. Release people <sup>[i.e., from guilt for offending you]</sup> and you will be released <sup>[i.e., by God (?)]</sup>

Give <sup>[to others]</sup> and you will have <sup>[things]</sup> given to you. People will pour into your lap a full measure <sup>[of goods]</sup>; it will be pressed down, shaken together and running over <sup>[the edges]</sup>. Because the standard you use in giving <sup>[to others]</sup> will be the standard used <sup>[by them or God]</sup> in giving back to you.”
Then Jesus told them a parable <sup>[i.e., a brief story to illustrate His teaching]</sup>: “Can a blind person lead a blind person? Will not both of them fall into a ditch?
The disciple is not more important than his teacher, but when every person becomes complete, <sup>[then]</sup> he will be like his teacher.
And why do you look for the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye but ignore the board in your own eye?

Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Brother, let me take that speck of sawdust out of your eye,’ when you cannot see the board in your own eye? You hypocrite, take the board out of your own eye first; then you will be able to see clearly enough to take out the speck of sawdust that is in your brother’s eye.
For there is no healthy tree that produces bad fruit; nor is there a diseased tree that produces wholesome fruit.
For each tree is recognized by its own fruit. For people do not harvest figs from thorn bushes, nor grapes from a brier bush.

The good person produces good things from the goodness stored up in his heart. And the evil person produces bad things from the evil <sup>[stored up in his heart]</sup>. For his mouth speaks what his heart is filled with.
“And why do you call me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and <sup>[yet]</sup> not obey what I tell you to do?
Every person who comes to me and listens to my words and obeys them, I will show you who he is like.
He is like a man building a house, who dug deep to lay the foundation on rock. And when a flood came the river pounded against that house, but could not shake it because it had been well built.

But the person who hears <sup>[my words]</sup> and does not obey them is like a man who built a house on the ground, without a foundation. <sup>[When]</sup> the river pounded against it, immediately it collapsed and the destruction of that house was complete.”
After Jesus had finished telling the people all He had to say, He went into Capernaum.
And a certain military officer’s slave <sup>[was there]</sup>, whom the officer highly valued. This slave was <sup>[so]</sup> sick that he was about to die.
And when the officer heard about Jesus, he sent <sup>[some]</sup> Jewish elders to Him asking that He come and heal his slave.
And when they came to Jesus, they begged Him earnestly, saying, “This officer deserves that you should do this for him <sup>[i.e., heal his slave]</sup>,
for he loves our nation and he is the one who built the synagogue for us.”

So, Jesus went with them. And when He got close to the military officer’s house, the officer sent <sup>[his]</sup> friends to Jesus, saying to Him <sup>[i.e., speaking on behalf of the military officer]</sup>, “Sir, do not trouble yourself, for I do not deserve for you to come into my house;
<sup>[and]</sup> for the same reason I did not think I deserved to come to you either. So, <sup>[just]</sup> say the word and <sup>[I know]</sup> my slave will be healed.

For I also am a man <sup>[who was]</sup> placed under the authority <sup>[of others]</sup>, and I have soldiers <sup>[who serve]</sup> under me. I can say to this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another one, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”

And when Jesus heard these things, He was amazed and said to the crowd that was following Him, “I tell you, I have never found <sup>[anyone with]</sup> such great faith, not <sup>[even]</sup> among the Israelites.”
And those who were sent <sup>[i.e., the officer’s friends. See verse 6]</sup> returned to the house and found the slave completely well.

And it happened soon after this that Jesus went to a town called Nain <sup>[Note: This was a town of Galilee about 6 miles southeast of Nazareth]</sup>. His disciples and a large crowd <sup>[also]</sup> went with Him.
Now when He approached the town gate, He saw a dead man being carried out; it was the only son of his widowed mother. And many people from the town were with her.
Now when the Lord saw her, He felt very sorry for her and said to her, “Do not cry.”
Then He went near the casket and touched it; <sup>[immediately]</sup> the pallbearers stopped. Jesus said, “Young man, I tell you, get up.”
And the dead man sat up and began talking. And Jesus gave him <sup>[back]</sup> to his mother <sup>[alive]</sup>.
Then everyone was gripped with fear and gave honor to God, saying, “<sup>[Surely]</sup> a great prophet has arisen among us and God’s <sup>[presence]</sup> has come to His people.”
And the report of this <sup>[incident]</sup> about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the entire surrounding district.
Then the disciples of John <sup>[the Immerser]</sup> told him all about these things <sup>[i.e., what Jesus was doing]</sup>.
So, John called to him two of his disciples and sent them to the Lord to ask, “Are you the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?”
And when these men came to Jesus they said, “John the Immerser has sent us to you to ask, ‘Are you the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?’”
At that very time Jesus was healing many people from diseases, those plagued with serious illnesses and those dominated by evil spirits. He <sup>[also]</sup> restored sight to many blind people.

So, Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things you have seen and heard; <sup>[how]</sup> blind people are receiving back their sight, and crippled people are walking <sup>[again]</sup>, and people with infectious skin diseases are being healed, and deaf-mutes are hearing <sup>[and speaking (?) again]</sup>, and people are being raised <sup>[from the dead]</sup>, and poor people are having the good news preached to them.
The person who does not find an occasion for having doubts about my identity is fortunate indeed.”
And when the messengers sent by John left, Jesus began saying to the crowds about John, “What did you expect to see when you went out into the desert? A tall stem swaying in the wind?
What did you expect to see? A man dressed in fancy clothes? Look, people who wear elaborate outfits and bask in luxury live in kings’ palaces.
But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I will say <sup>[he is]</sup>, and much more than a prophet.
He is the one about whom it was written <sup>[Mal. 3:1]</sup>, ‘Look, I <sup>[will]</sup> send my messenger on ahead of you. He will prepare the pathway in front of you.’
And I tell you, among <sup>[all]</sup> those born of women, there is no one greater than John <sup>[the Immerser]</sup>, yet the least significant person in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
And when all the people, together with the tax collectors, heard this they praised God for being so just, and were immersed with the immersion <sup>[preached]</sup> by John.
But the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, who would not be immersed by John, rejected God’s purpose for themselves.
“What then should I compare the people of this generation with?” <sup>[Jesus asked]</sup>. “And what are they like?

They are like children sitting in the open shopping market, calling to one another, saying, ‘We <sup>[pretended we]</sup> were making music <sup>[at a wedding]</sup> but you did not dance; we <sup>[pretended we]</sup> were wailing <sup>[at a funeral]</sup> but you did not cry <sup>[along with us]</sup>.’
For when John the Immerser came, he did not eat bread or drink wine <sup>[on festive occasions]</sup> and you said, ‘He is dominated by an evil spirit.’
<sup>[Then]</sup> the Son of man came eating and drinking <sup>[at festive occasions]</sup> and you said, ‘Look, he is a glutton and a drunkard, and a friend of tax collectors and worldly people.’
It proves that <sup>[God’s]</sup> wise counsel is right in the eyes of all of her children <sup>[i.e., in the eyes of people known for their wisdom]</sup>.”
Then one of the Pharisees invited Jesus to have a meal with him. So, He went to the Pharisee’s house and reclined at the <sup>[dinner]</sup> table. <sup>[Note: Matt. 23:6]</sup>.
And when a sinful woman from the town learned that He was having dinner at the Pharisee’s house, she brought an alabaster <sup>[i.e., stone]</sup> jar of liquid perfume,
and stood behind Jesus, at His feet, crying. She began wetting His feet with her tears and wiping them dry with the hair of her head. Then she kissed His feet and poured the perfume on them.
Now when the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would have known who touched him and what kind of a woman she was --- a sinner.”
Then Jesus said to him, “Simon <sup>[See verse 43]</sup>, I have something to say to you.” And he replied, “Teacher, <sup>[go ahead and]</sup> say it.”

<sup>[Jesus then gave this parable]</sup>: “A certain moneylender had two men who owed him money; one owed him five hundred coins <sup>[Note: The total amount was 500 twelve-hour days of a farm laborer’s pay, or about $54,000 in 2005]</sup>, and the other man <sup>[owed him]</sup> fifty coins <sup>[Note: About $5,400 in 2005]</sup>.
When these two men did not have <sup>[the money]</sup> to repay him, the moneylender canceled <sup>[the debts of]</sup> both of them. Now which of them will love him more?”
Simon answered, “The one who had the largest debt canceled, I suppose.” Jesus said to him, “You are right.”

Then Jesus turned to the woman <sup>[who had just poured the perfume on Him]</sup> and said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I came into your house and you did not give me any water for <sup>[washing]</sup> my feet, but she wet my feet with her tears and wiped them dry with her hair.
You did not give me a kiss <sup>[of greeting]</sup>, but from the time I came in <sup>[to your house]</sup> she has not stopped kissing my feet.
You did not pour <sup>[aromatic]</sup> oil on my head, but she poured perfume on my feet.

For this reason, I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven, for she has loved much. <sup>[Note: This suggests that her forgiveness was the result of her loving, but the point Jesus makes better fits the idea that her forgiveness produced love]</sup>. But the person who is forgiven of little <sup>[i.e., of only a few sins]</sup>, loves <sup>[only]</sup> a little bit.”
Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
And those who were reclining at the <sup>[dinner]</sup> table with Him began saying among themselves, “Who is this person who can even forgive sins?”
Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you <sup>[i.e., from condemnation]</sup>. Go <sup>[away]</sup> in peace.”

And it happened soon after this that Jesus went through one town and village after another, preaching and proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God. <sup>[Traveling]</sup> with Him were the twelve apostles

and certain women who had been healed from <sup>[domination by]</sup> evil spirits and diseases. <sup>[These women were]</sup> Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven evil spirits had been driven out,
and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod’s property manager, and Susanna and many others. These <sup>[all]</sup> had provided the material support for the group from their own means.
Then when a large crowd assembled and people from every town were coming to Jesus, He told them this parable <sup>[i.e., a brief story to illustrate His teaching]</sup>:

“A farmer went out <sup>[to his field]</sup> to plant grain. And as he sowed <sup>[i.e., scattering seeds by hand]</sup>, some of it landed along side of the road; it was stepped on by people and <sup>[eventually]</sup> birds of the sky came and ate it.
And other <sup>[seed]</sup> fell on rocky ground and as soon as it started to grow, it <sup>[quickly]</sup> withered away, because it had no moisture.
And other <sup>[seed]</sup> fell among thorns, which grew up with it and <sup>[eventually]</sup> choked out <sup>[the tender sprouts]</sup>.

And <sup>[still]</sup> other <sup>[seed]</sup> fell into fertile soil; it grew and produced a crop of one hundred times <sup>[as much as was planted]</sup>.” As He said these things, He called out; “The person who has ears to hear with ought to listen <sup>[to this]</sup> carefully.”
Then Jesus’ disciples asked Him what this parable meant.

And He said to them, “You <sup>[disciples]</sup> are being given an understanding of the secrets of the kingdom of God, but the rest of the people <sup>[are being told]</sup> in parables, so that <sup>[even though they appear to be]</sup> seeing, they will not <sup>[actually]</sup> see; and <sup>[even though they seem to]</sup> hear, they will not understand.
Now <sup>[the meaning of]</sup> the parable is this: The seed represents the word of God.

And those people <sup>[represented by the seed falling]</sup> along side of the road are those who have heard the word <sup>[of God]</sup>, but the devil comes and takes away the word from their heart, so that they do not believe it and receive salvation.

And those people <sup>[represented by the seed falling]</sup> on rocky ground are those who, upon hearing the word <sup>[of God]</sup>, accept it gladly, but since they have no root <sup>[system]</sup>, they believe it for <sup>[only]</sup> awhile, and <sup>[then]</sup> in times of trial fall away <sup>[from God]</sup>.

And those people <sup>[represented by the seed]</sup> that fell among thorns are the ones who have heard <sup>[the word]</sup>, and as they go about their business, they become choked by worry, by money and by worldly pleasures, and produce no mature crop.

And those people <sup>[represented by seed falling]</sup> in the fertile soil are those who possess a good and honest heart when they hear the word <sup>[of God]</sup>. They hold on to it and patiently <sup>[continue until they]</sup> produce a crop.

“And no one lights an <sup>[olive oil]</sup> lamp and then covers it with a basket or puts it under a bed. But he places it on a stand so that those entering <sup>[the house]</sup> will see <sup>[by]</sup> the light.
For there is nothing done in obscurity that will not be made known <sup>[openly]</sup>, and nothing done secretly that will not <sup>[eventually]</sup> become known and be revealed.

Pay attention to how you listen, for to the person who <sup>[already]</sup> has something, <sup>[more]</sup> will be given, but from the person who has <sup>[virtually]</sup> nothing, even what he thinks he has will be taken away from him.”
Then Jesus’ mother and <sup>[half-]</sup> brothers came to Him, but they could not get near Him because of the <sup>[large]</sup> crowd.
And it was told to Him <sup>[by someone]</sup>, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside wanting to see you.”
But He replied to them, “My mother and my brothers are those people who hear God’s word and obey it.”

Now it happened on one of those days that Jesus entered a boat with His disciples. He said to them, “Let us go over to the other side” <sup>[i.e., the east side of Lake Galilee]</sup>; so they launched out.
But while they were sailing, Jesus fell asleep. A windstorm blew down on the lake and <sup>[the boat]</sup> began filling with water so they were in great danger <sup>[of sinking]</sup>.

The disciples approached Jesus and woke Him, shouting, “Master, Master, we are sinking.” And He woke up and spoke sternly to the wind and the raging water; <sup>[then]</sup> the storm <sup>[immediately]</sup> stopped and <sup>[everything]</sup> became calm.

Then He said to the disciples, “Where is your faith?” They became afraid and were amazed, saying to one another, “Who can this <sup>[man]</sup> be, that he can even command the winds and the water and they obey him?”

<sup>[Finally]</sup> they arrived at the district of the Gerasenes, which is opposite <sup>[i.e., across the lake from the province of]</sup> Galilee. <sup>[Note: Matt. 8:28 says ‘Gadara.’ Geresa and Gadara were two towns about twelve miles apart in the same region east of Lake Galilee]</sup>.

And when He stepped out <sup>[of the boat]</sup> onto the shore, He was met by a certain man from the town, who was dominated by evil spirits. He had not worn any clothes for a long time and lived in the graveyard instead of a house.

And when he saw Jesus, he fell at His feet <sup>[i.e., in worship. See Mark 5:6]</sup> and shouted out with a loud voice saying, “What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Highest God? I beg you, do not torture me.”

For Jesus was commanding the evil spirits to be driven out of the man, because they had been controlling him for a long time. The man was continually being guarded and his hands and feet were bound with chains. <sup>[Sometimes]</sup> he broke loose from the chains and was driven into the desert by the evil spirits.

Then Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” And he answered, “ <sup>[My name is]</sup> ‘Legion’” <sup>[Note: This word means a number consisting of between 5,000 and 6,000]</sup>, for many evil spirits had entered the man.
And the evil spirits begged Jesus not to require them to go away into the “pit” <sup>[Note: This appears to be the place reserved for the devil and his evil spirits. See Rev. 9:1-11; 20:1-3]</sup>.

Now a herd of many <sup>[wild (?)]</sup> hogs was grazing on a <sup>[nearby]</sup> mountain, so the evil spirits begged Jesus to give them permission to enter <sup>[the bodies of]</sup> the hogs. So, He gave them permission <sup>[to do it]</sup>.
Then the evil spirits went out of the man and entered the hogs. The herd <sup>[immediately]</sup> rushed down the cliff into the lake and was drowned.
And when those who had been grazing the hogs saw what had happened, they ran and told it in the town and <sup>[around]</sup> the country.

People went out to see <sup>[i.e., to find out]</sup> what had happened. And <sup>[when]</sup> they came to Jesus and found the man from whom the evil spirits had been driven out sitting down at Jesus’ feet with his clothes on and perfectly sane, they were afraid.
Those who saw this told others how the man dominated by evil spirits was restored.
Then all the people around the district of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were gripped with great fear. So, He entered a boat and returned.
But the man from whom the evil spirits had been driven out begged Jesus to allow him to travel with Him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,

“Return to your house and tell people about the great things God has done for you.” So, he went on his way, proclaiming throughout the whole town <sup>[i.e., Geresa. See verse 26]</sup> the great things Jesus had done for him.
Then as Jesus returned <sup>[i.e., to the northwest shore of the lake, probably to the vicinity of Capernaum]</sup>, a crowd was waiting for Him and welcomed Him.
And just then an official of the <sup>[Jewish]</sup> synagogue named Jairus came to Him and fell at His feet <sup>[i.e., worshiping. See Matt. 9:18]</sup> and begged Him to go to his house,
for the official’s only daughter, who was about twelve years old, was dying. As Jesus was going, large crowds pressed in on Him.
Then a woman, who had been bleeding for twelve years, and who had spent all her income on doctors without being healed by any of them,
came up behind Jesus and touched the edge of His robe. Immediately her bleeding stopped.
Jesus said, “Who touched me?” And when everyone denied <sup>[doing it]</sup>, Peter and the disciples said, “Master, many people are pressing in on you and crowding you.”
But Jesus replied, “Someone did touch me, for I perceived that <sup>[healing]</sup> power had gone out from me.”

And when the woman realized that she had not escaped notice, she came trembling <sup>[with fear]</sup> and fell down in front of Him, declaring before all the people why she had touched Him and how she had been immediately healed.
Then Jesus said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace.”
While He was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue official’s house, saying <sup>[to him]</sup>, “Your daughter is dead; do not bother the Teacher.”

But when Jesus heard this, He said <sup>[to the man]</sup>, “Do not be afraid. Just believe <sup>[i.e., in my power to restore your daughter]</sup> and she will be made well <sup>[i.e., restored to life]</sup>.”
And when Jesus came to the <sup>[synagogue official’s]</sup> house, He did not allow anyone to enter with Him except Peter, James, John and the girl’s father and mother.

And all the people <sup>[in the house]</sup> were crying and beating their chests in mourning, so Jesus said <sup>[to them]</sup>, “Do not cry, for the girl is not <sup>[really]</sup> dead but <sup>[only]</sup> sleeping.”
And they laughed at Him scornfully, knowing that she was <sup>[already]</sup> dead.
But taking her by the hand, He called to her, saying, “Child, get up.”
And immediately her spirit returned and she stood up. Then Jesus ordered them to give her something to eat.
Her parents were amazed, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
Jesus then called the twelve apostles together and gave them power and authority over all evil spirits and <sup>[the ability]</sup> to heal diseases.
And He sent them out to preach the <sup>[coming]</sup> kingdom of God and to heal sick people.

He said to them, “Do not take anything with you during your travels; not <sup>[even]</sup> a walking stick <sup>[Note: By comparing this and the restriction in Matt. 10:10 with the permission given in Mark 6:8, the harmony seems to be “if you do not already have a walking stick, do not get one”]</sup>, or a traveling bag <sup>[for personal belongings]</sup>, or food, or money, or <sup>[even]</sup> two coats <sup>[Note: Mark 6:9 says, “take only one coat” confirming the idea that Jesus was saying, in effect, “do not take anything extra, go just as you are”]</sup>.
And whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that place.

And those people who do not welcome you, shake the dust off of your shoes as evidence against them.” <sup>[Note: This was a Jewish custom showing disdain, and intended to suggest their unworthiness]</sup>
So, they left and went throughout the villages preaching the good news and healing people everywhere.

Now when King Herod, the tetrarch <sup>[i.e., governor of that district]</sup> heard about all that was happening <sup>[concerning Jesus]</sup>, he was perplexed because some people were saying that John <sup>[the Immerser]</sup> had risen from the dead;

some <sup>[were saying]</sup> that <sup>[the prophet]</sup> Elijah had appeared; and <sup>[still]</sup> others <sup>[were saying]</sup> that one of the <sup>[other]</sup> prophets of old had risen again <sup>[from the dead]</sup>.
Then Herod said, “I had John decapitated, so who is this person about whom I hear such things?” And he became eager to see Jesus.

When the apostles returned they told Jesus what they had done. Then Jesus took them and went away privately to a town called Bethsaida. <sup>[Note: This town was on the east side of Lake Galilee, and apparently was a different “Bethsaida” from the one mentioned in Mark 6:45]</sup>.

But the crowds found out about it <sup>[i.e., where Jesus had gone]</sup>, so they followed Him <sup>[there]</sup>. He welcomed them and spoke to them about the <sup>[coming]</sup> kingdom of God and cured those who needed healing.

Then late in the day the twelve apostles came and said to Jesus, “Send the crowd away so they can go into the villages and the surrounding countryside to find lodging and food because this place is deserted.”

But He said to the apostles, “You men give them something to eat.” But they replied, “We have only five loaves of <sup>[barley]</sup> bread <sup>[See John 6:9]</sup> and two <sup>[probably smoked]</sup> fish, unless we go and buy <sup>[more]</sup> food for all these people.”
For there were about five thousand men <sup>[there]</sup>. So, He said to His disciples, “Have these people recline <sup>[on the grass. See Matt. 14:19]</sup> in groups of about fifty each.”
So, the disciples did this, and had all the people recline <sup>[on the grass in groups]</sup>.

Then Jesus took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, asked God’s blessing on the food. <sup>[Then]</sup> He broke it <sup>[in pieces]</sup> and gave it to the disciples to distribute to the crowds.
They all ate until they were full, then gathered up twelve baskets <sup>[full]</sup> of the remaining broken pieces.
And it happened while Jesus was praying off by Himself that His disciples joined Him. He asked them, saying, “Who are the crowds saying that I am?”

And they answered, “<sup>[Some say you are]</sup> John the Immerser, while others say <sup>[the prophet]</sup> Elijah, and <sup>[still others say]</sup> that one of the <sup>[other]</sup> prophets of old has risen again <sup>[from the dead]</sup>.”
Then Jesus said to them, “But who do you men say that I am?” And Peter answered, “<sup>[You are]</sup> the Christ of God <sup>[i.e., God’s specially chosen one]</sup>.”
Then He sternly ordered them not to tell this to anyone,

saying, “The Son of man must suffer many things and be rejected by the <sup>[Jewish]</sup> elders, leading priests and experts in the Law of Moses. Then He will be killed and <sup>[yet]</sup> be raised up on the third day.”

Then He said to everyone, “If anyone wants to be my follower, he should deny self <sup>[i.e., of always having its own way]</sup> and accept his cross <sup>[i.e., his responsibilities, with all their difficulties]</sup> everyday, and <sup>[then he can]</sup> become my follower.

For whoever would <sup>[try to]</sup> save his life <sup>[i.e., by neglecting spiritual things]</sup> will lose it <sup>[i.e., miss out on the blessings of God]</sup>. But whoever <sup>[willingly]</sup> loses his life <sup>[i.e., in commitment to God’s service]</sup> for my sake, that person will save it <sup>[i.e., obtain both temporal and spiritual blessings]</sup>.

For what is a person benefited if he gains the whole world <sup>[i.e., of material things]</sup> and <sup>[yet]</sup> loses <sup>[his spiritual life]</sup> or gives himself up <sup>[to spiritual ruin]</sup>?
For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, the Son of man will <sup>[also]</sup> be ashamed of him when He returns in His own splendor and that of the Father and of the holy angels.

But I tell you the truth, there are some people standing here who will surely not experience death until they see that God’s kingdom has come <sup>[i.e., in a demonstration of power. See Mark 9:1]</sup>.”

And it happened about eight days after Jesus said these things that He took Peter, John and James with Him and went up in the mountain to pray. <sup>[Note: This was probably Mt. Tabor, which was nearby]</sup>.
And as He was praying, the appearance of His face was <sup>[miraculously]</sup> changed and His clothing became dazzling white.
Then suddenly two men were <sup>[seen]</sup> talking with Jesus. They were Moses and Elijah,
who appeared in splendor, and were speaking of Jesus’ <sup>[coming]</sup> departure <sup>[i.e., His imminent death]</sup>, which He was soon to experience at Jerusalem.
Now Peter and those with him <sup>[i.e., James and John]</sup> had been very sleepy, but became wide awake when they saw the splendor of Jesus and the two men standing with Him.

And it happened, as the two men were leaving Him, that Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is <sup>[so]</sup> good for us to be here. Let us make three <sup>[small]</sup> shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” <sup>[But]</sup> he did not realize what he was saying. <sup>[See note at Matt. 17:4]</sup>
And while he was speaking, a cloud appeared and engulfed them and the apostles were afraid as Moses and Elijah entered the cloud <sup>[i.e., and disappeared]</sup>.
Then a voice spoke out of the cloud, saying, “This is My Son, <sup>[whom]</sup> I have chosen; listen to Him.”
And after the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. But the apostles kept quiet <sup>[about this incident]</sup>, and did not tell anyone at that time about anything they had seen.
And it happened on the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, that a large crowd met Jesus.
Just then a man from the crowd shouted out, “Teacher, I beg you, look at my son <sup>[i.e., favorably]</sup>, for he is my only child.

For when an evil spirit takes hold of him he suddenly screams and <sup>[then]</sup> it throws him into convulsions so that he foams <sup>[at the mouth]</sup>. And it does not want to leave him, <sup>[even after]</sup> severely bruising him.
And I begged your disciples to drive it out, but they could not.”

And Jesus replied, saying, “You perverse generation of faithless people! How long must I be <sup>[patient]</sup> with you, and put up with you <sup>[i.e., before you understand]</sup>? Bring your son here.”

And while the boy was approaching <sup>[Jesus]</sup>, an evil spirit threw him to the ground in a convulsion. But Jesus spoke sternly to the evil spirit and <sup>[then]</sup> returned him to his father healed.
And everyone was amazed at <sup>[seeing]</sup> the greatness of God. But while they were all amazed at everything Jesus was doing, He said to His disciples,
“Pay close attention to what I am saying, for the Son of man will be handed over to <sup>[evil]</sup> men.”
But they did not understand what He was talking about, for it was hidden from them, so that they would not understand its meaning. And they were afraid to ask Him about what He had said.
Then a dispute developed among Jesus’ disciples as to which one of them was the greatest.
When Jesus knew what they were disputing about in their minds, He took a small child and placed him at His side,

and said to the disciples, “The person who welcomes this little child in my name <sup>[i.e., because he belongs to me]</sup>, welcomes me <sup>[also]</sup>. And the person who welcomes me <sup>[i.e., into his life (?)]</sup> welcomes God, who sent me. For that person who is the least <sup>[significant]</sup> among you men is the one who is the greatest <sup>[in importance]</sup>.”

Then <sup>[the apostle]</sup> John answered and said, “Master, we saw someone driving out evil spirits by <sup>[using]</sup> your name, so we told him not to, because he was not following <sup>[you]</sup> with us.”
But Jesus said to him; “Do not try to stop him, for the person who is not against you is for you.”
And it happened when the time came for Jesus to be taken up <sup>[to heaven]</sup> that He firmly determined to go to Jerusalem.
So, He sent messengers on ahead of Him, who went and entered a Samaritan village to prepare for Him <sup>[i.e., for His food and lodging]</sup>.
And these Samaritans did not welcome Him because He was heading for Jerusalem. <sup>[Note: They perceived He was a Jew and Samaritans did not associate with Jews]</sup>.
And when His disciples James and John saw this, they said <sup>[to Him]</sup>, “Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to burn them up?”
But He turned to them and spoke sternly. Some manuscripts add “You do not know what kind of a spirit you represent,
for the Son of man did not come to destroy men’s lives but to save them.” So, they went to another village.
And as they were going along the road, a certain man <sup>[i.e., an expert in the Law of Moses. See Matt. 8:19]</sup> said to Him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”

Jesus replied to him, “The foxes have dens <sup>[in which to live]</sup> and the birds of the sky have nests <sup>[in which to roost]</sup>, but the Son of man does not have anywhere to lay His head.”
And <sup>[then]</sup> He said to another person, “Become my follower.” But he replied, “Lord, allow me to go and bury my father first.”

But Jesus replied to him, “Let the <sup>[spiritually]</sup> dead bury their own dead <sup>[i.e., let them care for their family members until they die]</sup>, but you go and proclaim the <sup>[coming]</sup> kingdom of God.”

Then another person said <sup>[to Jesus]</sup>, “Lord, I will become your follower, but first let me say goodbye to my family.” <sup>[Note: There was the likelihood that family members might try to dissuade him from his commitment to follow the Lord]</sup>.

But Jesus said to him, “Nobody who begins plowing <sup>[a field]</sup> and then looks back <sup>[i.e., in regret for beginning such an arduous task]</sup> is suited for <sup>[serving in]</sup> the kingdom of God.”

Now after these things <sup>[happened]</sup> the Lord appointed seventy-two other disciples <sup>[Note: Some ancient manuscripts say “seventy.” This group would have been in addition to the twelve. See Luke 9:1]</sup>, and sent them two by two on ahead of Him into every town and locality that He planned to visit.

And He said to them, “There is certainly plenty to harvest, but there are <sup>[too]</sup> few people to do the work. You should pray to the Lord of the harvest to send <sup>[more]</sup> workers out into the field to gather His crop.
<sup>[So]</sup>, go on; See, I am sending you out as lambs among wolves.
Do not carry a money belt, or a traveling bag <sup>[for personal belongings]</sup> or shoes; and do not extend <sup>[elaborate]</sup> greetings to anyone along the way.
And whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Let peace be upon this household.’

And if a son of peace is there <sup>[i.e., a peace-loving person]</sup>, your <sup>[request for]</sup> peace will rest upon him. But if he is not <sup>[a peace-loving person]</sup>, your <sup>[blessing of]</sup> peace will return to <sup>[rest on]</sup> you.

And you should remain in that same house <sup>[i.e., the peace-loving one]</sup>, eating and drinking whatever they serve you, for the worker deserves his wages. Do not move from house to house <sup>[i.e., wasting time]</sup>.
And into whatever town you enter and are welcomed, eat whatever is placed in front of you,
and heal the sick people who live there. Say to them, ‘The kingdom of God has come near to you.’
But whatever town you enter that does not welcome you, go out into its streets and say <sup>[to them]</sup>,
‘We are wiping off even the dust of your city that has stuck to our feet <sup>[in protest]</sup> against you. Yet you should know this, that the kingdom of God has come near <sup>[to you]</sup>.’
I tell you, the people of Sodom will be shown more leniency on that day <sup>[i.e., the Day of Judgment]</sup> than that town will receive.

“It is too bad for you, Chorazin! It is too bad for you, Bethsaida! For if the powerful miracles had been performed in Tyre and Sidon which were performed in your presence, they would have repented long ago by sitting in ashes and wearing sackcloth. <sup>[Note: This sackcloth was a coarse cloth made of goat hair and indicated deep remorse or mourning]</sup>.
But Tyre and Sidon will be shown more leniency in the judgment <sup>[day]</sup> than you will.

And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up <sup>[as high]</sup> as heaven? <sup>[Certainly not]</sup>, you will be brought down to the unseen place of departed spirits <sup>[i.e., you will become obscure or obliterated as a city]</sup>.

The person who listens to you, <sup>[actually]</sup> listens to me. And the person who rejects you, <sup>[actually]</sup> rejects me. And the person who rejects me, <sup>[actually]</sup> rejects Him who sent me <sup>[i.e., God]</sup>.”
So, the seventy-two disciples returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the evil spirits submitted to us in your name <sup>[i.e., by your authority]</sup>.”
And Jesus said to them, “I saw Satan falling from heaven as lightning.

See, I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions <sup>[i.e., to receive protection from accidental contact with venomous creatures. See Acts 28:5]</sup> and <sup>[to have authority]</sup> over all the power of the enemy <sup>[i.e., Satan. See verse 18]</sup>. And nothing at all will hurt you.
Nevertheless, do not rejoice over the <sup>[evil]</sup> spirits submitting to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven <sup>[i.e., in the book of life. See Rev. 3:5; Heb. 12:23]</sup>.”

At that very time Jesus rejoiced in <sup>[the power of]</sup> the Holy Spirit, and said <sup>[in prayer]</sup>, “I praise you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you concealed these things <sup>[i.e., His divine revelations]</sup> from those <sup>[claiming to be]</sup> wise and intelligent and have revealed them to babies <sup>[i.e., to simple, sincere, common people]</sup>. Yes, Father, this was pleasing to you.

Everything has been handed over to me by my Father and no one knows the Son except the Father. And <sup>[no one knows]</sup> the Father except the Son and whatever person the Son wants to reveal Him to.”
Then Jesus turned to the disciples and said privately, “The eyes that see the things you see are <sup>[truly]</sup> blessed,

for I tell you, many prophets and kings wanted to see the things that you see, but did not <sup>[get to]</sup> see them. And <sup>[they wanted]</sup> to hear the things that you hear, but did not <sup>[get to]</sup> hear them.”
Just then a certain teacher of the Law of Moses stood up and <sup>[attempted to]</sup> test Jesus, saying, “Teacher, what must I do to inherit <sup>[never ending]</sup> life?”
And Jesus said to him, “What is written in the Law of Moses? How do you read it?”

And the man answered Him, <sup>[Deut. 6:5]</sup>, “You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength and with all your mind.” And <sup>[Lev. 19:18 says]</sup>, “<sup>[You must love]</sup> your neighbor just as <sup>[you love]</sup> yourself.”
Then Jesus said to him, “You have answered correctly; <sup>[if]</sup> you do this, you will live” <sup>[i.e., forever. See verse 25]</sup>.
But, wanting to justify himself, the teacher of the Law of Moses asked Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”

Jesus answered him, “A certain man was traveling down from Jerusalem to Jericho <sup>[Note: This was a town about eighteen miles northeast of Jerusalem]</sup>, when he was attacked by robbers, who stripped him <sup>[of his clothing and belongings]</sup> and beat him up, then went away leaving him half dead.
And it just happened that a certain priest was traveling down that <sup>[same]</sup> road, and when he saw the <sup>[injured]</sup> man, he went around him on the opposite side of the road.

In the same way, a Levite <sup>[Note: Levites were assistants to the priests]</sup>, when coming to that place and seeing the <sup>[injured]</sup> man, also went around him on the opposite side <sup>[of the road]</sup>.

But <sup>[then]</sup> a certain Samaritan <sup>[Note: This was a person despised by the Jews and regarded by them as a ‘half-breed’]</sup>, as he traveled along, came to where the <sup>[injured]</sup> man was <sup>[lying]</sup>, and upon seeing him <sup>[lying there]</sup>, felt deep pity <sup>[for him]</sup>.

So, he went over to him, poured oil and wine on him <sup>[i.e., as emergency medical treatment]</sup>, then bandaged his wounds, placed him on his own animal, and took him to a lodge where he took care of him.

Then the next day he gave the lodge keeper two coins <sup>[Note: These coins amounted to two twelve-hour days of a farm laborer’s pay, or about $216 in 2005]</sup> and told him, ‘Take care of this <sup>[injured]</sup> man; and whatever more you have to spend on him I will repay you when I come back <sup>[this way]</sup> again.’
<sup>[Now]</sup> which of these three men do you think acted like a neighbor to the man attacked by the robbers?”
And the teacher of the Law of Moses said, “The person who showed pity on him.” Then Jesus replied, “You go and do the same thing.”
Now as they went on their way, Jesus entered a certain village <sup>[i.e., Bethany. See John 11:1]</sup>, and a certain woman named Martha welcomed Him into her house.
And she had a sister named Mary who was also sitting at the Lord’s feet, listening to what He said.

But Martha was preoccupied with all the preparations <sup>[i.e., for entertaining her beloved guest]</sup> and came up to Jesus and said, “Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Ask her to help me.”
But the Lord answered her, “Martha, Martha; you are worried and bothered about <sup>[so]</sup> many things.
But only one thing is <sup>[really]</sup> needful. For Mary has made the better choice, which will not be taken away from her.”

And it happened as Jesus finished praying at a certain place that one of His disciples said to Him, “Lord, teach us <sup>[how]</sup> to pray, just like John <sup>[the Immerser]</sup> taught his disciples.”
And He said to them, “When you pray you should say, ‘Father, may your name be highly honored. May your kingdom come <sup>[to earth]</sup>.
Give us day by day our bread for each day.
And forgive us of our sins, for we ourselves also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And do not allow us to be put to the test.’”
And Jesus said to His disciples, “Suppose one of you would go to his friend at midnight and say to him, ‘ <sup>[Please]</sup> lend me three loaves of bread,
for a friend of mine has come to <sup>[visit]</sup> me while on a trip and I do not have anything to feed him’

Then the man will answer from inside <sup>[his house]</sup>, ‘Do not bother me. My door is <sup>[already]</sup> locked, my children are <sup>[already]</sup> in bed with me and I cannot get up to give you anything.’

I tell you, even though he will not get up and give him something because he is a friend, yet because of his friend’s persistence, he will <sup>[eventually]</sup> get up and give him whatever he needs.

And I tell you, ask <sup>[God for something]</sup> and it will be given to you; look <sup>[to God for something]</sup> and you will find it; knock <sup>[on the door of opportunity]</sup> and it will be opened up to you.
For every person who asks will <sup>[certainly]</sup> receive; and the one who looks will <sup>[certainly]</sup> find; and to the person who knocks, the door will <sup>[certainly]</sup> be opened up.

And what father among you, whose son asks <sup>[him]</sup> Some ancient manuscripts add “for a loaf of bread, will give him a stone <sup>[instead]</sup>? Or, <sup>[if he asks]</sup>” for a fish, will give him a snake <sup>[instead]</sup>?
Or, if he asks <sup>[him]</sup> for an egg, will give him a scorpion <sup>[instead]</sup>? <sup>[Note: While at rest a scorpion rolls up in a ball that resembles an egg]</sup>.

If then you know how to give good gifts to your children, even though you are sinful <sup>[human beings]</sup>, how much more <sup>[likely]</sup> is it that your Father in heaven will give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him?”

<sup>[Once]</sup> Jesus was driving out an evil spirit from a <sup>[man who was]</sup> mute. And it happened when the evil spirit left <sup>[him]</sup> that the <sup>[former]</sup> mute began to speak. And the crowds were amazed.
But some of the people said, “He is driving out evil spirits by <sup>[the power of]</sup> Beelzebub, the chief of evil spirits.”
Then others, trying to test Him, asked Him to perform a <sup>[supernatural]</sup> sign from heaven.
But, knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom <sup>[plagued]</sup> with internal division will end in ruin. And a household divided from within will fall.
And if Satan also is divided against himself, how <sup>[then]</sup> can his kingdom stand? I tell you this because you are saying that I drove out evil spirits by <sup>[the power of]</sup> Beelzebub.
But, if I drive out evil spirits by <sup>[the power of]</sup> Beelzebub, by whom do your followers drive them out? So, they will be your judges.

But, if I drive out evil spirits by the finger of God <sup>[i.e., by God’s direct, supernatural intervention]</sup>, then <sup>[that is evidence that]</sup> the kingdom of God has come into your midst <sup>[i.e., by my presence and ministry]</sup>.
When a fully armed strong man guards his own courtyard <sup>[i.e., homestead]</sup>, his possessions are safe.

But when someone stronger than he attacks him and overpowers him, he takes away all the armament the strong man relied on, and <sup>[then]</sup> divides up his storehouse of possessions. <sup>[Note: The word here translated “possessions” may be translated “arms,” so the meaning could be “divides up his armament”]</sup>.
The person who is not on my side is <sup>[actually]</sup> against me. And the person who does not gather <sup>[people to me]</sup> is <sup>[actually]</sup> driving them away <sup>[from me]</sup>.

“When an evil spirit has been driven out of a man, it roams through desert regions trying to find rest, but <sup>[when it]</sup> cannot find any, it says <sup>[to itself]</sup>, ‘I will return to my house from which I came <sup>[i.e., the body of the person it dominated]</sup>.’
But when it returns <sup>[to that body]</sup> it finds it cleaned out and <sup>[newly]</sup> decorated.

Then it goes and takes with it seven other spirits, more evil than itself, and they reenter <sup>[the body]</sup> and <sup>[begin]</sup> to live there, so that the latter state of that person becomes worse than the former state.”
And it happened as He said these things that a certain woman called out to Him from the crowd, saying, “The woman who gave you birth and nursed you is <sup>[certainly]</sup> blessed.”
But Jesus said, “Yes, but those who listen to God’s message and obey it are <sup>[more]</sup> blessed.”

And when the crowds thronged around Jesus, He began saying, “This generation of people is evil, <sup>[for]</sup> it is looking for a <sup>[supernatural]</sup> sign. But no <sup>[such]</sup> sign will be given to it except the sign <sup>[demonstrated in the life]</sup> of Jonah.

For just like Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh <sup>[Note: Nineveh was the capital of ancient Assyria]</sup>, so the Son of man will also be <sup>[a sign]</sup> to this generation of people.

The queen of the South <sup>[Note: This queen was from Sheba, which was probably in Arabia. See I Kings 10:1]</sup> will stand up on the judgment day, along with the people of this generation, and will condemn them because she came from a great distance to hear <sup>[and see]</sup> Solomon’s wisdom <sup>[I Kings 10:1ff]</sup> and <sup>[now]</sup> Someone greater than Solomon is here <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup>.

The people of Nineveh will stand up on the judgment day with the people of this generation and will condemn them because they repented <sup>[i.e., changed their hearts and lives]</sup> when hearing the preaching of Jonah <sup>[Jonah 3:5-10]</sup> and <sup>[now]</sup> Someone greater than Jonah is here <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup>.

“No one lights an <sup>[olive oil]</sup> lamp and <sup>[then]</sup> places it in a hidden place or under a bushel-sized container, but on its stand, so that those who enter <sup>[the room]</sup> can see <sup>[by]</sup> the light.

Your eye is <sup>[like]</sup> a lamp to your body <sup>[i.e., your life]</sup>. When your eye sees things correctly, <sup>[then]</sup> your whole body <sup>[i.e., your entire life]</sup> is illuminated <sup>[i.e., directed into proper conduct]</sup>. But when it sees things wrongly, <sup>[then]</sup> your body <sup>[i.e., your life]</sup> is also full of darkness <sup>[i.e., is not being lived properly]</sup>.

Look therefore, <sup>[to see]</sup> whether or not the light that is in you <sup>[i.e., your perspective on things]</sup> might <sup>[actually]</sup> be darkness <sup>[i.e., a distorted perspective]</sup>.

If therefore your whole body <sup>[i.e., your entire life]</sup> is full of light <sup>[i.e., sees and responds to things correctly]</sup>, with no blind spots <sup>[i.e., areas where you do not see and act correctly]</sup>, your body <sup>[i.e., your life]</sup> will be fully illuminated <sup>[i.e., directed into proper conduct]</sup> just like a bright, shining lamp illuminating you <sup>[i.e., enabling you to see and respond to things properly]</sup>.”

Now while Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee <sup>[i.e., a strict sect of the Jewish religion]</sup> invited Him to have a meal with him. So, Jesus went in <sup>[to his house]</sup> and reclined <sup>[at the dinner table]</sup>. <sup>[Note: See Matt. 23:6]</sup>.
And when the Pharisee observed this, he was surprised that Jesus had not first washed himself ceremonially before the meal.

And the Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees <sup>[meticulously]</sup> clean the outside of the cup and the dish <sup>[i.e., this was an allusion to their lives]</sup>, but you are filled with greed for wealth and <sup>[other]</sup> evils on the inside.
You foolish people! Did not the One <sup>[i.e., God]</sup> who made the outside <sup>[i.e., your body]</sup> also make the inside <sup>[i.e., your spirit]</sup>?

But you should give to poor people what you have inside. <sup>[Note: This means either what is inside the dish or what is inside your heart]</sup>. And <sup>[then]</sup> you will see that everything is <sup>[ceremonially]</sup> clean to you <sup>[as well]</sup>.

“But it is too bad for you Pharisees! For you give a tenth of your mint, rue and every <sup>[other]</sup> herb <sup>[Note: These were small garden plants used for seasoning or medicine]</sup> and <sup>[yet]</sup> neglect <sup>[demonstrating]</sup> justice and the love of God <sup>[toward others]</sup>. But you should have done these things and not neglected to do the other things <sup>[as well]</sup>.
“It is too bad for you Pharisees! For you love <sup>[to occupy]</sup> the principal seats in the synagogues and <sup>[to receive special]</sup> greetings in the open shopping markets.
“It is too bad for you! For you people are like obscure grave sites that people walk over without knowing it.”
Then one of the teachers of the Law of Moses answered Jesus, saying, “Teacher, you insulted us too by saying this.”

And He replied them, “It is too bad for you teachers of the Law of Moses also! For you heap heavy responsibilities on people, which are difficult to bear, while you do not even lift a finger to bear them yourselves.
“It is too bad for you! For your forefathers killed the prophets and you build grave monuments to them <sup>[i.e., the prophets]</sup>.
So, <sup>[by doing this]</sup> you are witnesses <sup>[against yourselves]</sup> that you approve of the deeds of your forefathers, for they killed the prophets and you build grave monuments to them.

Therefore, the wisdom of God said, <sup>[Note: Is this a reference to Jesus? See I Cor. 1:30 and Matt. 23:34-36]</sup>, ‘I will send prophets and apostles to them <sup>[i.e., the Jewish people]</sup> and they will kill and persecute some of them,
so that the blood of all the prophets, shed from the beginning of the world, may be required of this generation of people.’

Yes, I tell you, this generation will be punished <sup>[for the death of the prophets]</sup>, from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who died between the Altar and the sanctuary <sup>[i.e., in the Temple area]</sup>. <sup>[Note: This punishment began about 37 years later in the war with the Romans, when Jerusalem was destroyed]</sup>.

“It is too bad for you teachers of the Law of Moses! For you took away the key of knowledge <sup>[i.e., preventing people from understanding God’s plan]</sup>. You did not enter <sup>[the kingdom of heaven. See Matt. 23:13]</sup> yourselves, and <sup>[you even]</sup> prevented those who <sup>[wanted]</sup> to enter <sup>[to get in]</sup>.”

And when Jesus left there <sup>[i.e., probably the Pharisee’s house. See verse 37]</sup>, the experts in the Law of Moses and the Pharisees began opposing Him with hostility and bombarding Him with many questions,
<sup>[and]</sup> plotting to trap Him by something He might say.

About this time, while a crowd of many thousands had assembled, <sup>[so large that]</sup> people were trampling on one another, Jesus first began telling His disciples, “Watch out for the leavening <sup>[effect]</sup> of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

But there is nothing <sup>[they do that will remain]</sup> covered up; it will <sup>[all]</sup> be exposed. <sup>[And there is]</sup> nothing done secretly that will not be made known <sup>[openly]</sup>.

Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness <sup>[i.e., in obscurity]</sup> will be heard in the light <sup>[i.e., openly]</sup>. And what you have whispered in someone’s ear in a back room, will be declared from the housetops.
And I tell you, my friends, do not be afraid of those who can kill your body and after that can do nothing more.

But I am warning you about whom you should fear. Fear the One <sup>[i.e., God]</sup> who, after He has killed <sup>[i.e., the body]</sup>, has power to throw <sup>[you]</sup> into hell <sup>[See note on Matt. 5:29]</sup>. Yes, I tell you, fear Him.

Are not five sparrows sold <sup>[i.e., in the market as food]</sup> for two coins? <sup>[Note: The coins here amount to just over one hour’s worth of a farm laborer’s pay, or about $10 in 2005]</sup>. Yet in the presence of God, not a <sup>[single]</sup> one of them is forgotten.

Indeed, <sup>[God even knows]</sup> the number of hairs on your head <sup>[Note: The average full head of hair has approximately 25,000 hairs]</sup>. <sup>[So]</sup>, do not be afraid, for you are <sup>[much]</sup> more valuable than many sparrows.
“And I tell you, every person who acknowledges me in front of people <sup>[i.e., to be his Lord and Christ]</sup>, the Son of man will also acknowledge him in front of God’s angels.
But that person who disowns me in front of people <sup>[i.e., denies that I am the Christ, or that he is a disciple. See John 18:15-17, 25-27]</sup>, will be disowned in front of God’s angels.
And every person who speaks an <sup>[abusive]</sup> word against the Son of man will be forgiven of it, but the person who speaks abusive words against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven of it.
And when people bring you in front of synagogue assemblies and rulers and authorities <sup>[i.e., for judgment]</sup>, do not worry about how to speak or what you should say.
For the Holy Spirit will teach you what you should say at that very time.”
Then someone out of the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the <sup>[family]</sup> inheritance with me.”
But He said to him, “Man, who appointed me to judge or <sup>[decide on how]</sup> to divide <sup>[the property]</sup> between you two?”
And <sup>[then]</sup> He said to them, “Be careful and guard yourselves against <sup>[practicing]</sup> all forms of greed, for a person’s life does not consist of having a lot of possessions.”
And He told them a parable <sup>[i.e., a brief story to illustrate His teaching]</sup>, saying, “The land of a certain rich man produced a bumper crop,
so he thought to himself, ‘What should I do, because I do not have any <sup>[more]</sup> room to store my crops?’

Then he said <sup>[to himself]</sup>, ‘This is what I will do. I will tear down my barns and build larger <sup>[ones]</sup>; then I will store all my grain and <sup>[other]</sup> goods there <sup>[i.e., farm supplies]</sup>.
And I will say to myself, ‘Soul, you have plenty of goods <sup>[i.e., grain and supplies]</sup> stored up for many years, <sup>[so]</sup>, take it easy; eat, drink and have fun.’
But God said to him, ‘You fool! Tonight your life will be taken away from you. And <sup>[then]</sup> who will get the things you have stored up <sup>[for yourself]</sup>?’

This is <sup>[the way it will be with]</sup> the person who stores up treasures for himself, and <sup>[yet]</sup> is not rich toward God <sup>[i.e., does place enough importance on spiritual things]</sup>.”
Then Jesus said to His disciples, “So, I tell you, do not worry about <sup>[the material things of]</sup> your life, <sup>[such as]</sup> what you will eat or what you will wear on your body.
For there are more important things in life than food, and <sup>[more important things about]</sup> your body than the clothing <sup>[you wear]</sup>.

Look at the ravens; they do not plant seeds or harvest <sup>[a crop]</sup>; they do not have a storage locker or a barn, and <sup>[yet]</sup> God feeds them <sup>[adequately]</sup>. Are you not a lot more valuable than those birds?
And which one of you can worry yourself into living a single day longer?
So, if you are not able to do even a little thing like that, why are you worrying about the rest?

“Consider how <sup>[wild]</sup> lilies grow; they do not work or weave, yet I tell you, even Solomon, clothed in his very finest royal robes, was never dressed <sup>[as luxuriously]</sup> as one of these <sup>[flowers]</sup>.

But if God so <sup>[beautifully]</sup> dresses the grass in a field, which is <sup>[green]</sup> today, and <sup>[then]</sup> tomorrow <sup>[dries up and]</sup> is thrown into the oven <sup>[i.e., as fuel]</sup>, will He not do even more in providing your clothing, you people with such little faith?
So, do not look <sup>[anxiously]</sup> for what you will eat, and what you will drink, and do not be <sup>[continually]</sup> worried <sup>[i.e., about such things]</sup>.
For all these things are what the <sup>[unconverted]</sup> nations of the world keep trying to get. But your Father <sup>[already]</sup> knows you need these things.
So, look for God’s kingdom and these things <sup>[i.e., food, drink and clothing]</sup> will be provided for you.

“<sup>[And]</sup>, do not be afraid, little flock <sup>[Note: Jesus refers to His small band of disciples as sheep following Him as their Shepherd]</sup>, for your Father was pleased to give you the kingdom.

Sell your possessions and give <sup>[the money]</sup> to poor people; provide yourselves with money belts that do not wear out <sup>[i.e., from the constant use of carrying much money all the time, instead of investing it in others’ needs]</sup>. <sup>[Then you will possess]</sup> an inexhaustible treasure in heaven, where no thief can approach, nor moth destroy.
For wherever you store your valuables, that is where your heart <sup>[i.e., your real concern]</sup> will be also.
“Fasten your belts <sup>[i.e., get things ready]</sup>, keep your lamps lit,

and be like men expecting their master to return from the wedding reception, so that when he does come and knock <sup>[at the groom’s house, See note on Matt. 25:1-12]</sup>, they will immediately open <sup>[the door]</sup> to him.

Those slaves whom their master finds being watchful when he returns are <sup>[certainly]</sup> blessed. Truly I tell you, he will fasten his belt <sup>[i.e., get things ready]</sup> and have them recline <sup>[at his dinner table]</sup> and will come and serve them.
And if he comes between nine o’clock and midnight or between midnight and three o’clock in the morning and finds them being watchful, those slaves will be blessed <sup>[for doing so]</sup>.
But you should realize this, if the owner of the house had known exactly when the burglar was coming, he would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
So, you also should be ready, for the Son of man will return at a time when you least expect Him.”
Then Peter said, “Lord, are you telling this parable for our benefit <sup>[only]</sup> or for all people?”

And the Lord answered, “Who then is the trustworthy and sensible property manager, to whom his master will assign the management of his household, to provide adequate and timely meals for its members?
That servant will be happy when his master returns to find him doing <sup>[his job]</sup> well.
Truly I tell you, he will put that slave in charge of his entire estate.
But if that slave should think to himself, ‘My master will not be back soon,’ and then becomes physically abusive to his men and women servants, and eats and drinks and gets drunk,

that slave’s master will return on a day that he least expects, and at an hour unknown <sup>[to him]</sup>. And <sup>[that master]</sup> will whip him to shreds and will turn him over to suffer the same fate as unfaithful servants receive.
And that slave who knew what his master wanted, but did not make preparations <sup>[i.e., for his master’s return]</sup>, or do what his master wanted, will be whipped with many lashes.

But the one who did not know <sup>[i.e., what his master wanted]</sup>, yet did things deserving of punishment, will be whipped with few lashes. And to the person who has been given much <sup>[responsibility]</sup>, there will be much required <sup>[of him]</sup>. And to whom people entrust much <sup>[responsibility]</sup>, they will ask <sup>[much]</sup> more from him.

“I have come to bring fire to the earth. <sup>[Note: This means division within families. See verses 51-53]</sup> And how I wish that fire were already started <sup>[i.e., so I could complete my mission sooner]</sup>!
But I have an immersion to experience <sup>[i.e., overwhelming suffering to undergo. See Mark 10:38-39]</sup>, and what stress I am under until it happens!
Do you think that I came to bring <sup>[only]</sup> peace to the earth? No, I tell you, but division instead.
For from now on there will be a household of five persons divided <sup>[against one another]</sup>, three against two and two against three.

And a father will be divided against his son and the son against his father; a mother <sup>[will be divided]</sup> against her daughter and the daughter against her mother; a mother-in-law <sup>[will be divided]</sup> against her daughter-in-law and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”
And Jesus also said to the crowds, “When you see a cloud rising in the west, you immediately say, ‘A rainstorm is coming up,’ and sure enough, it does.
And when there is a south wind blowing, you say, ‘It is going to be a scorching day,’ and sure enough, it is.
You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but why do you not know how to interpret <sup>[events of]</sup> this present time?
