But, so that I would not become too conceited over the tremendous revelations <sup>[I received]</sup>, a “thorn in the flesh” was given to me as a messenger from Satan to torment me. <sup>[Note: This “thorn” was probably some physical handicap, possibly defective eyesight. See Gal. 4:14-15; 6:11]</sup>.
I pled with the Lord about this matter three times, that He would remove this problem from me.

But God said to me, “My unearned favor is enough for you, for my power is made complete in <sup>[your]</sup> weakness.” Therefore, I will most gladly boast, so that the power of Christ can rest on me.

So, I am pleased to <sup>[endure]</sup> weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions and difficulties, <sup>[all]</sup> for Christ’s sake. For when I am weak <sup>[physically]</sup>, then I am strong <sup>[spiritually]</sup>.

I have become foolish <sup>[i.e., by my boasting]</sup>, but you made me do it <sup>[i.e., in order to vindicate myself]</sup> For I should have been commended by you <sup>[i.e., for my effective ministry there in Corinth]</sup>, because I was not inferior in any way to those “super apostles” <sup>[See 11:5]</sup>, even though I am nothing.
Truly, I endured <sup>[opposition]</sup> patiently, while showing evidence of being an apostle among you. I performed <sup>[supernatural]</sup> signs, and wonders and powerful miracles.
For in what way were you people inferior to the rest of the churches, unless it was that I was not a <sup>[financial]</sup> burden to you? Forgive me for this wrong.

Now, this is the third time I have been ready to come to you, and I will not be a<sup>[financial]</sup> burden to you. For I do not want your possessions but you yourselves, because children should not <sup>[have to]</sup> save up for their parents, but parents for their children.
So, I will most gladly spend <sup>[all I have]</sup>, and <sup>[even]</sup> spend myself for the sake of your souls. If I love you so much, should you love me less <sup>[than that]</sup>?

So, let it be <sup>[said]</sup> that I did not become a burden to you. But being a clever person, <sup>[some are saying]</sup> that I took you in by trickery. <sup>[Note: Paul is saying that even though he took no financial support from the church, they are accusing him of taking financial advantage of them through other people]</sup>.
Did I take <sup>[financial]</sup> advantage of you by any of those men I sent to you?

I urged Titus <sup>[i.e., to go to you to arrange for the special offering. See 8:6]</sup>, and I sent the brother with him. <sup>[See 8:18]</sup>. Did Titus take any <sup>[financial]</sup> advantage of you? Do we not <sup>[both]</sup> have the same attitude <sup>[in this matter]</sup>? Do we not <sup>[both]</sup> follow the same procedures?

You have been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves <sup>[have you not]</sup>? We are <sup>[simply]</sup> speaking <sup>[the truth]</sup> before God in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ. But everything <sup>[we are doing]</sup>, dear friends, is to build you up <sup>[spiritually]</sup>.

For I am afraid that somehow, when I come <sup>[to you]</sup>, I might not find you the way I want you to be, and you might not find me the way you want me to be. I am afraid there might be quarrelling, jealousy, angry outbursts, factious spirits, slander, gossip, arrogance and <sup>[other]</sup> disturbances <sup>[there]</sup>.

I am afraid that, when I come, my God will humiliate me in front of you, and I will be grieved over many <sup>[of those]</sup> who have sinned in the past without repenting of the <sup>[moral]</sup> impurity, sexual immorality and unrestrained indecency they have practiced.
This will be my third visit to you. <sup>[Deut. 19:15 says]</sup>, “Every matter should be settled by the testimony of two or three witnesses.”

I told you the last time I was there, and now before returning, I <sup>[again]</sup> warn those who have sinned in the past as well as the rest, that if I come again I will not spare you <sup>[i.e., I will rebuke and discipline you]</sup>,

since you are looking for proof that Christ is speaking through me. Christ is not weak in His dealing with you, but is powerful among you. <sup>[Note: Paul probably refers to the positive effects of his ministry there in Corinth]</sup>.

For He was weak when He was crucified <sup>[i.e., by assuming a human body capable of suffering and death]</sup>, but <sup>[now]</sup> He lives by God’s power. For we too are weak, <sup>[along]</sup> with Him <sup>[i.e., each of us experiences the frailties of a human body]</sup>, but by <sup>[this same]</sup> power from God, we will live with Him to serve you. <sup>[Note: The various translations differ widely on how to properly translate this difficult verse]</sup>.

Put yourselves to a test; see if you are <sup>[really]</sup> in the faith <sup>[i.e., see if you are genuine Christians]</sup>; examine yourselves! Or, do you not realize that Jesus Christ is in your hearts, unless <sup>[of course]</sup> you fail the test <sup>[i.e., unless you are counterfeit Christians]</sup>.
But I hope that you people will realize that we <sup>[apostles]</sup> are not counterfeit.

Now we pray to God that you people will not do anything wrong. That is not so that we will appear approved <sup>[by God]</sup>, but so that you will do what is right, even though we may appear to be counterfeits.

For we <sup>[apostles]</sup> have no power <sup>[to act]</sup> against the truth, but <sup>[only]</sup> for the truth. <sup>[Note: Paul here alludes to his gifts and ministry being intended only to promote what was right according to God’s truth]</sup>.
For we are glad when we are weak and you are powerful. And we are also praying for your complete restoration <sup>[i.e., to spiritual maturity]</sup>

This is the reason I am writing these things while away from you, so that I will not have to deal severely with you when I am there. For the Lord gave me authority to build people up <sup>[spiritually]</sup> and not to tear them down.

Finally, brothers, good-bye <sup>[or, “be glad”]</sup>. Be fully restored <sup>[spiritually]</sup>. Be comforted <sup>[or, “exhort one another”]</sup>. Think alike on things. Be at peace among yourselves. And the God of love and peace will be with you.
Greet one another with a holy kiss <sup>[i.e., to signify affection, yet without sensuality]</sup>.
All the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> send you their greetings.
May the favor of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with all of you.

<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, an apostle, (not from <sup>[the authority of]</sup> men, or through the <sup>[agency]</sup> of a man, but by <sup>[a direct commission from]</sup> Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised Him from the dead).
<sup>[It also comes from]</sup> all of the brothers who are with me <sup>[and is being sent]</sup> to the churches of Galatia <sup>[i.e., Iconium, Derbe, Lystra and Antioch of Pisidia]</sup>.
May you have unearned favor and peace from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,

who gave Himself to <sup>[save us from]</sup> our sins so that He might deliver us from <sup>[the control and condemnation of]</sup> this present evil world. <sup>[This is]</sup> according to the will of our God and Father,
to whom <sup>[may there]</sup> be honor forever and ever. May it be so.

I am astonished that you people are so quickly abandoning God <sup>[i.e., by rejecting His truth]</sup>, who called you <sup>[to Himself]</sup> through the unearned favor bestowed <sup>[on us]</sup> by Christ. <sup>[You are turning]</sup> to a different “gospel,”
although it is really not a different one, but rather some people are causing you trouble by trying to pervert the <sup>[true]</sup> Gospel of Christ.

However, if we <sup>[apostles]</sup> or even an angel <sup>[claiming to be]</sup> from heaven were to preach any other “gospel” than the one we have <sup>[already]</sup> preached to you, let that person be cut off from God’s <sup>[salvation]</sup>.

I repeat what I have already said: If any person preaches to you a “gospel” other than what you have already received <sup>[from us apostles]</sup>, he should be cut off from God’s <sup>[salvation]</sup>.

For am I seeking <sup>[to gain]</sup> the favor of men or of God? Or, am I striving to please people <sup>[by what I do]</sup>? If I were still <sup>[trying]</sup> to please people, I would not be a <sup>[true]</sup> servant of Christ.
For I would like to clarify for you, brothers, the situation regarding the Gospel that I have preached: It did not originate from man.

<sup>[Furthermore]</sup>, I did not receive this <sup>[Gospel message]</sup> from any person, nor was I taught it <sup>[by anyone]</sup>. Instead, it came to me through <sup>[a divine]</sup> revelation from Jesus Christ.
For you have heard about the way I lived in the past, when I was in the Jewish religion, <sup>[that is]</sup>, how I mercilessly persecuted the church of God, making havoc of it.
I advanced in the Jewish religion beyond many of my contemporaries among my countrymen. I have been extremely more zealous in advocating the traditions <sup>[taught]</sup> by my forefathers.
But then it was the good pleasure of God, who set me apart <sup>[for this purpose]</sup> before I was born, to call me <sup>[to this ministry]</sup> through His unearned favor.

<sup>[And when I was called]</sup>, God revealed His Son to me so that I could preach <sup>[about]</sup> Him to the Gentiles. <sup>[When this happened]</sup> I immediately avoided discussing the matter with anyone.

I did not go up to Jerusalem to <sup>[discuss it with]</sup> those who had become apostles before me either; instead I went away into Arabia. <sup>[Note: This is probably when Paul received supernatural power from the Holy Spirit]</sup>. Then afterward, I returned to Damascus. <sup>[Note: This was in Syria where Paul was converted]</sup>.
Then, three years later I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas <sup>[i.e., Peter]</sup>, and stayed with him for fifteen days.
But <sup>[on this trip]</sup> I did not see any of the other apostles except James, the Lord’s brother. <sup>[Note: This “James” was not one of the original twelve apostles. See Matt. 10:2-4]</sup>.
Now <sup>[realizing I am]</sup> in God’s presence, I am not lying about any of these things.
<sup>[Then after that]</sup> I went to the territories of Syria and Cilicia. <sup>[Note: Cilicia was where Paul’s home town of Tarsus was located. See Acts 9:30]</sup>.
<sup>[At this time]</sup> I was still unknown personally to churches in <sup>[the province of]</sup> Judea, which are in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ.
They had only heard it said about me, “This person, who had once persecuted us <sup>[Christians]</sup>, is now preaching the faith he once made havoc of.”
So, they gave honor to God for <sup>[what He was doing through]</sup> me.
Then, fourteen years later I went up to Jerusalem again,<sup>[along]</sup> with Barnabas, and I took Titus also with me <sup>[thistime]</sup>.

<sup>[It was]</sup> in response to <sup>[a divine]</sup> revelation that I went <sup>[there]</sup> and explained to them <sup>[i.e., the apostles and elders]</sup> the Gospel <sup>[message]</sup> that I had been preaching among the Gentiles. Then <sup>[later on I presented it]</sup> privately before those who were highly regarded <sup>[i.e., the three apostles, verse 9]</sup> so <sup>[they would have verification that]</sup> my running <sup>[i.e., ministry]</sup> had not been, nor was now, in vain.
But they did not even require that Titus, a Greek <sup>[i. e., a Gentile]</sup> who was with me, become circumcised.

For <sup>[certain]</sup> false brothers, who were secretly brought in to observe us practicing our liberty in Christ Jesus <sup>[i.e., by not having Titus circumcised]</sup>, attempted to place us <sup>[back]</sup> under the bondage <sup>[of Mosaic Law keeping]</sup>.
But we did not submit ourselves to their influence for even an hour, so that you people might be able to continue <sup>[following]</sup> the truth of the Gospel.

These apostles, who were highly regarded (although it really does not matter to me what they are, for God does not show partiality to any particular person); these men, I say, provided me with nothing <sup>[concern­ing the Gospel message]</sup>.

On the contrary, they saw that I had <sup>[already]</sup> been entrusted <sup>[by God]</sup> with the Gospel for the uncircumcised ones <sup>[i.e., for Gentiles]</sup>, just as Peter <sup>[had been entrusted by God]</sup> with the Gospel for the circumcised ones <sup>[i.e., for Jews]</sup>.
For God assigned Peter to be an apostle to the circumcised ones <sup>[i.e., Jews]</sup>, and me to be an apostle to the Gentiles.

Also, when they perceived that this <sup>[special]</sup> favor <sup>[of preaching to Gentiles]</sup> was granted to me, James, Cephas <sup>[i.e., Peter]</sup> and John, the highly regarded pillars <sup>[i.e., leaders of the church]</sup> extended to me and Barnabas their right hands <sup>[signifying an agreement]</sup> to share <sup>[in the preaching of the Gospel]</sup>, with us going to the Gentiles and they going to the circumcised ones <sup>[i.e., Jews]</sup>.
The only thing that they encouraged us to do was to remember <sup>[the needs of]</sup> poor people, which I was very eager to do. <sup>[See Acts 24:17]</sup>.
But when Cephas came to Antioch <sup>[of Syria]</sup>, I confronted him personally <sup>[about a matter]</sup> for which he deserved to be condemned.

For, before certain <sup>[leaders]</sup> came from James <sup>[i.e., Jesus’ brother, a leader of the Jerusalem church, See Acts 12:17; 15:13-22]</sup>, he had eaten <sup>[a social meal]</sup> with some Gentiles. But when these men came, he abruptly discontinued this practice out of fear of the circumcised ones <sup>[i.e., out of concern for what the Jews would think of him]</sup>.
Then the rest of the Jews also discontinued <sup>[eating with Gentiles]</sup> along with Peter, until even Barnabas was influenced to go along with this hypocritical practice.

But, when I saw that they did not act in harmony with <sup>[the principles of]</sup> the truth of the Gospel <sup>[message]</sup>, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, “If you, being a Jew, act like a Gentile <sup>[by showing such discrimination]</sup>, how do you expect to persuade Gentiles to live like Jews <sup>[i.e., like Christian Jews who are taught to love all people]</sup>?”
Although we are Jews by natural birth, and not sinful Gentiles <sup>[who do not have a relationship with God]</sup>,

still we know that a person is not justified <sup>[i.e., made right with God]</sup> by <sup>[perfect obedience to]</sup> the Law of Moses, but rather through faith in <sup>[the person and work of]</sup> Christ. <sup>[Knowing this]</sup> we have trusted in Christ Jesus <sup>[to save us]</sup>, so that we might be made right with God by trusting in Him and not by <sup>[our compliance with]</sup> the requirements of the Law of Moses. Because by such law-compliance no one can be made right with God.

But, if while we were trying to be made right with God through a relationship with Christ, we were discovered to still be sinners <sup>[by acting like unsaved Gentiles]</sup>, does that mean Christ is responsible for our sin? Of course not.

For if I try to build up again what I had already destroyed <sup>[i.e., by returning to depend on compliance with the Law of Moses to become right with God, after having rejected this system by trusting in Christ to save me]</sup>, I would certainly be guilty of sin.
For it was by means of <sup>[this system of]</sup> law-keeping that I <sup>[realized I]</sup> was dead. This was so I could find life through a relationship with God.

I have been crucified <sup>[i.e., died]</sup> with Christ, and so I am not the one who lives <sup>[anymore]</sup> but rather, it is Christ who lives in my heart. And <sup>[now]</sup> my entire life is being lived by trusting in the Son of God <sup>[to save and keep me]</sup>, because He loved me and gave Himself up for me.

<sup>[In doing this]</sup> I am not trying to disregard God’s unearned favor, for if a person could become right with God by <sup>[perfect obedience to the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses, then Christ died for nothing.

You foolish people <sup>[of the churches]</sup> of Galatia <sup>[Note: This was a province in present-day Turkey]</sup>, who tricked you? Was not Jesus Christ graphically portrayed before you as being put to death on a cross?

The only thing I would like to learn from you is this: Did you receive the Holy Spirit <sup>[into your lives]</sup> by <sup>[meeting the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses or by hearing <sup>[and obeying the message]</sup> of faith <sup>[in Christ]</sup>?

Are you so foolish <sup>[as to believe that after]</sup> having begun <sup>[your Chris­tian lives]</sup> through <sup>[the power of]</sup> the Holy Spirit, you can <sup>[now]</sup> become complete by <sup>[living according to your human strength]</sup>?
Did you suffer so many things <sup>[for the faith]</sup> all for nothing? <sup>[That is]</sup>, if it really was for nothing?

Therefore, as God has provided you with the Holy Spirit and has produced miracles among you, <sup>[do you think He has done all this]</sup> as a result of your obeying the requirements of the Law of Moses or as a result of hearing <sup>[and responding to the message of]</sup> the faith?
<sup>[An example of this is]</sup> Abraham, who believed God’s <sup>[promise]</sup> and <sup>[because of such faith]</sup> he was considered right with God.
You should know, then, that people who have faith are <sup>[truly]</sup> God’s children.

And, predicting that God would consider the Gentiles right with Him by <sup>[their]</sup> faith, the Scriptures previously recorded <sup>[this promise of]</sup> the Gospel to Abraham by saying <sup>[Gen. 12:3]</sup>, “All nations will receive a blessing through your descendant <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup>.”
So then, all those who have faith <sup>[in Jesus]</sup> receive a blessing along with Abraham, a man of faith.

For all who seek to be right with God by trying to meet all the requirements of the Law of Moses are under a curse <sup>[from God]</sup>, because it is written <sup>[Deut. 27:26]</sup>, “Every person is cursed <sup>[i.e., cut off from God]</sup> who does not continue to obey all of the requirements written in the Law of Moses.”

Now it is evident that no person can be made right with God by <sup>[obeying all the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses for <sup>[Hab. 2:4 says]</sup>, “The righteous person will obtain life by <sup>[his]</sup> faith <sup>[in God]</sup>.”
Now the Law of Moses is not based on one’s faith, for <sup>[Lev. 18:5 says]</sup>, “The person who obeys the requirements of the Law of Moses will obtain life by doing so.”

Christ bought us back from the curse <sup>[caused by our not obeying all the requirements]</sup> of the Law of Moses; He became a curse for us <sup>[by dying on the cross]</sup>, for it is written <sup>[Deut. 21:23]</sup>, “Every one who hangs on a tree <sup>[i.e., a cross]</sup> is cursed <sup>[i.e., cut off from God]</sup>.”

<sup>[This happened]</sup> so that the blessing <sup>[promised]</sup> to Abraham might be received by the Gentiles through <sup>[the person and work of]</sup> Christ, so that we might receive the promised <sup>[gift of the]</sup> Holy Spirit through <sup>[our]</sup> faith <sup>[in Jesus]</sup>.

Brothers, I will use an illustration from the way people deal with each other: Even though an agreement is made between people, once it is finally settled, no one can cancel it or include additional provisions to it.

Now <sup>[specific]</sup> promises were made to Abraham and to his seed <sup>[i.e., descendants]</sup>. <sup>[God]</sup> did not say “seeds” <sup>[plural]</sup> as though He were referring to many persons but “seed” <sup>[singular]</sup> showing that He meant one <sup>[descendant]</sup>; <sup>[Gen. 13:15 says]</sup> “and to your seed,” referring to Christ.

Here is what I am saying: The Agreement originally made by God <sup>[with Abraham]</sup>, with its attending promises, was not canceled when the Law of Moses was given some four hundred and thirty years later.

For if the inheritance <sup>[of never ending life]</sup> can be obtained by <sup>[obeying the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses, it would not come from the promise <sup>[of God]</sup>; but God provided <sup>[this inheritance]</sup> for Abraham according to <sup>[His]</sup> promise.

What then is <sup>[the benefit of]</sup> the Law of Moses? It was introduced <sup>[as part of God’s revelation]</sup> in order to define what sin was, until the seed <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup> would come, to whom the promise <sup>[of never ending life]</sup> was made. The Law of Moses was ordained through <sup>[the medium of]</sup> angels and <sup>[delivered]</sup> through the hand of an intermediary <sup>[i.e., Moses]</sup>.
Now an intermediary does not serve in a situation involving <sup>[only]</sup> one person. However, God is one <sup>[Being]</sup>.

Is the Law of Moses contrary to the promises of God? Certainly not! For if there had been a law given which could provide <sup>[never ending]</sup> life, then a person could be considered right with God by <sup>[obeying the requirements of such]</sup> a law.

But the Scriptures indicate that everything <sup>[i.e., everyone]</sup> is under the control of sin, so that the promise <sup>[of never ending life]</sup> could be available <sup>[only]</sup> to those who have faith in Jesus Christ.

But before faith <sup>[in Christ]</sup> became available, we <sup>[Jews]</sup> were kept in bondage under <sup>[condemnation for not obeying perfectly]</sup> the Law of Moses. <sup>[This condition existed]</sup> until the faith <sup>[i.e., the Gospel message]</sup> was made known <sup>[to people]</sup>.

So, the Law of Moses became like our “transportation to school,” bringing us to <sup>[the school of]</sup> Christ where we <sup>[learn how to be]</sup> made right with God by faith <sup>[in Christ]</sup>.
But now that faith <sup>[in Christ]</sup> has become available, we are no longer in need of this “transportation to school.”
For all of you are children of God through your faith in Christ Jesus <sup>[as God’s only Son]</sup>.
For all of you who were immersed into <sup>[a relationship with]</sup> Christ have been clothed with Him <sup>[i.e., His life]</sup>.

<sup>[In this relationship]</sup> there is to be no <sup>[distinction between]</sup> Jews or Greeks <sup>[i.e., Gentiles]</sup>, slaves or free persons, men or women; for all of you are united into one <sup>[body]</sup> because of your relationship with Christ Jesus.

And if you belong to Christ, then you are <sup>[truly]</sup> Abraham’s seed <sup>[i.e., his spiritual descendants]</sup> and thereby <sup>[you will]</sup> inherit what was promised to him <sup>[i.e., God’s blessings]</sup>.

But as long as the person who has an inheritance coming to him is a child, he is really no better off than a slave, even though he himself is <sup>[rightfully]</sup> entitled <sup>[to the inheritance]</sup>.
He is <sup>[still]</sup> under <sup>[the direction of]</sup> guardians and managers <sup>[of his affairs]</sup> until the day his father decides <sup>[to give him his inheritance]</sup>.
So also, when we <sup>[Jews]</sup> were like children, we were under bondage to the basic worldly rules <sup>[of the Law of Moses]</sup>.
But, when <sup>[God’s]</sup> timing fully arrived, He sent His Son, born to a woman <sup>[i.e., Mary]</sup>, born during <sup>[the time when]</sup> the Law <sup>[of Moses was still in effect]</sup>,
so that He could buy back <sup>[from Satan]</sup> those who were <sup>[still]</sup> under <sup>[obligation to]</sup> the Law of Moses, and that we could become adopted children.
And because you are <sup>[His]</sup> children, God sent the Holy Spirit of His Son into our hearts, to call out, “Abba,” <sup>[which means]</sup> “Father.”
So <sup>[now]</sup>, you are no longer a slave but a child, and since you are a child, you also will receive an inheritance through <sup>[the blessings of]</sup> God.
At that time, when you did not know God <sup>[as your Father]</sup>, you were in bondage to those who were, by nature, not really gods at all.

But now that you have come to know God <sup>[as your Father]</sup>, or rather, to be known by Him <sup>[as His children]</sup>, how can you turn back again to the weak, cheap, elementary teachings <sup>[of the Law of Moses]</sup>, to which you <sup>[seem to]</sup> desire to become enslaved?
You are observing <sup>[certain]</sup> days, and months, and seasons, and years <sup>[as binding religious holidays]</sup>.
I am afraid for you, <sup>[i.e., for your spiritual safety]</sup>, that possibly I may have spent a lot of effort on your behalf for nothing.
I urge you, brothers, become like me <sup>[in this matter]</sup>, for I have become like you. You did not do anything wrong to me, <sup>[even as I have not wronged you]</sup>.
But you are aware <sup>[I am sure]</sup> that when I first came preaching the Gospel <sup>[message]</sup> to you, I had a physical handicap.
And when you could have been tempted to look down on me <sup>[because of the nature of my affliction]</sup>, instead you welcomed me as <sup>[if I were]</sup> an angel, or even Christ Jesus Himself!

Now where are those good feelings you had for me? For I can <sup>[honestly]</sup> testify that <sup>[at one time]</sup> you would have even gouged out your eyes and given them to me <sup>[for sight]</sup> if that had been possible.
So then, have I <sup>[now]</sup> become your enemy because I have told you the truth <sup>[about your condition]</sup>?

Certain people are showing quite an interest in you, but it is not for your benefit. Instead, they are trying to separate you <sup>[from me]</sup>, in hope that you will seek them out <sup>[for help]</sup>.
Now, it is fine for someone to want to seek you out anytime, if it is for a good purpose, and not just when I am around <sup>[to be impressed]</sup>.

My little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, I am again deeply pained <sup>[as a woman in childbirth]</sup> over your <sup>[spiritual]</sup> condition, until <sup>[the life of]</sup> Christ becomes formed in your character.
But I would like to be present with you and change the tone <sup>[of my remarks]</sup>, because I am very distressed over your condition.
Tell me, those of you who desire to be under <sup>[obligation to the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses: Are you really paying attention to what the Law of Moses says?
For it is written in the law about Abraham’s two sons <sup>[Gen. 16]</sup>; one <sup>[was]</sup> by his slave woman <sup>[Hagar]</sup> and the other by the free woman, <sup>[his wife, Sarah]</sup>.

Now the son <sup>[Ishmael]</sup>, born to the slave woman, was born simply of their natural relationship; but the son <sup>[Isaac]</sup>, born to the free woman, was born in fulfillment of a <sup>[divine]</sup> promise.

This set of circumstances contains an illustration; for these two women <sup>[Hagar and Sarah]</sup> represent the two Agreements <sup>[between God and mankind]</sup>. The one <sup>[Agreement]</sup>, given at Mount Sinai, represents Hagar, whose children became <sup>[the Israelites]</sup>, enslaved under bondage <sup>[to the requirements of the Law of Moses]</sup>.

Now this woman, Hagar, is similar to Mount Sinai, in Arabia, and represents the present <sup>[earthly]</sup> Jerusalem <sup>[i.e., Israel]</sup>, consisting of children in bondage <sup>[to the Law of Moses]</sup>.
But the heavenly Jerusalem <sup>[Heb. 12:22]</sup> consists of free people, and is the mother of us <sup>[Christians]</sup>.

For it is written <sup>[Isa. 40:1]</sup>, “<sup>[Woman]</sup> you should be happy that you have not had children. Cry over your inability to experience the pain of childbirth. For the deserted woman has had more children than the one with a husband.”
Now, brothers, you <sup>[Christians]</sup> are children of the promise <sup>[made to Abraham. See 3:29]</sup> just like Isaac, <sup>[Abraham’s son]</sup> was.

But, just as it was then --- the son born in the ordinary way <sup>[i.e., Ishmael]</sup> persecuting the one born according to the Holy Spirit’s <sup>[leading, i.e., Isaac]</sup> --- so it is now <sup>[i.e., Jews urging compliance with the requirements of the Law of Moses persecuting those who refuse to return to dependence on that law for salvation. See 3:1-3]</sup>.

What does the Scripture say about this matter? <sup>[It says, Gen. 21:10-12]</sup>, “Get rid of the slave woman <sup>[Hagar]</sup>, with her son <sup>[Ishmael]</sup>; for the son of the slave woman <sup>[Ishmael]</sup> will not inherit <sup>[what God promised to Abraham and his descendants]</sup>, along with the son of the free woman <sup>[i.e., Isaac]</sup>.”
Therefore, brothers, we <sup>[Christians]</sup> are not the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> children of the slave woman <sup>[Hagar]</sup> but of the free woman <sup>[Sarah]</sup>.

Christ set us free <sup>[from condemnation under the Law of Moses]</sup>, so we could stay free! Remain where you are then, and do not get tangled up again in the enslaving harness <sup>[of the Law of Moses]</sup>.

Look, I Paul am telling you that, if you revert to <sup>[the practice of]</sup> circumcision <sup>[i.e., the Jewish rite of identity, signifying the responsibility to observe the Law of Moses]</sup>, then Christ’s <sup>[sacrifice]</sup> will be of no value to you.
Yes, I declare to every person who attempts to require circumcision <sup>[as a means of gaining acceptance with God]</sup>, that he must also obey everything else required by the Law of Moses.

Those of you who seek to be right with God by observing the requirements of the Law of Moses have severed yourselves from <sup>[your relationship with]</sup> Christ; you have fallen away from God’s unearned favor.
For we are waiting for the hope <sup>[to be fulfilled]</sup> of being made right with God by <sup>[the power of]</sup> the Holy Spirit.

For in <sup>[our relationship with]</sup> Christ neither the practice of circumcision nor refraining from its practice matters in any way; but <sup>[all that really matters is having a genuine]</sup> faith <sup>[in Christ]</sup> that causes us to work <sup>[for Him]</sup> out of a genuine love <sup>[for Him and others]</sup>.
You <sup>[Christians]</sup> were running <sup>[the race of life]</sup> well; who hindered you <sup>[from making further progress]</sup> so that <sup>[now]</sup> you are no longer obeying the truth?
<sup>[Your]</sup> being persuaded <sup>[to do this]</sup> was not motivated by God, who called you <sup>[into His fellowship]</sup>.
A little bit of yeast permeates the whole batch of dough <sup>[i.e., a few people can influence everyone else to do the wrong thing in this matter]</sup>.

<sup>[However]</sup>, I have confidence in you, since we both have a relationship with the Lord, that you will not think differently <sup>[than I do]</sup> about this matter. But whoever is causing you trouble will be rightfully judged <sup>[and punished]</sup> for it.

But, brothers, if I were still preaching <sup>[that a person had]</sup> to be circumcised, why am I being persecuted? <sup>[Note: Paul’s argument seems to be “I must not be preaching circumcision since I am being persecuted by those who believe in it”]</sup>. <sup>[If I preached that a person had to be circumcised]</sup> then my preaching that Christ died on the cross would not be such an offense <sup>[to those who believe in it]</sup>.
I wish that those who are causing such a problem over wanting people to be circumcised would just mutilate themselves <sup>[while they are at it!]</sup>.
For you brothers were called <sup>[by God]</sup> to enjoy your freedom; but do not use this freedom as an excuse for living a sinful life; instead, use it to render loving service to each other.
For the whole Law of Moses can be fulfilled by <sup>[obeying this]</sup> one requirement <sup>[Lev. 19:18]</sup>: “You should love your neighbor the way you love yourself.”
But if you <sup>[unlovingly]</sup> bite and eat one another <sup>[i.e., figuratively]</sup>, be careful that you do not totally consume one another.
But, I am saying to you, live in harmony with the Holy Spirit’s <sup>[desire for you]</sup> and <sup>[then]</sup> you will not <sup>[always]</sup> be giving in to the wrong desires of your body.

For what your body wants is in conflict with what the Holy Spirit wants <sup>[for you]</sup>, and what the Holy Spirit wants conflicts with what your body wants, for these <sup>[two desires]</sup> are contrary to one another, so that you do not <sup>[always]</sup> do what you <sup>[really]</sup> want to.
But, if you are led by <sup>[what]</sup> the Holy Spirit <sup>[wants you to do]</sup>, you are not under <sup>[obligation to meet the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses.
Now those things done by the sinful, physical nature <sup>[of a person]</sup> are evident; they are these: sexual immorality, moral impurity, indecent conduct,
idol worship, occultic practices, hatefulness, dissension, jealousy, angry outbursts, factious spirits, divisiveness, party spirits,
envy, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these. I warn you again, as I have done before, that those people who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
But the fruit produced by the Holy Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, generosity, faithfulness,
gentleness and self-control. There can be no law against <sup>[practicing]</sup> these things.
And those persons who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified <sup>[i.e., put out of their lives, Col. 3:4ff]</sup> the sinful cravings of the body, along with their passionate desires.
If we live by the <sup>[guidance of the]</sup> Holy Spirit, we should also walk in <sup>[the ways of]</sup> the Holy Spirit.
We should avoid foolish pride as well as provoking or envying one another.

Brothers, if any one of you is overwhelmed by a <sup>[particular]</sup> sin, those of you who are spiritually minded should <sup>[attempt to]</sup> restore such a person <sup>[to faithfulness]</sup>. But do it in a gentle way, being very careful that you yourself do not become tempted <sup>[to get involved in any sin]</sup>.
Take on yourselves the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> burdens of one another, for in doing so you will be fulfilling Christ’s law <sup>[of love, John 13:35]</sup>.
For any person who thinks he is someone <sup>[important]</sup>, when he is really nothing, is self-deceived.
But let each person test his own accomplishments so that he can determine if he should get credit for them or if his neighbor should.
For each person should shoulder the responsibility of his own work load.
But the person who is taught the word <sup>[of God]</sup> should provide his teacher with what he needs.
Do not be deceived; God will not be fooled. Whatever a person does with his life, he will reap <sup>[the results of it]</sup>.

If he engages in things to satisfy his own sinful desires, he will be <sup>[spiritually]</sup> destroyed for such conduct, but the one who engages in things to please the Holy Spirit will receive never ending life through the <sup>[same]</sup> Spirit <sup>[as a result]</sup>.
And we should not become tired of doing good things, for eventually we will receive <sup>[never ending life]</sup> if we do not give up.
So then, we should do the right thing toward every person whenever we have the opportunity, and especially toward people who are part of God’s family <sup>[i.e., those making up “the faith”]</sup>.
Look at how large the letters are that I am using in writing this, and I am writing them myself.
Those who attempt to make a good impression by trying to force you to be circumcised really want only to avoid being persecuted for their commitment to Christ.

For not even those who practice circumcision comply with <sup>[the requirements of]</sup> the Law of Moses, yet they want you to be circumcised so they can take credit for this physical evidence <sup>[of your compliance with the Law of Moses]</sup>.

But the last thing I want to do is brag <sup>[about such things]</sup>. For <sup>[if I had any bragging to do at all]</sup>, it would be about <sup>[salvation through]</sup> the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, for it is through Him that this <sup>[sinful]</sup> world has been crucified <sup>[i.e., died]</sup> as far as I am concerned and I have been crucified <sup>[i.e., died]</sup> as far as it is concerned.
For it does not matter whether a person practices circumcision or refrains from practicing it; what really matters is <sup>[whether or not he is]</sup> a new creature <sup>[in the eyes of God]</sup>.

To all those people who live by this rule, may there be peace and mercy <sup>[from God]</sup> in their lives; that is, upon <sup>[those people who are truly]</sup> God’s “Israel” <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup>.
From now on no one should harass me <sup>[about such matters]</sup> for I carry around on my body the scars <sup>[left there in my service]</sup> for Christ.
May the unearned favor of our Lord Jesus Christ be in your inner person, brothers. May this be so.

<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus, according to God’s will, <sup>[and is being sent]</sup> to the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> at Ephesus <sup>[Note: This was a major city in the western province of Asia Minor, and is now a part of Turkey. The words “at Ephesus” are omitted in some manuscripts, suggesting it was a circular letter]</sup>, and the faithful ones in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus <sup>[everywhere else (?)]</sup>.
May you have unearned favor and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be praised, for He has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly realms in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ. <sup>[Note: The phrase “heavenly realms” is used somewhat differently throughout this book. Here it refers to Christians as part of the church on earth]</sup>.
Even before the creation of the world God chose us, who are in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ, to be dedicated <sup>[to Him]</sup> and to be without just blame in His sight. Out of love
God predetermined that we should be <sup>[considered]</sup> His adopted children through <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ. This is what pleased God and is what He wanted,
so that we would praise His glorious, unearned favor, which was freely given to us through the One whom God loved <sup>[i.e., Christ]</sup>.

In <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ we have redemption <sup>[i.e., we have been bought back from Satan]</sup> through His blood, and have forgiveness of sins according to the abundance of God’s unearned favor,
which He lavished on us. <sup>[He used]</sup> wisdom and understanding,
in doing what He intended through Christ, to make known to us His secret plan, according to what pleased Him.
<sup>[He did this]</sup> to fulfill His plan at the proper time, by bringing together everything <sup>[in the universe]</sup> under Christ’s headship --- things both in heaven and on earth.

It is in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ that we <sup>[Jewish Christians]</sup> also were selected as <sup>[God’s]</sup> inheritance <sup>[i.e., to receive His never ending blessings]</sup>, having been predetermined according to the plan of God, who works out everything in harmony with what He wants.
The end <sup>[i.e., of being so predetermined]</sup> was that we <sup>[Jewish Christians]</sup>, who had previously placed our hope in Christ, should bring about the praise of God’s splendor.

You <sup>[Gentiles]</sup> also, who are in Christ, after having heard the message of truth, the good news about your salvation, and <sup>[after]</sup> believing in Him, were sealed <sup>[i.e., as evidence of ownership]</sup> with the promised Holy Spirit. <sup>[or, “the Holy Spirit who brings promises”]</sup>.

He is a down payment of our inheritance, until God’s own possession <sup>[i.e., His people]</sup> receives redemption. <sup>[Note: Here “redemption” refers to receiving a new body in heaven]</sup>. May this bring about praise to God’s splendor!
For this reason also, since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all of the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>,
I do not stop thanking <sup>[God]</sup> for you. I keep asking, in my prayers,

that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, will give you a spirit <sup>[i.e., a disposition]</sup> of wisdom and revelation <sup>[or, “give you the spiritual gifts of wisdom and revelation”]</sup>, so you can have full knowledge of God.

I pray that your minds will be enlightened so that you will know what the hope is, to which God has called you, and what the glorious abundance is, of God’s inheritance in the saints <sup>[Note: Paul wanted them to appreciate the value God placed on them]</sup>,
and what God’s tremendous power <sup>[working]</sup> in us believers is. <sup>[This power is]</sup> the same as that utilized by God’s mighty strength,
which He exerted when raising Christ from the dead and seating Him at His right side in the heavenly realms <sup>[i.e., heaven itself]</sup>.

<sup>[There He is exalted]</sup> high above all rulers and authorities and powers and dominion and every name <sup>[i.e., title of honor]</sup> that can be given, not only in this present age but <sup>[also]</sup> in the one to come.
And God placed everything in subjection under His <sup>[i.e., Christ’s]</sup> feet, and appointed Him to be head over all things to the church,
which is His <sup>[spiritual]</sup> body. The church is completely filled by Christ <sup>[or, “Christ is completely filled by God”]</sup>, who completely fills everything in every way.
And you <sup>[Gentiles]</sup> were once <sup>[spiritually]</sup> dead because of your shortcomings and sins.

You practiced such things when you lived according to the evil ways of this world, and according to the ruler of the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> powers of the air. <sup>[Note: This refers to Satan, whose influence permeates life around us]</sup>. He is the <sup>[evil]</sup> spirit who is now at work in people who are disobedient <sup>[to God]</sup>.

All of us <sup>[Jews]</sup> also once lived among such people, fulfilling the improper cravings of our physical being and doing what our bodies and minds desired. In this natural <sup>[unconverted]</sup> state we were deserving of <sup>[God’s]</sup> wrath, just like the rest <sup>[of mankind]</sup>.
But God, who has great love for us and abundant mercy <sup>[toward us]</sup>,
restored <sup>[spiritual]</sup> life to us with Christ, even though we were <sup>[spiritually]</sup> dead because of our sins --- it is by God’s unearned favor that you have been saved.
God raised us up with Christ <sup>[See Col. 2:12]</sup> and seated us with Him in the heavenly realms <sup>[i.e., in the church. See 1:3]</sup> in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus.

This was so that, to future ages <sup>[of people on earth and in heaven]</sup>, God could demonstrate the tremendous abundance of His unearned favor, expressed by His kindness to us in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus.
For you have been saved by God’s unearned favor, through <sup>[your]</sup> faith, and that <sup>[salvation]</sup> was not the result of your own doing; it was the gift from God.
It was not the result of your own <sup>[good]</sup> deeds <sup>[See Titus 3:5]</sup>, so that no one could boast <sup>[about it]</sup>.

For we <sup>[Christians]</sup> are the product of God’s workmanship, <sup>[re-]</sup> created in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus for <sup>[the purpose of]</sup> doing good deeds. God had previously determined that this should be our practice.

So, you Gentiles by birth should remember what you once were. You are called “Uncircumcision” by those who are called “Circumcision” <sup>[i.e., the Jews]</sup>, (which refers to a physical procedure performed by people).

<sup>[Remember]</sup> that you were at that time without Christ; <sup>[you were]</sup> excluded from citizenship in the nation of Israel; <sup>[you were]</sup> foreigners regarding the Agreements of <sup>[God’s]</sup> promises. You had no hope and were without God in the world.

But now <sup>[that you are]</sup> in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ, you <sup>[Gentiles]</sup>, who were once far away <sup>[from God]</sup>, have been brought near <sup>[to Him]</sup> through the blood of Christ.
For Christ is <sup>[the source of]</sup> our peace. He made both <sup>[Jews and Gentiles]</sup> one people, and broke down the barrier wall of hostility that divided them,

by abolishing in His fleshly body the <sup>[entire Jewish]</sup> law of commandments contained in decrees. <sup>[This was]</sup> so that He could create in <sup>[unity with]</sup> Himself one new person <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup> out of the two peoples <sup>[i.e., Jews and Gentiles]</sup>, thereby bringing about peace <sup>[between them]</sup>.

<sup>[This was also intended]</sup> to reestablish harmony between both of these peoples and God in <sup>[this]</sup> one body, through <sup>[Christ’s death on]</sup> the cross. By doing this He put to death the hostility <sup>[between them]</sup>.
And Christ came and preached peace to you <sup>[Gentiles]</sup>, who were far away <sup>[from God]</sup>, and peace to those <sup>[Jews]</sup> who were near <sup>[to Him]</sup>.
For through Him we <sup>[Jews and Gentiles]</sup> both have access to the Father by means of one Holy Spirit.

So then, you <sup>[Gentiles]</sup> are no longer foreigners and aliens <sup>[i.e., to God’s promises. See verse 12]</sup>, but you are fellow-citizens with the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>, and are part of God’s family.
<sup>[You are]</sup> built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets <sup>[See I Cor. 12:28]</sup>, with Christ Jesus Himself being the principal stone by which the entire building is aligned.
In <sup>[harmony with]</sup> Him the entire building <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup> is properly assembled, rising into a dedicated temple for the Lord. <sup>[See I Cor. 3:16-17]</sup>.
In <sup>[harmony with]</sup> Him you people are also being constructed together into a place where God lives by His Spirit.
This is the reason that I, Paul, the prisoner of Christ Jesus on behalf of you Gentiles, <sup>[pray for you. See 3:14-19]</sup>.

<sup>[Note: Paul here digresses from his prayer, begun in 1:15-16, to explain the circumstances of his being a prisoner on behalf of these Gentiles]</sup>. <sup>[I assume]</sup> you have heard about my commission <sup>[to minister]</sup> to you, which was given to me by God’s unearned favor.

<sup>[It is]</sup> that God’s secret plan <sup>[i.e., revealed in the Gospel preached to Gentiles. See verse 6]</sup> was made known to me by way of a revelation. <sup>[See Acts 9:15; 22:21]</sup>. I have already written to you <sup>[about this]</sup> briefly <sup>[i.e., probably a reference to 1:9ff]</sup>.
So, when you read this you will be able to understand my insight into the secret plan of Christ,
which was not told to people of previous generations as it has now been revealed to His <sup>[i.e., Christ’s]</sup> holy apostles and prophets by the Holy Spirit.

This secret plan is that the Gentiles <sup>[now]</sup> share in the inheritance along with us <sup>[Jews]</sup>, and are fellow-members of the body <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup>, and that they share, along with us <sup>[Jews]</sup>, in the promise of the Gospel, in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus.
I was made a minister <sup>[of the Gospel]</sup> according to the gift of God’s unearned favor, which was given to me according to the working of His power.
This favor of preaching the unfathomable riches of Christ to the Gentiles was given to me <sup>[even though]</sup> I am the least significant of all saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>.

<sup>[I was given the responsibility]</sup> to enlighten all people regarding the working out of God’s secret plan, which has been kept hidden for ages in <sup>[the heart of]</sup> God, who created everything.

This was done so that now God’s many-sided wisdom could be made known through the church, to the rulers and authorities in the heavenly realms <sup>[Note: “Heavenly realms” here refers to the world around us, where these angelic beings (good or bad) view the unfolding of God’s wonderful plan]</sup>,
according to God’s eternal purpose which He carried out through Christ Jesus our Lord.
It is in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ that we have boldness and confident access <sup>[to God]</sup> through our faith in Him.
So, I am asking you people not to become discouraged over my sufferings on your behalf, for they are for your benefit <sup>[eventually]</sup>.
This is the reason that I bow my knees <sup>[in prayer]</sup> to the Father,
from whom every family in heaven and on earth receives its name. <sup>[Note: Here Paul resumes the prayer he began in verse 1]</sup>.
<sup>[I pray]</sup> that, according to God’s glorious abundance, He would empower you to become <sup>[spiritually]</sup> strong through His Holy Spirit in your inner person,
so that Christ will live in your hearts through faith. <sup>[I pray]</sup> that you will be rooted and grounded in <sup>[your]</sup> love,
so that you, along with all the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>, will be <sup>[spiritually]</sup> strong enough to grasp how wide and long and high and deep <sup>[Christ’s love is]</sup>;
and to know that His love for us surpasses <sup>[our]</sup> knowledge, so that you people will be completely filled with the fullness of God.
May there be to God, who is able to do infinitely more <sup>[for us]</sup> than we could <sup>[even]</sup> ask or think, according to <sup>[His]</sup> power at work in us ---
<sup>[I say]</sup> may there be praise to God in the church and in <sup>[or, “for”]</sup> Christ Jesus throughout all generations of people for ages and ages. May it be so.
I urge you, therefore, as a prisoner of the Lord, to live in a way that measures up to <sup>[the standard required by]</sup> God when He called you.
Live in a humble, gentle and patient way, showing tolerance <sup>[for the weaknesses]</sup> of one another out of love <sup>[for them]</sup>.
Eagerly try to maintain the unity produced by the Holy Spirit by being bound together in peace.
There is <sup>[only]</sup> one body <sup>[i.e., church]</sup> and one Holy Spirit, just as God also gave you one hope when He called you <sup>[See Titus 3:7]</sup>.
<sup>[There is]</sup> one Lord <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup>, one faith <sup>[i.e., one body of teaching]</sup>, one immersion <sup>[i.e., in water for the forgiveness of sins]</sup>,
and one God and Father of all, who is over all and <sup>[works]</sup> through all and is in all <sup>[obedient believers]</sup>.
But <sup>[God’s]</sup> unearned favor was given to each one of us, according to the gift allotted <sup>[to us]</sup> by Christ.

Therefore, it says <sup>[Psa. 68:18]</sup>, “When He ascended on high <sup>[i.e., to heaven]</sup>, He led a procession of captured prisoners <sup>[behind Him]</sup> <sup>[Note: This originally referred to God leading His people in triumph over Israel’s enemies, but here is applied to Christ]</sup>, and gave gifts to <sup>[various]</sup> people.”

(But what does “He ascended” mean if not that He also <sup>[first]</sup> descended to the lower parts of the earth <sup>[i.e., to the earth below]</sup>? <sup>[Note: Of the three principal interpretations given to this verse: a) Christ descended to earth, b) Christ descended to the grave, c) Christ descended to Mary’s womb. The first is to be preferred. See John 3:13; 6:38, 62]</sup>.

The One who descended <sup>[i.e., from heaven to earth]</sup> is the same One <sup>[i.e., Christ]</sup> who also ascended high above the all the heavens, so that He could fill the entire universe <sup>[with His presence]</sup>).
And He gave some people <sup>[the gift of being]</sup> apostles; and some people prophets; and some people evangelists; and some people pastors <sup>[i.e., elders]</sup> and teachers,

for the purpose of equipping the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> for the work of serving <sup>[others]</sup>, in order to build up the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> body of Christ <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup>.

<sup>[This is to be done]</sup> until we all <sup>[eventually]</sup> arrive at unity of the faith and full knowledge of the Son of God; at complete <sup>[spiritual]</sup> maturity; at the <sup>[full]</sup> height of Christ’s <sup>[spiritual]</sup> stature.

Then we will no longer be <sup>[spiritual]</sup> babies, tossed back and forth <sup>[by waves]</sup>, and blown around by every wind of <sup>[false]</sup> teaching, <sup>[deceived]</sup> by the clever trickery of people using deceptive schemes.

But <sup>[instead]</sup>, we should speak <sup>[and practice]</sup> truth in a loving way, so that we can, in all respects, grow up <sup>[spiritually]</sup> into <sup>[union with]</sup> Christ, who is the head <sup>[i.e., of His body, the church]</sup>.

From Him all <sup>[members of]</sup> the body are properly joined together by every supporting ligament, as each part contributes to the growth of the body and builds itself up through <sup>[mutual]</sup> love.
So, I tell you this, and insist <sup>[on it]</sup> by <sup>[the authority of]</sup> the Lord: Do not continue to live with worthless thoughts as <sup>[unconverted]</sup> Gentiles do.

Their understanding <sup>[of spiritual things]</sup> is darkened and excluded from the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> life that God gives, because of the ignorance in their minds and the stubbornness in their hearts.

They have stopped caring <sup>[i.e., about morality]</sup>, so God has abandoned them to lives of unrestrained indecency and to engage in all kinds of <sup>[moral]</sup> impurity, with a constant desire for more.
But you people did not learn Christ that way <sup>[i.e., the above described life is not compatible with a commitment to Christ]</sup>.
<sup>[I am]</sup> assuming that you have heard Him <sup>[i.e., through the Gospel message]</sup> and were taught the truth that is in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> Jesus.
You were <sup>[taught]</sup> to put away <sup>[the conduct of]</sup> your former lifestyle, your old person who is being corrupted by deceitful desires,
and to be renewed in your inner person.
And you should put on the new person who has been <sup>[re-]</sup> created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
So then, put away lying and <sup>[Zech 8:16]</sup>, “Each one should speak truthfully with his neighbor,” because we are parts of one another <sup>[i.e., in the one body]</sup>.

<sup>[Psa. 4:4 says]</sup>, “Be angry <sup>[i.e., at wrongdoing. See Mark 3:5]</sup>, but do not sin <sup>[i.e., by harboring bitterness or retaliation]</sup>.” Do not allow the sun to go down while you are still provoked.
And do not give the devil an opportunity <sup>[to lead you into sin]</sup>.

The person who has stolen must stop doing it; instead, he should work hard, doing something worthwhile with his own hands, so that he can have <sup>[money]</sup> to share with the person who needs it.

Do not allow unwholesome <sup>[Note: The Greek word for “unwholesome” is “rotten, diseased”]</sup> language to come from your mouth, but only what is helpful for building up those who need it, so that you can impart favor <sup>[i.e., benefit]</sup> to those who listen <sup>[to you]</sup>.

And do not grieve God’s Holy Spirit by whom you were sealed <sup>[i.e., given evidence of God’s ownership]</sup> until the day of redemption <sup>[i.e., until you receive your new body in heaven. See 1:14]</sup>.
So, put away from you all <sup>[kinds of]</sup> bitterness and anger and wrath and brawling and slander, along with all <sup>[kinds of]</sup> malice <sup>[i.e., bad attitudes]</sup>,
and be kind and compassionate toward one another, forgiving each other, just as God also forgave you <sup>[because of your fellowship]</sup> in Christ.
So, as dearly loved children, you people should <sup>[try to]</sup> be like God.
Live in a loving way, just like Christ loved us and gave Himself up <sup>[to die]</sup> for us as an offering and fragrant smelling sacrifice to God.

But you should not even talk about sexual immorality, or any type of <sup>[moral]</sup> impurity, or greed. <sup>[Note: Possibly “greed” here means “desire for more” of such things]</sup>. Such talk is not suitable for saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>.
Nor should obscenity, silly chatter or double-meaning jokes be used, because they are not proper either. But instead, <sup>[you should express]</sup> thanksgiving.

For you <sup>[should]</sup> know this for certain: No sexually immoral or <sup>[morally]</sup> impure person, or greedy person, who is <sup>[the same as]</sup> an idol worshiper, has any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God.
Do not let anyone deceive you with empty words, for because of such things, the wrath of God will fall upon people who are disobedient <sup>[to Him]</sup>.
So, do not participate with such individuals,

for you people were once darkness <sup>[i.e., when living in sin]</sup>, but now you are light in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> the Lord. <sup>[So]</sup>, live like people who belong to light <sup>[i.e., who live godly lives]</sup>,
(for the product of light consists of everything good and right and true).
Then you will demonstrate <sup>[or, “learn about”]</sup> a life that is very pleasing to the Lord.
Do not have fellowship with <sup>[people who practice]</sup> worthless deeds of darkness <sup>[i.e., sinful living]</sup>, but expose them instead.
For it is shameful even to talk about the things they do in private.

But when all <sup>[such]</sup> things are exposed by the light <sup>[i.e., the truth]</sup>, they become <sup>[clearly]</sup> visible <sup>[i.e., their true nature is revealed]</sup>, for everything so revealed becomes illuminated. <sup>[Note: Possibly this means “it is light that clearly reveals everything”]</sup>.

So, it says, “You who are asleep, wake up and rise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you <sup>[i.e., illuminate you]</sup>.” <sup>[Note: The foregoing words may have been an early saying, used at someone’s immersion, which included the ideas of “rising up” (See Col. 2:12) and “illumination” (See Heb. 6:4)] </sup>.
So, be careful how you live. Do not live ignorantly, but wisely,
making the most of every opportunity, because these days are evil.
Therefore, do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord wants you to do.
And do not get drunk on wine, which results in ruined lives, but be filled with the Holy Spirit.
Speak to one another by using psalms, hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making music with your hearts to the Lord.
Always give thanks to God the Father for everything in the name <sup>[i.e., by the authority]</sup> of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ.
Wives, you should submit to your own husbands, as you would to the Lord.
For the husband is the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> head over his wife, just as Christ is also the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> head over the church, since He is the Savior of the body.
But just as the church submits to Christ, so wives also should submit to their husbands in everything.
Husbands, you should love your wives just as Christ also loved the church, and gave up His life for her,

so that He could dedicate her <sup>[to Himself]</sup>, after cleansing her by means of the washing of water <sup>[i.e., immersion]</sup>, accompanied by the word. <sup>[Note: “The word” here refers either to a person’s profession of faith (See Rom. 10:9) or to the Gospel message (See Rom. 10:17), both of which are associated with immersion]</sup>.
<sup>[This is]</sup> so He could present the church to Himself in all her splendor, without any kind of stain or wrinkle, but that she should be holy and without <sup>[moral]</sup> flaw.
In the same way also, husbands should love their own wives as they love their own bodies. The man who loves his own wife <sup>[actually]</sup> loves himself.
For no <sup>[normal]</sup> person ever hated his own <sup>[physical]</sup> body, but <sup>[instead]</sup> he feeds and cares for it, just as Christ also does for the church,
because we are parts of His <sup>[spiritual]</sup> body.

<sup>[Gen. 2:24 says]</sup>, “For this reason a man will leave <sup>[the home of]</sup> his father and mother, and will cling to his wife, <sup>[so that]</sup> the two will become one flesh <sup>[i.e., united in such a close relationship, as to practically constitute one body]</sup>.”
The secret <sup>[truth]</sup> of this is great, but I am talking about Christ and the church.
However, each of you men individually should also love his own wife the way he loves himself. And the wife should respect her husband.
Children, you should obey your parents in the Lord <sup>[i.e., as part of your Christian responsibility]</sup>, for this is the right thing to do.
<sup>[Ex. 20:12 says]</sup>, “Show honor to your father and mother,” (which is the first commandment that carries the promise),
“that things may go well with you and that you will live a long time on earth.”
And you fathers <sup>[Note: This word probably includes both parents]</sup>, do not stir up your children to become angry, but bring them up in the training and instruction of the Lord.
Slaves, you should obey those who are your earthly masters, out of reverence and eagerness to please them, with a sincere heart, as you would <sup>[obey]</sup> Christ.
Do not serve them only when you are being watched, just to gain their approval, but as slaves of Christ, doing what God wants from your heart.
Render your service wholeheartedly, as you would to the Lord, and not <sup>[just]</sup> to people,
knowing that whatever good deed a person does, he will be rewarded for it from the Lord, whether he is a slave or a free person.

And you masters should treat your slaves the same way; do not threaten them, knowing that God, who is both their Master and yours, is in heaven and He does not respect any particular person <sup>[over another]</sup>.
Finally, be <sup>[spiritually]</sup> strong in the Lord and in His mighty power.
You people should put on the full armor of God, so that you will be able to withstand the devil’s schemes.

For our struggle is not <sup>[actually]</sup> against human beings, but against rulers and authorities and leaders of this world’s darkness, and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms. <sup>[Note: “Heavenly realms” here refers to the struggle against Satanic powers which permeates life around us]</sup>.

Therefore, put on the full armor of God, so that you can withstand <sup>[the devil, See verse 11]</sup> when the evil day comes. And, after you have done everything you can, still remain standing firm <sup>[in the faith]</sup>.
So, you people should stand firm <sup>[in the faith]</sup>, with the belt of truth firmly buckled around you, and with the “flack jacket” of righteousness on your chest.
And put on your shoes, ready to <sup>[go and]</sup> spread the Gospel of peace.
And along with everything else, take up the shield of faith, with which you will be able to extinguish all the fire-tipped arrows of the evil one.
And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Holy Spirit, which is God’s word.

<sup>[Then]</sup> offer all kinds of prayers and requests in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> the Holy Spirit at all times, and be alert constantly in making requests for all the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>.
And <sup>[also pray]</sup> for me to be given boldness of speech in opening my mouth to make known the secret plan of the Gospel,
for which I am an ambassador in prison. <sup>[And pray]</sup> that I may be able to proclaim the Gospel boldly, as I ought to.

But, so that you people also can know how I am doing and what is going on with me, Tychicus, the dearly loved brother and faithful minister in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord, will tell you everything.
I have sent <sup>[i.e., am sending]</sup> him to you for this very purpose, so you can know our situation and that he can encourage your hearts.
May there be peace and love, with faith, to the brothers <sup>[there]</sup> from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
May God’s unearned favor be upon all of those <sup>[there]</sup> who love our Lord Jesus Christ with an undying love.

<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus, <sup>[and is being sent]</sup> to all the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> who are at Philippi <sup>[Note: This is a town in present-day northeastern Greece, and the first place in Europe where Paul established Christianity]</sup>, along with the overseers <sup>[Note: These men were also called elders, shepherds or pastors]</sup> and deacons.
May unearned favor and peace be upon you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
I thank my God <sup>[in prayer]</sup> every time I think of you.
In my every request for all of you, I make it with joy
for your sharing <sup>[with me]</sup> in <sup>[carrying on]</sup> the work of the Gospel from the first day <sup>[I left there]</sup> until now. <sup>[See 4:14-16]</sup>.
I am confident of this very thing, that God, who began something good among you, will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ’s <sup>[return]</sup>.

It is right for me to think this way about all of you, because I have you in my heart <sup>[i.e., you are dear to me]</sup>, for you people are sharing in God’s unearned favor with me <sup>[Note: Paul here refers to his ministry]</sup>, both in my imprisonment and in my defense and confirmation of the Gospel <sup>[i.e., in my efforts to give proof of the Gospel’s truth]</sup>.
For God is my witness that I long to see you with the <sup>[same]</sup> affection that Christ Jesus has <sup>[for you]</sup>.
And I pray that your love may grow stronger and stronger, <sup>[along]</sup> with how much you know, and <sup>[that you may grow]</sup> in your ability to comprehend things.
Then you will be able to approve of <sup>[only]</sup> what is best and be sincere and without just blame on the day of Christ’s <sup>[return]</sup>,
having been full of qualities of righteousness, which come from Jesus Christ and produce honor and praise to God.
Now I want you to know, brothers, that the things that happened to me have resulted in the advancement <sup>[of the influence]</sup> of the Gospel rather <sup>[than hindering it]</sup>,
so that my imprisonment for Christ became known throughout the entire headquarters of the governor <sup>[i.e., among all his personnel]</sup>, and to everyone else.
And most of the brothers in the Lord’s <sup>[service]</sup>, through my imprisonment, gained confidence to speak out God’s message with greater courage and without fear.
Some people, indeed, are proclaiming Christ out of envy and strife <sup>[i.e., out of rivalry]</sup>, while others do it from goodwill.
Those who do it out of love <sup>[for me]</sup> know that I am prepared to defend the Gospel <sup>[i.e., before government authorities]</sup>.
But the others preach Christ out of rivalry, not sincerely, hoping to stir up trouble for me while I am in prison.

So, what <sup>[should I think about the situation]</sup>? Just this: I am happy and will continue to be happy that, in every way, Christ is being preached, <sup>[regardless of]</sup> whether it is in pretense or with sincerity.

For I know that, through your requests <sup>[in prayer]</sup> for me and the help provided by the Holy Spirit of Jesus Christ, this situation will result in my being rescued <sup>[i.e., released from imprisonment]</sup>.

It is my eager expectation and hope that I will not have to be ashamed about anything, but that I will have the courage, <sup>[both]</sup> now and always, <sup>[to so live]</sup> that Christ will be exalted <sup>[i.e., honored]</sup> in my body, whether I live or die.
For life to me is Christ, and if I die, it would be gain.
But if <sup>[continuing]</sup> to live in my body means accomplishing more work <sup>[for Christ]</sup>, then I do not know which to choose.

So, I am in a dilemma between the two choices. <sup>[On the one hand]</sup> I have the desire to depart <sup>[from this life]</sup> and be with Christ <sup>[in heaven]</sup>, which would be far better.
Yet <sup>[on the other hand]</sup>, to remain in my body is more necessary for your sakes.
So, being convinced of this, I know I will remain <sup>[in my body]</sup> and continue with all of you for your spiritual progress and joy in the faith,
so that, by my being with you again, you will be able to rejoice all the more in <sup>[our lives together with]</sup> Christ Jesus.

So, <sup>[whatever happens]</sup>, live your daily lives in such a way as to be worthy of the Gospel of Christ, so that, whether I come and see you or remain absent from you, I will hear about you, that you are standing firm in one spirit <sup>[i.e., united in purpose, and]</sup> contending with one mind for the faith of the Gospel <sup>[i.e., for the body of Christian teaching]</sup>.

<sup>[I also want to hear]</sup> that you are not being frightened by those who oppose you, which <sup>[courage in the face of opposition]</sup> is proof <sup>[to them, or you]</sup> that they will be destroyed, but that you will be saved, and this will <sup>[all]</sup> be done by God.
Because God has granted you <sup>[the privilege]</sup>, on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake.
<sup>[So]</sup>, you are now experiencing the same struggle you <sup>[once]</sup> saw me going through, and now are hearing that I still am.

Is there anything to be encouraged about by being in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ? Is there any comfort from His love? Is there any sharing with the Holy Spirit? <sup>[Have you received]</sup> any affection and compassion <sup>[from Christ]</sup>?
<sup>[Then]</sup> make me completely happy by being like-minded, having the same love <sup>[for one another]</sup>, being united in spirit and having the same purpose.
Do not do anything from rivalry or from empty conceit, but in humility, consider others better than yourselves.
Each of you should not look out for your own interests <sup>[only]</sup>, but also for those of other people.
You should think about things the way Christ Jesus did.
He existed in the form of God <sup>[i.e., He shared God’s very nature]</sup>, but did not consider <sup>[remaining]</sup> equal with God something <sup>[to continue]</sup> to hold onto.
Instead, He gave up what He had and took on the form <sup>[i.e., the nature]</sup> of a slave, becoming like a man, <sup>[and even]</sup> His appearance was found to be like a man’s.
He humbled Himself <sup>[by]</sup> becoming obedient <sup>[to God]</sup> to the point of death, even death on a cross.
Therefore, God also exalted Him to the highest position and gave Him the name <sup>[i.e., “Lord.” See verse 11]</sup>, which is superior to every <sup>[other]</sup> name.

<sup>[This was]</sup> so that, in <sup>[honor of]</sup> the name of Jesus, everyone’s knee in heaven, on earth and under the earth <sup>[i.e., all rational creatures]</sup> should bow <sup>[i.e., before God]</sup>,
and that everyone’s mouth should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

So then, my dear ones, just as you people were always obedient <sup>[to my teaching]</sup> when I was there with you, so now, in my absence <sup>[also]</sup>, be <sup>[obedient]</sup> all the more. <sup>[Continue to]</sup> complete your own salvation <sup>[i.e., by living faithfully to the end. See Rom. 13:11]</sup> with fear and trembling <sup>[i.e., with a reverent and submissive spirit]</sup>,
for it is God who is at work in you, both to motivate the desire and to carry out what pleases Him.
Do everything without complaining and arguing,

so that you will be without just blame and innocent <sup>[of wrongdoing]</sup>, children of God without fault in the middle of a crooked and perverted generation of people. You must shine among them like stars lighting up the sky,

as you hold out <sup>[i.e., offer to people]</sup> the message of <sup>[never ending]</sup> life. Then I will have something to boast about on the day of Christ’s <sup>[return, See 1:6]</sup>, that I had not run the race for nothing nor had worked for nothing.

But even if I am poured out like a drink offering <sup>[See Ex. 29:38-41]</sup> on the sacrifice and service produced by your faith, I am glad and will rejoice with all of you. <sup>[Note: Paul here likens the possible pouring out of his blood to the wine and oil of the Old Testament sacrifice, which is then likened to the Philippians’ faithful service to God]</sup>.
And you should be glad and share your joy with me also.

But I hope in <sup>[harmony with the wishes of]</sup> the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I could be encouraged when I learn of your situation <sup>[i.e., when Timothy returns with news from Philippi]</sup>.
For I do not have anyone else <sup>[i.e., as a fellow-worker]</sup> who thinks the same way I do, and who will genuinely care for your <sup>[spiritual and material]</sup> needs.
For everyone else is looking out for his own interests, not for those of Jesus Christ.
But you people know about his proven reliability, how he served with me in advancing <sup>[the work of]</sup> the Gospel just like a son working with his father.
So, I hope to send him <sup>[to you]</sup> as soon as I see how things will go with me.

But I am fully persuaded in <sup>[harmony with the wishes of]</sup> the Lord that I, myself, also will go to you soon. <sup>[Note: Paul here expresses the hope of being released from imprisonment, in spite of previous hints that he might be put to death. See 1:20; 2:17]</sup>.
But I considered it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow-worker and fellow-soldier, and your messenger <sup>[to me]</sup> and minister to my needs.
For he longed to see all of you and was very distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
And indeed, he was <sup>[so]</sup> sick that he almost died, but God had pity on him, and not only on him but also on me, so that I would not have one sorrow on top of another.

So, I am all the more eager to send him to you people, so that when you see him again you will be glad, and that my sorrow will be lessened <sup>[i.e., in knowing that you are relieved to see that he is well again]</sup>.
So, you should welcome him in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord with great joy, and regard such people with honor,
because he almost died for the work of Christ, risking his life to provide me with what you were not able to.

Finally, my brothers, rejoice in <sup>[your relationship to]</sup> the Lord. It does not trouble me to write the same things to you again, because you will be safer if I do. <sup>[Note: These “same things” may refer to warnings against false teachers which Paul had either spoken to them or written about previously]</sup>.

Watch out for the “dogs!” <sup>[Note: “Dogs” were viewed with disgust by the ancient Jews (Deut. 23:18; II Kings 8:13; Rev. 22:15), so the term is used here derogatorily for Jewish Christians who were trying to impose on the church the requirements of the Law of Moses]</sup>. Watch out for the evil workers! Watch out for those who mutilate the body meaninglessly! <sup>[Note: Paul here alludes, in a derogatory wordplay, to the Jewish practice of circumcision]</sup>.

For we <sup>[Christians]</sup> are the <sup>[true]</sup> circumcision <sup>[i.e., the real “Jews” in God’s eyes. See Rom. 2:28-29]</sup>. We worship by means of God’s Holy Spirit <sup>[i.e., are empowered and motivated by the Holy Spirit]</sup>, and boast in <sup>[the person and work of]</sup> Christ Jesus, and <sup>[we]</sup> place no confidence in the flesh <sup>[i.e., in any outward ritual or status for salvation]</sup>.

However I, myself, have <sup>[reasons for such]</sup> confidence in the flesh. <sup>[But]</sup> if any other person has <sup>[reasons for placing]</sup> confidence in the flesh, I have more <sup>[reasons]</sup>.

<sup>[I was]</sup> circumcised eight days after I was born; <sup>[I am]</sup> from the nation of Israelites; from the tribe of Benjamin and of Hebrew ancestry <sup>[i.e., an outstanding Hebrew]</sup>. As far as the Law of Moses is concerned, <sup>[I am]</sup> a Pharisee <sup>[i.e., a strict sect of the Jewish religion]</sup>.
As far as enthusiasm is concerned, I persecuted the church; as far as being righteous according to the Law of Moses is concerned, I was without just blame.
However, those things which were <sup>[once regarded as]</sup> gain to me, I have <sup>[now]</sup> considered to be loss for <sup>[the sake of]</sup> Christ.

What is more, I consider everything to be a loss compared to the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus <sup>[as]</sup> my Lord, for whose sake I have suffered the loss of all things. <sup>[I now]</sup> consider <sup>[all of]</sup> them to be trash, in order to gain Christ

and to be found in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Him, not having a righteousness of my own, acquired by observing the Law of Moses, but one acquired through believing in Christ. This righteousness, which comes from God, is based on faith.
I want to know Christ <sup>[as Savior]</sup> and to experience the power <sup>[produced]</sup> by His resurrection, and to share in His sufferings and become like Him in His death,
in order to, somehow, be raised out of the dead <sup>[i.e., to have never ending life]</sup>.

<sup>[It is]</sup> not that I have already arrived at the goal <sup>[of spiritual maturity]</sup>, or have already been made perfectly <sup>[righteous]</sup>, but I am pushing onward in order to take hold of what Christ Jesus took hold of me for <sup>[i.e., to receive all that Christ intended for me]</sup>.

Brothers, I consider that I, myself, have not yet taken hold <sup>[of Christ’s ultimate purpose for me]</sup>. But there is one thing that I am doing; I am forgetting about my past <sup>[See verses 5-7]</sup>, and reaching forward to what lies ahead.
I am pushing onward toward the goal for the prize of the upward call <sup>[i.e., to heaven]</sup> from God in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ.
So, all of us who are mature <sup>[Christians]</sup> should think this way. And if you people think differently about anything, God will reveal that to you also.
So, we should continue living by the same <sup>[standard]</sup> that we have <sup>[already]</sup> attained.
Brothers, you people should join together in imitating me, and take note of those who are following my example.

For I have often told you before, and am telling you again with tears in my eyes, that many people are living as the enemies of the cross of Christ <sup>[i.e., their lives oppose the purpose of Christ’s death]</sup>.

Their <sup>[ultimate]</sup> end is <sup>[spiritual]</sup> destruction; their god is their appetite; they boast of what they ought to be ashamed of, and they <sup>[constantly]</sup> think about worldly things.
But we are <sup>[really]</sup> citizens of heaven and we are waiting for a Savior <sup>[to return]</sup> from there, the Lord Jesus Christ,
