who will change our lowly <sup>[physical]</sup> bodies and make them like His glorious body. <sup>[He will do this]</sup> according to the power that enables Him even to bring everything into submission to Himself.

So, my dearly loved brothers <sup>[and sisters, See next two verses]</sup>, those whom I long to see, <sup>[who are]</sup> my <sup>[reason for]</sup> being glad and <sup>[who are]</sup> my crown of victory; stand firm in <sup>[your devotion to]</sup> the Lord, my dear ones.
I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to be like-minded in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> the Lord.

Yes, and I am asking you also, <sup>[my]</sup> faithful partner <sup>[Note: This person is unknown, but may have been one of Paul’s preacher companions]</sup>, help those women, for they have worked hard with me in <sup>[spreading]</sup> the Gospel, along with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow-workers, whose names are in the book of life. <sup>[See Rev. 3:5; Heb. 12:23]</sup>.
Rejoice in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> the Lord all the time. I will say it again: Rejoice!

You should let your gentleness be known to all people. The Lord is near. <sup>[Note: This “nearness” could refer to Christ’s second coming or to His nearness to His people in a spiritual sense]</sup>.
Do not worry about anything, but in everything, by prayer and <sup>[special]</sup> petition, along with thanksgivings, you should make your requests known to God.
And <sup>[when you do]</sup>, the peace from God, which surpasses all comprehension, will guard your hearts and thoughts <sup>[from anxiety]</sup> in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus.

Finally, brothers, give <sup>[careful]</sup> thought to these things: Whatever is true, whatever is noble, whatever is right, whatever is pure, whatever is lovable, and whatever is commendable. If it is excellent; if it is praiseworthy; <sup>[think about it]</sup>.
You people should do those things which you have learned and received and heard from me, and seen in me, and <sup>[then]</sup> the God <sup>[who gives]</sup> peace will be with you.

I am rejoicing very much in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> the Lord that now, after some time, you people have revived your concern for me. Actually, you were concerned, but lacked the opportunity <sup>[to show it]</sup>.
I am not saying this because of being in need, for I have learned to be content regardless of my circumstances.

I know how to get along in humble circumstances and how to live in prosperity. In every situation and in all circumstances, I have learned the secret both to be full and to be hungry, both to have plenty and to be in need.
I can do everything through Christ, who gives me the strength.
Nevertheless, it was really good of you people to share <sup>[with me]</sup> during my troubles <sup>[i.e., imprisonment]</sup>.

As you Philippians also know, during the early days of <sup>[my]</sup> preaching the Gospel <sup>[in those regions]</sup>, when I left Macedonia <sup>[Note: This was the northernmost province of Greece]</sup>, no church shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving <sup>[i.e., financial help]</sup>, except you only.
For even <sup>[when I was]</sup> in Thessalonica, you sent <sup>[money]</sup> more than once to meet my needs.
<sup>[Now]</sup> it is not that I am looking for a gift, but I am looking for the benefit that <sup>[such a gift]</sup> will add to your <sup>[spiritual]</sup> account <sup>[with God]</sup>.

But I have everything <sup>[I need]</sup>, and in abundance; I am fully supplied, having received from Epaphroditus the gifts that you sent, <sup>[which are like]</sup> a fragrant-smelling sacrifice, acceptable and very pleasing to God.
And my God will supply all your needs <sup>[also]</sup>, according to His glorious wealth <sup>[enjoyed by fellowship]</sup> in Christ Jesus.
Now may there be honor to our God and Father forever and ever. May it be so.
Greet every saint <sup>[i.e., God’s holy person]</sup> in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me <sup>[also]</sup> send their greetings to you.
All the saints <sup>[here with me]</sup> send you their greetings, especially those belonging to <sup>[Emperor]</sup> Caesar’s household.
May the favor of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by God’s will, and <sup>[from]</sup> our brother Timothy,

<sup>[and is being sent]</sup> to the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> and faithful brothers in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ <sup>[who live]</sup> at Colosse. May you have unearned favor and peace from God our Father.
Whenever we pray for you, we always thank God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,
for we have heard about <sup>[the evidence of]</sup> your faith in <sup>[the service of]</sup> Christ Jesus, and about the love you have <sup>[shown]</sup> for all the saints.
<sup>[You have these qualities]</sup> because of the hope stored up for you in heaven, which <sup>[hope]</sup> you previously heard about through the message of truth when the Gospel

came to you. This Gospel is <sup>[now]</sup> also producing fruit all over the world <sup>[i.e., throughout the Roman Empire]</sup> and growing <sup>[in its influence]</sup>, just as it is doing among you people also, since the day you <sup>[first]</sup> heard and understood God’s unearned favor in <sup>[all its]</sup> truth.
You learned <sup>[this truth]</sup> from Epaphras, our dearly loved fellow-slave, who is a faithful minister of Christ <sup>[to you]</sup> on our behalf.
He also told us about your love, <sup>[which is produced]</sup> by the Holy Spirit.

So, this is why, since the day we heard about these things, we do not stop praying for you and requesting that you people be filled <sup>[by God]</sup> with the knowledge of what He wants <sup>[for your lives]</sup>, and with spiritual wisdom and understanding.
<sup>[We pray for you]</sup> to live a life deserving of the Lord, pleasing to Him in every way, producing fruit in every good deed and growing in the knowledge of God.
<sup>[We also pray that you will be]</sup> strengthened with great power, according to God’s glorious might, so that you will have great endurance and patience <sup>[coupled]</sup> with joy.

<sup>[May you]</sup> give thanks to the Father who has enabled you to share <sup>[i.e., eventually]</sup> in the inheritance of the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> in <sup>[the kingdom of]</sup> light.
He rescued us from the domain of <sup>[spiritual]</sup> darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of the Son whom He loves,
through whom we receive redemption <sup>[i.e., are bought back from Satan]</sup>, which is the forgiveness of <sup>[our]</sup> sins.
He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn One <sup>[i.e., who takes preeminence]</sup> over all that is created.

For all things were created by Him; things in heaven and on earth; things visible and invisible, whether they are thrones <sup>[i.e., reigning beings]</sup>, or lords, or rulers or authorities. <sup>[Note: “Authorities” here may refer to angelic beings]</sup>. All things were created by Him and for Him.
And He existed before all things and all things are held together by Him.

And He is the head of the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> body, the church. He is <sup>[its]</sup> source, the firstborn One from the dead <sup>[i.e., the first one to return to life, never to die again]</sup>, so that He could rank first <sup>[in importance]</sup> over all things.
For God was very pleased to have all of <sup>[His]</sup> fullness dwell in Christ <sup>[See 2:9]</sup>,

and through Him to reconcile all things to Himself, whether things on earth or things in heaven. He made peace <sup>[with mankind]</sup> through the <sup>[sacrificial]</sup> blood of Christ’s <sup>[death on the]</sup> cross.
And though you were once estranged <sup>[from God]</sup> and were enemies <sup>[of His]</sup> in your mind because of your evil deeds,

yet now God has reconciled <sup>[you]</sup> to Himself through the death of Christ’s physical body in order to present you holy, without fault and free from just blame before Him <sup>[on judgment day]</sup>.

<sup>[This will happen]</sup> if you continue in the faith <sup>[Note: This means either “continue to observe God’s teaching” or, “continue to believe”]</sup>, firmly grounded and established, and not moved away from the hope offered by the Gospel message, which you have heard and which was preached to people throughout the entire created world under heaven. I, Paul, was made a minister of this Gospel.

Now I rejoice in <sup>[spite of]</sup> my sufferings for your sake, and I am filling up <sup>[i.e., completing]</sup> in my physical body what was lacking in the afflictions Christ endured for His body’s sake, the church.
I was made a minister of the church according to the task given to me by God to fully present His message to you.
<sup>[This message is]</sup> the secret which has been kept hidden throughout the ages and generations, but has now been revealed to God’s saints <sup>[i.e., His holy people]</sup>.

He was pleased to make known to these people the wealth of His splendor among the Gentiles; this hidden secret, which is Christ among you, <sup>[is]</sup> the hope of being honored <sup>[i.e., in heaven]</sup>.
We proclaim Him, warning and teaching every person with all wisdom, so that we may present every person <sup>[to God]</sup> complete in <sup>[their fellowship with]</sup> Christ.
This is the reason that I work hard and struggle also, using God’s mighty power which is at work in me.

I want you to know how much I am struggling for you <sup>[Note: This probably refers to the great effort Paul was exerting in prayer, preaching, etc.]</sup>, and for those at Laodicia <sup>[Note: This was a town about ten miles west of Colosse]</sup>, and for all those who have never met me in person.

<sup>[I do this]</sup> so that their hearts will be encouraged <sup>[while]</sup> being united in <sup>[mutual]</sup> love, <sup>[and]</sup> so that they will have the wealth of a fully assured understanding, in order to fully know about God’s hidden secret, <sup>[which is]</sup> Christ.
In Him all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden.
I say this so that no one will deceive you with persuasive arguments.

For even though I am absent <sup>[from you]</sup> in body, still I am present with you in spirit, rejoicing and observing <sup>[i.e., with my mind’s eye]</sup> your orderly behavior and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
So, since you have accepted Christ Jesus as Lord, live in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Him,
rooted and built up in <sup>[your relationship to]</sup> Him, established by your faith <sup>[or, “in the faith”]</sup>, just as you were taught, overflowing with thanksgiving.

Pay attention so that no one takes you <sup>[spiritually]</sup> captive through his philosophy and empty deception based on human tradition, according to the principles of the world and not according to Christ.
For all of the fullness of Deity lives in His body,
and you people have been made full <sup>[i.e., spiritually complete]</sup> in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Him, who is the <sup>[spiritual]</sup> head over all who rule and have authority.

In <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Him you people were also circumcised with a circumcision not performed by human beings, but performed by Christ, consisting of the removal of the body of <sup>[sinful]</sup> flesh.

<sup>[This happened when you were]</sup> buried with Christ in <sup>[the act of]</sup> immersion, from which you were also raised up with Him through <sup>[your]</sup> faith in the accomplishment of God, who raised Him up from the dead.

And when you were <sup>[spiritually]</sup> dead because of your sins and your lack of being physically circumcised <sup>[Note: This refers to their having been unconverted Gentiles and therefore outside of a relationship with God]</sup>, God made you alive <sup>[again]</sup> together with Christ, having forgiven us for all of our sins.

He canceled the document written in ordinances <sup>[Note: This obviously refers to the Mosaic Law]</sup> that was against us and was opposed to us, and has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross.

<sup>[In doing this]</sup> Christ disarmed the rulers and authorities <sup>[i.e., evil forces]</sup> and made a public display of them, triumphing over them through the cross. <sup>[Note: This could possibly be rendered “God triumphed over them through Christ”]</sup>.

So, do not allow anyone to judge <sup>[i.e., condemn]</sup> you for what you eat, or what you drink, or regarding <sup>[your non­observance of]</sup> a <sup>[yearly]</sup> festival, or a <sup>[monthly]</sup> “new moon” observance, or a <sup>[weekly]</sup> Sabbath day.

These things are <sup>[only]</sup> a shadow of future things, but the body <sup>[i.e., the real substance of things]</sup> belongs to Christ. <sup>[Note: The idea here is that the ritual observances of the Mosaic Law were like shadows pointing to the reality of life in Christ]</sup>.

Do not allow anyone, who delights in acting humble <sup>[i.e., living an ascetic life]</sup> and who worships angels, to rob you of your reward <sup>[in heaven]</sup>. <sup>[Note: This “angel worship” could mean holding angels in high regard because of their role as God’s messengers, or the claim that God can be approached only through angelic intermediaries]</sup>. He places great importance on what he <sup>[claims to]</sup> see <sup>[i.e., visions]</sup>, being inflated with pride by his fleshly mind for no <sup>[good]</sup> reason.

He is not holding on <sup>[firmly]</sup> to the head <sup>[i.e., Christ, the head of the church]</sup>, from whom the entire body, being nourished and supported by its ligaments and joints, is caused to grow in size by God.

Since you died with Christ to the basic principles of the world, <sup>[then]</sup> why, as though you <sup>[still]</sup> lived in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> the world, do you submit yourselves to <sup>[its]</sup> rules, <sup>[such as]</sup>:
“Do not handle <sup>[this]</sup>; do not taste <sup>[that]</sup>; do not touch <sup>[those things]</sup>?”

(All these things <sup>[i.e., the things handled, tasted or touched]</sup> are destined to perish with <sup>[constant]</sup> use). <sup>[These rules are]</sup> based on the commands and teachings of men.

They certainly have an appearance of wisdom, <sup>[displaying]</sup> self-prescribed worship and <sup>[false]</sup> humility and asceticism, but they lack any value in curbing the desires of the physical passions.

If then you people have been raised up together with Christ <sup>[i.e., from your burial in immersion, See 2:12-13 with Rom. 6:4]</sup>, pay attention to the things that are above <sup>[i.e., heavenly things]</sup>, where Christ is seated at the right side of God.
Focus your minds on the things that are above, not on earthly things.
For you died <sup>[with Christ, See 2:20]</sup>, and your life is <sup>[now]</sup> hidden with Christ in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God.

<sup>[So]</sup>, when Christ, who is your <sup>[or, “our” source of]</sup> life, appears <sup>[i.e., at His second coming]</sup>, then you people also will appear with Him in splendor <sup>[i.e., in heaven]</sup>.

So, put to death <sup>[i.e., stop practicing]</sup> the desires of your physical bodies <sup>[such as]</sup> sexual immorality, <sup>[moral]</sup> impurity, lust, evil desires and greed, which is <sup>[in reality]</sup> idolatry.
For the wrath of God will come <sup>[upon disobedient people]</sup> for practicing such things.
You people also once lived like this when you practiced these things.
But now you also should stop practicing them: anger, wrath, malice, slander, filthy language from your mouth.
Do not lie to one another, since you have put away <sup>[the conduct of]</sup> your old person with its practices,
and have put on the new person, who is being <sup>[daily]</sup> renewed with full knowledge, according to the image of God, who <sup>[re-]</sup> created him.

<sup>[In this state]</sup> there cannot be <sup>[such distinctions as]</sup> Greek <sup>[i.e., Gentile]</sup> and Jew; circumcised people and non-circumcised people; barbarian and Scythian <sup>[Note: These last two words refer to foreigners whose language was unintelligible and who were uncivilized heathens]</sup>, or slave and free person; but Christ is all <sup>[that is important]</sup> and <sup>[He is]</sup> in all <sup>[of His people]</sup>.
So, as God’s elect <sup>[i.e., His people]</sup>, holy and dearly loved <sup>[by Him]</sup>, clothe yourselves with a heart of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience.
Tolerate one another’s <sup>[weaknesses]</sup> and forgive each other if anyone <sup>[of you]</sup> has a complaint against someone else. You should forgive people just as the Lord forgave you.
And beyond all these things, clothe yourselves with love, which binds these things together in complete <sup>[unity]</sup>.
You should let the peace given by Christ rule in your hearts, since you people were called <sup>[to be at peace]</sup> in one body <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup>; and you should be thankful.

You should let the message of Christ live in you abundantly <sup>[i.e., as individuals and collectively]</sup>. Use <sup>[much]</sup> wisdom in teaching and warning one another with psalms, hymns and spiritual songs, singing with favor <sup>[i.e., with thankfulness]</sup> in your hearts toward God.
And whatever you do, whether in <sup>[your]</sup> words or actions, do everything in the name <sup>[i.e., by the authority]</sup> of Christ, giving thanks to God the Father through Him.
You wives should submit to your husbands, as this is proper in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord.
You husbands should love your wives and not be harsh toward them.
You children should obey your parents in everything, for this is very pleasing to the Lord.
You fathers <sup>[Note: By implication, this would include both parents]</sup> should not stir up your children <sup>[i.e., irritate or exasperate them]</sup>, so that they do not become discouraged.

You slaves should obey your earthly masters in everything, <sup>[and]</sup> do not do it just when they are looking <sup>[at you]</sup>, or to gain their favor, but do it with a sincere heart, out of reverence for the Lord.
In whatever you do, work wholeheartedly, as though it were for the Lord and not <sup>[just]</sup> for people,
knowing that you will receive from the Lord a <sup>[spiritual]</sup> inheritance as a reward, <sup>[for]</sup> you are <sup>[really]</sup> serving the Lord.
And the person who does what is wrong will be repaid for his wrongdoing, for there is no favoritism <sup>[with God]</sup>.
You masters should treat your slaves in a just and fair way, knowing that you also have a Master <sup>[who]</sup> is in heaven.
Continue to pray regularly; stay alert and be thankful.

At the same time, pray for us also, that God will open up a door <sup>[of opportunity]</sup> for <sup>[proclaiming]</sup> the message, so we can tell about the <sup>[previously]</sup> hidden secret of Christ, for which I have also been imprisoned.
<sup>[Pray]</sup> that I will make it <sup>[clearly]</sup> known, as I ought to.
Be wise in the way you live in front of outsiders <sup>[i.e., non-Christians]</sup>, making the most of every opportunity.
Your speech should always be favorable, seasoned with salt <sup>[i.e., interesting and appropriate]</sup>, so that you may know how to properly answer each person <sup>[you talk with]</sup>.

Tychicus will tell you all about my affairs. <sup>[Note: This man probably delivered this letter as well as the one to the Ephesians. See Eph. 6:21]</sup>. He is our dearly loved brother and faithful minister and fellow-slave in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord.
I have sent <sup>[i.e., am sending]</sup> him to you for this very purpose, so that you can know our situation and that he can encourage your hearts.

<sup>[He is coming]</sup>, together with Onesimus our faithful and dearly loved brother, who is one of you. <sup>[Note: This could mean either “from your town” or, “a fellow-Christian”]</sup>. They will tell you about everything that is happening here.

Aristarchus, my fellow-prisoner, sends you his greetings, along with Barnabas’ cousin <sup>[John]</sup> Mark. <sup>[See Acts 12:12]</sup>. (You have received instructions to welcome Mark if he comes to you).
J, who is called Justus, also sends his greetings. These men are the only Jews among my fellow-workers for God’s kingdom; they have proved to be an encouragement to me.

Epaphras, who is one of you <sup>[See 1:7; 4:9]</sup>, a slave of Christ Jesus, sends you his greetings. He always struggles in his prayers on your behalf so that you will remain mature and be fully convinced <sup>[that you are living]</sup> by all that God wants <sup>[for your life]</sup>.
For I testify about Epaphras, that he has worked hard for you, and <sup>[also]</sup> for those people in Laodicia and Hierapolis. <sup>[Note: These are towns close to Colosse]</sup>.
Luke, my dearly loved physician, and Demas <sup>[See II Tim. 4:10]</sup> send you their greetings.
Greet the brothers who are at Laodicia, and Nympha, along with the church that meets in her house.

And when this letter has been read to you people, see that it is read in the church of the Laodicians also. And see that you read the letter <sup>[coming]</sup> from Laodicia also. <sup>[Note: This could have been a reference to the letter to the Ephesians]</sup>.

And tell Archippus <sup>[Note: He may have been the preacher at Colosse or Laodicia. See Philemon 1:1-2]</sup>; “See to it that you fulfill your ministry <sup>[there]</sup>, which you have received in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord.”

This greeting <sup>[is being written]</sup> by me, Paul, in my own handwriting. Remember my imprisonment. <sup>[Note: This was probably a request for them to pray for him]</sup>. May God’s favor be with you.

<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, and Silvanus and Timothy, <sup>[and is being sent]</sup> to the church of the Thessalonians <sup>[Note: This was a town in present-day northeastern Greece]</sup> in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God, the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. May God’s unearned favor and peace be upon you.
We always thank God for all of you, <sup>[regularly]</sup> mentioning you in our prayers.
We continually remember, in the presence of our God and Father, the work produced by your faith, the labor prompted by your love, and your endurance motivated by your hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
We know, my brothers who are dearly loved by God, about your election <sup>[i.e., that you were chosen by God because of your obedient faith]</sup>,

for our Gospel came to you, not merely in words, but also in power, in <sup>[a demonstration of]</sup> the Holy Spirit <sup>[See I Cor. 2:4]</sup> and with <sup>[“our” or “your”]</sup> deep conviction <sup>[of its truth]</sup>. You people know what kind of men we were while living among you for your benefit.
And you became imitators of us and of the Lord. <sup>[And even though]</sup> you suffered much affliction, you welcomed the message with joy <sup>[given to you]</sup> by the Holy Spirit,
and so became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia. <sup>[Note: These two provinces make up present-day Greece]</sup>.

For the Lord’s message sounded forth from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but your faith toward God <sup>[also]</sup> spread everywhere <sup>[else]</sup>, so that we do not have to say anything <sup>[about you]</sup>.
For those people themselves report on what kind of a reception we had from you, and how you people turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God.

And <sup>[they tell how you are]</sup> waiting for His Son <sup>[to come]</sup> from heaven, the One He raised up from the dead --- this Jesus, who rescues us from the coming wrath <sup>[of God]</sup>.
For you brothers <sup>[should]</sup> know that our coming to you was not a waste of time.

But <sup>[even though]</sup> we had already suffered and been mistreated at Philippi <sup>[See Acts 16:19-24]</sup>, as you people know, we had the boldness, <sup>[with help]</sup> from God, to tell you His message in spite of much struggle <sup>[i.e., opposition]</sup>.
For our appeal <sup>[to you was]</sup> not based on error, or impure <sup>[motives]</sup>, or deception.
Instead, we speak as men approved by God to be entrusted with the Gospel. So, we speak, not trying to please people but God, who examines <sup>[the motives of]</sup> our hearts.
For we were never found using flattering words at any time, as you know, nor did we try to cover up any greedy desires. God can testify <sup>[to this]</sup>.

<sup>[And]</sup> we were not looking for honor from people, either from you or from anyone else, even though, as apostles of Christ, we could have become a burden <sup>[to you]</sup>. <sup>[Note: The idea seems to be that the apostles could have claimed financial support, but chose not to]</sup>.
But we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother caring for her children.
And so, since we have such deep affection for you, we were very pleased to share with you, not only the Gospel of God, but also our own lives, because you had become very dear to us.
For you brothers remember how we labored and toiled, working hard night and day, so that we would not burden any of you <sup>[while]</sup> we proclaimed to you the Gospel of God.
You can testify, <sup>[just]</sup> as God can, how holy, righteous and blameless our conduct was toward you believers.
For you know that we treated each one of you like a father treats his own children, exhorting you, and comforting you,
and urging you to live up to the standards of God, who calls you into His own kingdom and splendor.

And because of this we also thank God continually that, when you people received the message of God, which you heard from us, you did not accept it as a message from <sup>[mere]</sup> human beings, but as it truly is, the message from God, which is at work in you believers.

For you brothers became imitators of the churches of God in Judea, which are in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ, for you people also suffered the same things from your own countrymen that they did from the Jews,

who killed the Lord Jesus and the <sup>[Old Testament]</sup> prophets and also drove us <sup>[apostles]</sup> out <sup>[of Thessalonica and Berea. See Acts 17]</sup>. They are not pleasing to God and are hostile to all people,

for they <sup>[even]</sup> try to prevent us from speaking to the Gentiles, so that they can be saved. <sup>[In doing this]</sup> they are always piling up one sin on another. But the wrath <sup>[of God]</sup> has come upon them at last <sup>[or, “completely”]</sup>.

But brothers, after we were separated from you for a short time --- in person but not in spirit --- we became all the more eager <sup>[to come to you]</sup> and strongly desired to see you in person. <sup>[Note: The word “separated” here denotes the traumatic experience of a child being forcibly removed from its parents. It had been about six months since Paul left Thessalonica under adverse circumstances]</sup>.

For we <sup>[really]</sup> wanted to come to you <sup>[and]</sup> I, Paul, <sup>[even tried to come]</sup> more than once, but Satan prevented us. <sup>[Note: This was probably accomplished through the influence of certain people or circumstances]</sup>.

For what is <sup>[the basis of]</sup> our hope, or joy, or crown of honor in the presence of the Lord Jesus when He comes back? <sup>[Note: By “crown” Paul alludes to the laurel wreath awarded to the victor in an athletic contest]</sup>. Will it not be you people?
For you people are <sup>[indeed]</sup> our <sup>[occasion for]</sup> honor and joy.

So, when we could not stand it any longer <sup>[Note: Paul’s concern for the spiritual well-being of the Thessalonian Christians became unbearable]</sup>, we <sup>[i.e., I, See verse 5]</sup> thought it best to be left behind, alone, at Athens <sup>[Note: Paul was willing to labor alone at Athens, so he could send his fellow-workers to minister to others. See Acts 17]</sup>,
and so we <sup>[i.e., I]</sup> sent Timothy, our brother and God’s fellow-worker in <sup>[the ministry of]</sup> the Gospel, to strengthen and encourage you in your faith,
so that no one would be shaken by <sup>[having to experience]</sup> these difficult times. <sup>[See 2:14]</sup>. For you yourselves know that we are destined to have such experiences.
For even when we were with you, we warned you that we were going to be persecuted, and as you know, it has turned out that way.

<sup>[So]</sup>, for this reason, when I could not stand it any longer, I also sent <sup>[Timothy]</sup> to find out about your faith. I was afraid that somehow the tempter <sup>[i.e., Satan]</sup> had tempted you <sup>[to give up]</sup> and that our work <sup>[on your behalf]</sup> will have become useless.

But now Timothy has returned to us from you and brought us good news about your faith and love, and <sup>[reported]</sup> that you have good memories of us. <sup>[He told us that you are]</sup> longing to see us, just as we also long to see you.
So, brothers, through all of our distress and persecution, we were encouraged over you because of your faith,

for now we <sup>[really]</sup> live, if you <sup>[continue to]</sup> stand firm in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord <sup>[i.e., Paul could enjoy life much more in knowing that the Thessalonians were living for the Lord faithfully]</sup>.
For how can we thank God <sup>[enough]</sup> for you, in return for all the joy we have over you, in the presence of our God?
Night and day we pray very earnestly that we will be able to see you in person, so we can provide what is lacking in your faith.
Now may our God and Father Himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, open the way for us to go to you.
And may the Lord cause your love for one another, and for everyone, to increase and overflow, just as ours does for you also.

May the Lord strengthen your hearts so you will be <sup>[found]</sup> without just blame and holy, in the presence of our God and Father, when our Lord Jesus Christ returns with all of His holy ones <sup>[i.e., angels. See Mark 8:38; II Thess. 1:7]</sup>.

Finally then, brothers, we request and urge you, in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord, that you should live <sup>[dedicated lives]</sup> and please God. Just as you people received direction from us <sup>[on doing this]</sup>, see that you <sup>[continue to]</sup> do it more and more.
For you know <sup>[i.e., are aware of]</sup> the instructions we gave you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
For it is God’s will that you should be dedicated to Him, <sup>[that is]</sup>, you should avoid sexual immorality.
Each of you should know how to control his own body in a dedicated and honorable way,
not in passionate lust, as the <sup>[unconverted]</sup> Gentiles, who do not know God, do.
And none of you should violate and exploit his brother <sup>[or sister]</sup> in such a matter. For the Lord will punish people for all these things, just as we told you before and warned you.
For God did not call us to be morally impure, but to be dedicated to Him.
So, the person who rejects <sup>[this exhortation]</sup> is not <sup>[merely]</sup> rejecting what man says but God <sup>[Himself]</sup>, who gives His Holy Spirit to you.
Now you people do not need anyone to write to you about loving <sup>[your]</sup> brothers, for you yourselves have been taught by God to love one another.

For you certainly do practice it toward all the brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup> throughout Macedonia. <sup>[Note: This was the province in which Thessalonica was located]</sup>. Yet we urge you, brothers, to continue doing it more and more.

<sup>[We also urge you to]</sup> make it your aim to live a quiet life, and to attend to your own business matters, and to work with your hands, as we urged you. <sup>[Note: Possibly this exhortation was directed toward the tendency of some people there to neglect ordinary responsibilities in view of an imminent return of Christ]</sup>.
<sup>[Do these things]</sup> so that you will live in a proper way in front of outsiders <sup>[i.e., non-Christians]</sup>, and not have <sup>[to depend on people for]</sup> any needs.
But we want you to be informed, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep <sup>[in death]</sup> so that you do not grieve, as other people do <sup>[i.e., the unsaved]</sup>, who have no hope.

For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again <sup>[from the dead]</sup>, <sup>[we also believe]</sup> that God will bring <sup>[back to life]</sup>, along with Jesus, those in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Him who have fallen asleep <sup>[in death]</sup>.

For we say this to you by <sup>[the authority of]</sup> the word of the Lord <sup>[Note: Paul was either quoting what Jesus had previously said, or was giving a revelation he had received from the Lord. See I Cor. 14:37]</sup>, that we <sup>[Christians]</sup> who are <sup>[still]</sup> alive, who are left <sup>[on earth]</sup> when the Lord returns, will certainly not precede those who have fallen asleep. <sup>[Note: The point Paul is making is that the living will not be “changed” (I Cor. 15:51-52) and taken to heaven before the dead are raised]</sup>.

For the Lord Himself <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup> will come down from heaven with a commanding shout <sup>[Note: This probably refers to Jesus’ voice. See John 5:25, 28]</sup>, <sup>[and]</sup> with the archangel’s voice <sup>[i.e., Michael. See Jude 9]</sup> and with the sound of God’s trumpet; <sup>[at this]</sup> the dead in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ will rise <sup>[from the dead]</sup> first.

Then, we who <sup>[will still]</sup> be alive, that are left, will be caught up together with them into the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And so <sup>[from then on]</sup> we will always be with the Lord.
So, encourage one another with these words.
Now brothers, you do not need to have anything written to you about the times and dates <sup>[i.e., of Christ’s second coming]</sup>,
for you know perfectly well that the day of the Lord <sup>[i.e., when He returns, See II Thess. 2:1-2]</sup> will arrive like a thief coming in the night.

<sup>[For]</sup> when people are saying, “Peace,” and “Safety,” then <sup>[is when]</sup> destruction will come upon them suddenly, like labor pains on a pregnant woman, and they will not be able to escape it.
But you brothers are not in the dark about that day, so that it sneaks up on you like a thief,

for all of you people belong to the light; you belong to the daytime. We do not belong to the night or to darkness. <sup>[Note: In this passage “light” and “daytime” refer to spiritual illumination, while “darkness” and “night” refer to spiritual ignorance]</sup>.
So then, we should not be <sup>[spiritually]</sup> asleep, like the rest <sup>[i.e., the unsaved world]</sup>, but we should be alert and sober <sup>[i.e., in control of our senses]</sup>.
For people sleep at night, and get drunk at night <sup>[i.e., generally speaking]</sup>.
But, since we belong to the daytime <sup>[i.e., are enlightened]</sup>, we should be sober. We should put on the “flack jacket” of faith and love, and for a helmet, the hope of being saved.
For God did not appoint us to <sup>[suffer His]</sup> wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ
who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep <sup>[i.e., alive or dead]</sup>, we could live together with Him.
Therefore, <sup>[continue to]</sup> encourage one another, and build each other up <sup>[spiritually]</sup>, just as you are also doing.

But we urge you, brothers, to know <sup>[i.e., appreciate and respect]</sup> those who work hard among you, and who lead you and warn you in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord. <sup>[Note: These men were probably the elders. See Acts 20:28; Heb. 13:17]</sup>.
And regard them very highly, in a loving way, because of the work they do. You should live at peace with one another.

And we urge you, brothers, to warn those who are idle <sup>[Note: The word “idle” originally meant, “insubordinate, or unruly.” See II Thess. 3:6-7, 11]</sup>, encourage those who are timid, help the <sup>[spiritually]</sup> weak and be patient with everyone.
Make sure that no one does anything wrong <sup>[back]</sup> to someone who wrongs him, but always look for something good you can do for one another and for everyone.
You should be joyful at all times.
Never stop praying.
Thank <sup>[God]</sup> for everything, for this is what God wants for you in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> Christ Jesus.
Do not squelch the <sup>[influence of the]</sup> Holy Spirit <sup>[in your lives]</sup>.
Do not look down on prophetic messages.
Put everything <sup>[i.e., speakers and their messages]</sup> to the test, <sup>[then]</sup> hold onto what is good.
Avoid <sup>[the practice of]</sup> every kind of evil.

And may God Himself, who gives peace, dedicate you completely, and may your spirit, soul and body <sup>[i.e., your entire person]</sup> be kept without just blame at <sup>[i.e., until]</sup> the return of our Lord Jesus Christ.
God, who calls you <sup>[into His fellowship]</sup> is faithful; He will also do <sup>[what He promises]</sup>.
Brothers, pray for us.
Greet all of the brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup> with a holy kiss <sup>[i.e., to signify affection, yet without sensuality]</sup>.
I am ordering you, by <sup>[the authority of]</sup> the Lord, that this letter be read to all the brothers.
May the unearned favor of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, <sup>[and is being sent]</sup> to the church of the Thessalonians in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
May you have unearned favor and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
We ought to thank God for you always, brothers, since it is proper, because your faith is growing so much and the love that every one of you has for each other is increasing more and more.
Therefore, we ourselves speak proudly about you among the churches of God because of your steadfastness and faith amid all the persecutions and afflictions that you are enduring.

The way you are faithfully enduring <sup>[such]</sup> suffering for the kingdom of God is clear evidence that God’s judgment is right in <sup>[eventually]</sup> counting you worthy of it <sup>[i.e., of entering the eternal phase of the kingdom]</sup>.
For it is a just thing for God to repay with affliction those people who are troubling you,

and to provide <sup>[future]</sup> rest for you, along with us, who are afflicted. <sup>[This will occur]</sup> when Jesus is revealed from heaven in blazing fire <sup>[i.e., at His second coming]</sup>, accompanied by His mighty angels.
He will inflict punishment on those people who do not know God <sup>[as Savior]</sup>, and on those who do not obey the Gospel message of our Lord Jesus.
They will pay the penalty of never ending destruction, being banished from the presence of the Lord and from His mighty splendor.

On that day Jesus will return to be honored by His saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup> and to be marveled at <sup>[i.e., in reverence]</sup> by all those who had believed <sup>[in Him]</sup>. <sup>[This will happen]</sup> because you have believed our testimony to you <sup>[about Christ]</sup>.

This is why we always pray for you also, so that our God will consider you worthy to be <sup>[i.e., to have been]</sup> called <sup>[into the kingdom]</sup> by Him, and will fulfill, by His power, every good purpose <sup>[or, “desire for goodness”]</sup> of yours and every act produced by <sup>[your]</sup> faith.
<sup>[We pray this]</sup> so that the name of our Lord Jesus will be honored by you, and that you will be honored by Him, according to the unearned favor of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Now concerning the return of our Lord Jesus Christ, and our being gathered to Him, brothers,

we request that you people do not become shaken up in your mind, nor alarmed, either by a spirit <sup>[i.e., by some prophetic prediction]</sup>, or by a report, or by a letter supposedly from us, that the day of the Lord <sup>[i.e., when Jesus returns]</sup> has already come.

Do not allow anyone to deceive you in any way, for it will not occur until the rebellion comes first and the man of lawlessness becomes evident. <sup>[Note: This “rebellion” refers to a significant falling away from the teaching and practice of true Christianity (See I Tim. 4:1), characterized by the rise of an authoritarian religious leader]</sup>. This man is doomed to be destroyed

<sup>[for]</sup> he opposes <sup>[the true Gospel message]</sup> and exalts himself against everything that is called “God” or that which is worshiped. So, he sits in God’s temple <sup>[i.e., the church (?)]</sup> displaying himself as <sup>[though he were]</sup> God.
Do you not remember that I used to tell you these things when I was still with you?

<sup>[But]</sup> for now, there is something holding him back <sup>[i.e., holding back the man of lawlessness from emerging, See verses 3-4]</sup>, and you people know what it is. <sup>[It is restraining him]</sup> so that he will not be revealed until his own <sup>[due]</sup> time.

For this mysterious lawlessness is already at work <sup>[or, “this lawlessness is already secretly at work”]</sup>; but the one who is holding it back <sup>[will continue to do so]</sup> until he is removed.

And then the lawless person <sup>[See verse 3]</sup> will be revealed, the one whom the Lord Jesus will destroy with the breath of His mouth and bring to an end by the brilliant appearance of His presence <sup>[i.e., at His second coming]</sup>.
The coming <sup>[of this lawless person]</sup> is according to the work of Satan, <sup>[and will be]</sup> attended by all kinds of powerful <sup>[i.e., miraculous]</sup> signs and false wonders,
and all kinds of wicked deception toward those people who are perishing <sup>[spiritually]</sup>, because they refused to love the truth so they could be saved.
And this is the reason that God sends them a strong delusion <sup>[to influence them]</sup>: It is so that they would believe the lie,
in order that all those who have not believed the truth, but have taken pleasure in wrongdoing, would be condemned.

But we ought to thank God for you always, brothers <sup>[who are]</sup> dearly loved by the Lord, because God has chosen you from the beginning <sup>[Note: Some manuscripts use the Greek word for “first fruits” instead of “beginning” here, but it is difficult to know in what sense the Thessalonian Christians were “first”]</sup>, in order to be saved through being set apart by the Holy Spirit and through belief of the truth <sup>[of the Gospel]</sup>.
He called you <sup>[to this salvation]</sup> through our Gospel message, in order that you could obtain the splendor of our Lord Jesus Christ <sup>[in heaven]</sup>.
So then, brothers, stand firm <sup>[in the faith]</sup>, and hold onto the <sup>[inspired]</sup> traditions which you were taught, whether by word <sup>[of mouth]</sup> or by a letter from us.
Now, may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself and God our Father, who has loved us and given us never ending encouragement and good hope through <sup>[His]</sup> unearned favor,
encourage and strengthen your hearts in every good thing you do and say.
Finally, brothers, pray for us, so that the Lord’s message will spread quickly and be honored <sup>[by people]</sup>, even as it was among you.
And <sup>[pray]</sup> that we will be rescued from wicked and evil people, because not everyone has faith.
But the Lord is faithful, and He will strengthen and protect you from the evil one <sup>[Note: The Greek says just “the evil”]</sup>.
And we have confidence in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord concerning you, that you are doing, and will continue to do the things we command you.
And may the Lord direct your hearts to love <sup>[people]</sup> as God does, and to be steadfast as Christ was.

Now we command you, brothers, in the name <sup>[i.e., by the authority]</sup> of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you people stay away from every brother <sup>[or sister]</sup> who lives an idle life <sup>[See note on I Thess. 5:14]</sup>, and not according to the <sup>[inspired]</sup> tradition which you received from us.
For you, yourselves, know how you ought to follow our example, for we did not live in idleness when we were among you;
neither did we eat anyone’s food without paying for it. But by hard labor and toil, we worked night and day so as not to be a burden to any of you.
<sup>[We did this]</sup>, not because we did not have a right <sup>[to support]</sup>, but so we could become an example for you to imitate.
For even when we were with you, we gave you this order: If any person does not <sup>[want to]</sup> work, he should not <sup>[get to]</sup> eat.
For we hear that some of you are living in idleness, refusing to work, but <sup>[instead]</sup> are just being busybodies.
Now we command and urge such people, in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord Jesus Christ, that they work quietly and earn their own living.
But you, brothers, should not get tired of doing what is right.

And if any person does not obey our instructions, <sup>[given]</sup> in this letter, you should take note of him so that you do not associate with him, in hope that he will become ashamed <sup>[of his conduct]</sup>.
And yet do not consider him to be an enemy, but warn him as <sup>[you would]</sup> a <sup>[Christian]</sup> brother.
Now may the Lord Himself, <sup>[who is the source]</sup> of peace, give you peace at all times and in every circumstance. May the Lord be with all of you.
This greeting <sup>[is being written]</sup> by me, Paul, in my own handwriting; it is the signature I write in every letter.
May the unearned favor of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus, by the command of God our Savior and Christ Jesus our hope <sup>[of never ending life. See Titus 3:7]</sup>.

<sup>[It is being sent]</sup> to Timothy, my true son in the faith <sup>[i.e., in the realm of Christianity, not in a physical sense]</sup>. May you have unearned favor, mercy and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

As I urged you to do when I was leaving Macedonia <sup>[See Acts 20:1ff]</sup>, I want you to remain in Ephesus in order to urge certain people not to teach a different <sup>[i.e., false]</sup> doctrine,
and not to listen to legends or exhaustive lists of ancestors. These <sup>[only]</sup> serve to arouse speculations, rather than <sup>[promote]</sup> God’s plan which is based on faith.
But the goal of this instruction is <sup>[to]</sup> love <sup>[God and people]</sup> from a pure heart, a good conscience and a sincere faith.
Some people have strayed away from these things and have turned aside to useless discussions.
They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses, even though they do not understand what they are saying or what they are affirming with <sup>[such]</sup> confidence.
But we know that the Law of Moses is good if a person uses it properly.

We <sup>[also]</sup> know this: law <sup>[or, “the Law of Moses”]</sup> is not intended for a righteous person, but for lawbreakers and rebels, for ungodly people and sinners, for unholy and irreverent people, for people who kill their parents, for murderers,
for sexually immoral people, for homosexuals, for kidnappers, for liars, and for people who give false testimony. And <sup>[law is for]</sup> everything else that is against the sound teaching
found in the glorious Gospel of the blessed God, which was entrusted to me.
I thank Christ Jesus our Lord who has strengthened me, appointing me to the ministry <sup>[i.e., to His service]</sup>, because He considered me trustworthy,

<sup>[even]</sup> though I had previously spoken against Christ, and persecuted <sup>[Christians]</sup> and acted outrageously. However, I obtained mercy because I acted out of ignorance, not believing <sup>[in Christ]</sup>.
<sup>[But]</sup> the unearned favor of our Lord was given to me in great abundance, producing faith and love which are <sup>[found]</sup> in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> Christ Jesus.
This saying is trustworthy and deserves to be fully accepted: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, and I am the worst of them all.

However, even though I was the worst, I obtained mercy so that the patience of Jesus Christ could be shown toward the worst <sup>[of sinners]</sup> as an example for those people who would later believe in Him in order to <sup>[receive]</sup> never ending life.
Now, may there be honor and praise forever and ever to the eternal King, the immortal, invisible, only God. May it be so.

I give you this instruction, my son Timothy, in keeping with the previously spoken prophecies <sup>[i.e., predictions]</sup> about you, so that by <sup>[following]</sup> them you will fight the good battle,
holding on to <sup>[your]</sup> faith and a good conscience. Some people have rejected these things, resulting in the “shipwreck” of their faith <sup>[i.e., their spiritual life was ruined]</sup>.

Among these people are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I delivered over to Satan <sup>[Note: This probably refers to withdrawing fellowship from them. See I Cor. 5:1-5]</sup>, so that they would learn not to speak against God <sup>[anymore]</sup>.

First of all, then, I urge you to offer <sup>[special]</sup> requests, prayers, intercessions <sup>[Note: “Intercessions” probably refers to praying on behalf of others]</sup>, and thanksgivings for all people.

<sup>[Offer them]</sup> for kings and for all people in prominent <sup>[governmental]</sup> positions, so that we can live a peaceful and quiet life in all reverence <sup>[toward God]</sup> and respectability <sup>[toward people]</sup>.
This is good and acceptable <sup>[behavior]</sup> in the sight of God our Savior,
who wants all people to be saved and come to a <sup>[full]</sup> knowledge of the truth.
For there is one God and one Mediator between God and mankind --- the man Christ Jesus,

who gave Himself as a ransom on behalf of all people <sup>[i.e., as a substitute in place of others]</sup>. The proper time for the testimony <sup>[of this event to be proclaimed]</sup> has arrived. <sup>[Note: The correct meaning of the foregoing sentence is very difficult to determine]</sup>.
I was appointed to be a preacher <sup>[of this testimony]</sup> and an apostle and teacher of the true faith to the Gentiles. (I am telling the truth and not lying).

Therefore, I want the men to pray in every place <sup>[i.e., in all worship assemblies. See verses 1-2]</sup>, lifting up holy hands without being angry or argumentative. <sup>[Note: This passage stresses both the physical and spiritual aspects of prayer]</sup>.

I also want the women to dress themselves in proper clothing, with modesty and discretion, not with <sup>[elaborately]</sup> braided hair, or gold <sup>[ornaments]</sup>, or pearls, or expensive clothing,
but with good deeds, which are <sup>[more]</sup> appropriate for women who claim to be godly.
A woman should learn <sup>[the Scriptures]</sup> in quietness, being fully submissive.
But I do not allow a woman to teach or to exercise authority over a man <sup>[in the assembly]</sup>, but she should remain quiet.
For Adam was created first, and then Eve was. <sup>[See Gen. 1:27; 2:21-22]</sup>.
And Adam was not <sup>[the one]</sup> deceived, but <sup>[it was]</sup> the woman who, when deceived, fell into sin.

But she <sup>[i.e., womankind]</sup> will be saved <sup>[from condemnation, in spite of the “curse” of suffering pain]</sup> through childbearing <sup>[See Gen. 3:16]</sup>, provided they <sup>[i.e., all women]</sup> continue to have faith and love and holiness, along with discretion.
This is a trustworthy saying: If any man eagerly desires to be an overseer <sup>[i.e., an elder]</sup>, he desires a noble task.

Now the overseer must be above reproach, the husband of <sup>[only]</sup> one wife, sober-minded <sup>[Note: When this word, used here figuratively, is used in its literal sense, it means to abstain from intoxicating drinks]</sup>, sensible, respectable, hospitable, capable of teaching.
<sup>[He must]</sup> not be a drunkard or a fighter, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not wanting to get money by questionable <sup>[or, dishonest]</sup> means.
<sup>[He must]</sup> manage his own family well and, with dignity, keep his children in subjection <sup>[or, “respectful”]</sup>.
For if a man does not know how to manage his own family, how will he be able to take care of the church of God?

<sup>[He must]</sup> not be a new convert or else he could become conceited <sup>[i.e., over being in a leadership position]</sup> and fall into the <sup>[same]</sup> condemnation incurred by the devil.

He must also have a good reputation among outsiders <sup>[i.e., non-Christians]</sup>, or else he could fall into reproach <sup>[i.e., from worldly people]</sup> and <sup>[therefore]</sup> into the devil’s trap.

Similarly, deacons should be serious-minded <sup>[i.e., dignified]</sup>, not saying one thing while meaning another, not drinking a lot of wine <sup>[Note: The wine of Paul’s day was used as a common drink, and was either non-intoxicating or far less intoxicating than wine today]</sup>, not wanting to get money by questionable <sup>[or, dishonest]</sup> means.
They should keep holding on to the deep truths of the faith with a clear conscience.

And these <sup>[prospective deacons]</sup> should also first be tested, <sup>[and]</sup> then they should <sup>[be appointed to]</sup> serve <sup>[as deacons]</sup> if they are found to be above reproach.

Women <sup>[i.e., probably the deacons’ wives]</sup>, in the same way, should be serious-minded <sup>[i.e., dignified]</sup>, not gossips, <sup>[but]</sup> sober-minded <sup>[see verse 2]</sup> and trustworthy in everything.
Deacons should be husbands of one wife, managing their children and their own family affairs well.

For those who have served well <sup>[as deacons]</sup> acquire for themselves a good standing <sup>[i.e., they are respected in the church]</sup>, and the ability to speak boldly <sup>[or, to act confidently]</sup> in the faith that is in Christ Jesus <sup>[i.e., in the Christian faith]</sup>.
Although I hope to visit you soon, I am writing these things <sup>[in this letter]</sup>
so that, if I am delayed, you will know how people ought to behave themselves in God’s family, which is the church of the living God, the <sup>[supporting]</sup> column and foundation of the truth.

And without question the revealed secret of godly living is great. <sup>[It is this]</sup>: He <sup>[i.e., Christ, or God]</sup>, who appeared in a fleshly body <sup>[was]</sup> vindicated <sup>[of false charges]</sup> by the Holy Spirit; <sup>[He was]</sup> seen by angels, was preached among the nations, was believed in throughout the world <sup>[and finally]</sup> was taken up into glory <sup>[i.e., heaven]</sup>.

Now the Holy Spirit specifically says that in later times <sup>[Note: “Later times” in the New Testament usually refers to the period following the first coming of Christ and the establishment of the church]</sup>, some people will fall away from the faith <sup>[i.e., the true teaching of Christianity]</sup>. They will pay attention to deceitful spirits <sup>[i.e., lying people]</sup> and the teaching of evil spirits.
<sup>[Such teaching will be spread]</sup> by hypocritical liars, whose consciences have been seared over as with a hot iron <sup>[i.e., making them insensitive to their guilt]</sup>.

They forbid people to get married and require them to avoid <sup>[eating]</sup> certain foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and <sup>[fully]</sup> know the truth.
For everything created by God is permitted <sup>[to be eaten]</sup> and nothing should be refused, if it is received with gratitude,
for it is consecrated <sup>[i.e., made acceptable]</sup> by the word of God and prayer.
If you point out these things to the brothers you will be a good minister of Christ Jesus, and will be nurtured by the message of the faith and of the good teaching which you have been following.
But you should have nothing to do with those worldly myths <sup>[See 1:4]</sup> and superstitious tales <sup>[often]</sup> told by old women. Instead, train yourself for living a godly life.

For physical training is useful to some extent <sup>[or, “for a short time”]</sup>, but living godly is useful in every way, for it promises benefits <sup>[not only]</sup> for the present life, but <sup>[also]</sup> for the life to come.
This <sup>[foregoing]</sup> saying is trustworthy and deserves to be fully accepted.
For that reason we work hard and struggle, because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, <sup>[and]</sup> especially of those who believe <sup>[in Christ]</sup>.
Command and teach these things <sup>[i.e., the foregoing teaching. See verse 6-7]</sup>.

Do not allow anyone to look down on you because you are <sup>[so]</sup> young, but you should be an example to those who believe <sup>[in Christ]</sup> in your speech, in your conduct, in your love, in your faith <sup>[and]</sup> in your purity.
Until I come <sup>[to you]</sup>, pay attention to the <sup>[public]</sup> reading <sup>[of Scripture]</sup>, to exhorting people and to teaching.

Do not neglect <sup>[using]</sup> the gift <sup>[i.e., of serving as an evangelist. See II Tim. 4:5]</sup>, which was given to you in response to a prophetic message <sup>[or, prediction]</sup>, along with the laying on of the elders’ hands <sup>[i.e., signifying your appointment]</sup>.
Be attentive to these things <sup>[i.e., all of the foregoing]</sup>; be involved in <sup>[practicing]</sup> them, so that your progress <sup>[in the ministry]</sup> will be evident to everyone.
Pay close attention to yourself and to what you teach <sup>[people]</sup>. Continue doing these things, for in doing them you will both save yourself and those who listen to you.
Do not rebuke an older man, but appeal to him as <sup>[you would to]</sup> your father. <sup>[Appeal to]</sup> the younger men as <sup>[you would to]</sup> your brothers;
<sup>[to]</sup> older women as <sup>[you would to]</sup> mothers; <sup>[to]</sup> the younger women as <sup>[you would to]</sup> your sisters, in a <sup>[morally]</sup> pure way.
Show consideration for true widows <sup>[i.e., those destitute of family members to care for them]</sup>.

But if any widow has children or grandchildren, these should first learn <sup>[how]</sup> to show respectful consideration for their own family members, and <sup>[thus]</sup> to repay their parents <sup>[i.e., for bringing them up and caring for their material needs]</sup>, for this is pleasing in the sight of God.
Now the woman who is a true widow, and living all alone, has placed her hope in God and continues <sup>[to offer]</sup> special requests and prayers <sup>[for help (?)]</sup> night and day.
But the widow who devotes herself to unrestrained pleasure is <sup>[spiritually]</sup> dead, even while still living.
Command these things also, so that they <sup>[i.e., the widows and their families]</sup> can live above reproach.

But if anyone does not provide for <sup>[the needs of]</sup> his own <sup>[relatives]</sup>, and especially for his own <sup>[immediate]</sup> family, he has denied the faith <sup>[i.e., the fundamental beliefs of Christianity]</sup>, and is worse than an unbeliever.

<sup>[But]</sup> do not put a widow on the list <sup>[i.e., of people being helped by the church. See Acts 6:1]</sup> who is under sixty years old. <sup>[And she must]</sup> have been the wife of one man,

having a reputation for doing good deeds, <sup>[such as]</sup> bringing up children, showing hospitality to strangers, washing the feet of the saints <sup>[i.e., God’s holy people]</sup>, assisting people in trouble and devoting herself to doing all <sup>[other]</sup> kinds of good deeds.

But refuse <sup>[to put]</sup> younger widows <sup>[on the list. See verse 9]</sup>, because when they become restless for sensual pleasure, contrary to <sup>[their commitment to]</sup> Christ, they want to get married <sup>[again]</sup>.

Thus, they bring the judgment <sup>[for being untrustworthy]</sup> on themselves because they have broken their previous pledge. <sup>[Note: This probably refers to younger widows who pledged themselves to fulfill some important church activity and then quit suddenly to get married. But some suggest it refers to them leaving the faith and marrying a non-Christian]</sup>.
And on top of that, they learn to be idle, going around from house to house; and not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies, talking about things they should not be.

Therefore, I want the younger widows to get married, have children and manage their homes, so that they do not give the enemy <sup>[i.e., non-Christians]</sup> an opportunity for speaking against <sup>[the church]</sup>.
For some <sup>[young widows]</sup> have already turned away <sup>[from their commitment to God]</sup> to serve Satan.

<sup>[So]</sup>, if any woman believer has widows <sup>[in her family]</sup>, she should take care of them and not require the church to be burdened, so that it can take care of the true widows <sup>[i.e., destitute ones]</sup>.

The elders who lead <sup>[the church]</sup> well should be considered deserving of double honor <sup>[Note: This probably refers to financial support in addition to the honor of the office]</sup>, especially those who work hard at <sup>[preaching]</sup> the word and at teaching it.
For the Scripture says <sup>[Deut. 25:4]</sup>, “You should not put a muzzle on the ox while it tramples over the grain.” And <sup>[Luke 10:7]</sup>, “The worker deserves his wages.”
Do not listen to an accusation against an elder unless it comes from two or three witnesses.
You should rebuke in front of everyone <sup>[in the church]</sup> those elders who continue to sin, so that the rest <sup>[of the congregation]</sup> will be afraid <sup>[of sinning]</sup>.

I solemnly urge you, in the presence of God, and Christ Jesus and the elect <sup>[i.e., chosen]</sup> angels, that you obey these instructions without showing prejudice <sup>[toward anyone]</sup>, or doing anything out of partiality.

Do not be in a hurry to lay hands on anyone <sup>[i.e., to appoint them to office. See 4:14]</sup>, and do not participate in the sins of other people. <sup>[Note: If Timothy failed to carefully determine the qualifications of a prospective elder before appointing him, it would make him partially responsible for any wrongdoing by that elder]</sup>. Keep yourself pure <sup>[i.e., of involvement in anyone’s sins]</sup>.
Do not continue drinking water only, but use a little wine <sup>[i.e., for its medicinal value]</sup> for your stomach and for your frequent <sup>[other]</sup> ailments.
The sins of some people are evident, preceding them to <sup>[the day of]</sup> judgment, but the sins of other people follow them there.
In the same way, the good deeds <sup>[of people]</sup> are <sup>[also]</sup> evident, and even those <sup>[deeds]</sup> which are not <sup>[good]</sup> cannot be hidden.

All those people who are under the reins of slavery <sup>[i.e., in subjection to someone]</sup> should consider their own masters as deserving of full respect, so that God’s name and <sup>[His]</sup> teaching will not be spoken against <sup>[i.e., by unbelievers]</sup>.

And those slaves who have believing masters should not despise them, since they are <sup>[Christian]</sup> brothers, but rather they should serve them all the more <sup>[faithfully]</sup>. For those <sup>[masters]</sup> who benefit from their service are believers and dearly loved <sup>[by them and God]</sup>. Teach and exhort people regarding these things.

If anyone teaches a different doctrine <sup>[i.e., from what has been urged above]</sup>, and does not agree to <sup>[proclaim the]</sup> sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and to teaching that is godly <sup>[i.e., in harmony with godliness]</sup>,
that person is conceited. He does not understand anything, but has an unhealthy concern for controversy and arguments over words, which produce envy, dissension, abusive language, evil suspicions,

<sup>[and]</sup> continual wrangling between people with corrupt minds, who are deprived of the truth. They think that <sup>[professing]</sup> godliness is a way to obtain <sup>[financial]</sup> gain.
But godliness <sup>[itself]</sup> is great gain, <sup>[when it is]</sup> accompanied by contentment.
For we did not bring anything into the world, and we cannot take anything out of it either.
But if we have <sup>[enough]</sup> food and clothing, we will <sup>[i.e., should]</sup> be content with that.
But those people who want to get rich fall into temptation and a trap and into many foolish and harmful desires, which plunge people into ruin and <sup>[spiritual]</sup> destruction.

For, loving money is a source of all kinds of evil, <sup>[and]</sup> some people who crave it have been led astray from the faith, and have stabbed themselves <sup>[in the heart]</sup> with many sorrows.
But you, man of God, run from these things <sup>[See verses 3-10]</sup>, and pursue right living and godliness, having faith, love, perseverance and gentleness.

Fight the good battle of the faith; take hold of never ending life, to which you were called <sup>[i.e., by the Gospel. See II Thess. 2:14]</sup>, <sup>[when]</sup> you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses. <sup>[Note: This “confession” probably refers to a person’s acknowledgment of Jesus as Lord and Christ prior to his immersion. See Acts 2:36; Rom. 10:9-10]</sup>.

I <sup>[solemnly]</sup> urge you in the presence of God, who gives life to everything, and of Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when He testified in front of Pontius Pilate <sup>[See John 18:37; Matt. 27:17]</sup>,

to obey this command without <sup>[moral]</sup> blemish or blame. <sup>[Note: This “command” probably refers to Timothy’s responsibility to fulfill his ministry. See verses 11-12; II Tim. 4:1-2]</sup>. <sup>[Do this]</sup> until our Lord Jesus Christ returns,
which God will bring about in its <sup>[or, “His”]</sup> own time. <sup>[For]</sup> He is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings, and the Lord of lords.

He alone possesses immortality <sup>[Note: That is, only God possesses an eternal existence, with neither beginning nor ending. See John 5:26]</sup>, and lives in unapproachable light. No one has seen Him, or can see Him. May there be honor and never ending power to Him. May it be so.

<sup>[Solemnly]</sup> urge those who are rich in this present world not to be arrogant, nor to place their hope in the uncertainty of <sup>[their]</sup> wealth, but in God, who generously supplies us with everything to enjoy.
<sup>[Urge them]</sup> to do what is good, to be rich in <sup>[the practice of]</sup> good deeds, to be generous and willing to share <sup>[with others]</sup>.

<sup>[In that way]</sup> they will store up for themselves a treasure <sup>[of never ending rewards]</sup> for providing a solid foundation for the future, so that they can take hold of the true life <sup>[i.e., in heaven]</sup>.
<sup>[So]</sup>, Timothy, guard <sup>[the Gospel message]</sup> which has been entrusted to you, while avoiding worldly, empty chatter and the opposing ideas of what is falsely called “knowledge,”
which some people have claimed <sup>[to have]</sup>, and in doing so have strayed from the faith. May <sup>[God’s]</sup> unearned favor be upon you.

<sup>[This letter is from]</sup> Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, according to <sup>[the ministry of proclaiming]</sup> the promise of never ending life, which is <sup>[obtained]</sup> through <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus.
<sup>[It is being sent]</sup> to Timothy, my dearly loved son. May you have unearned favor, mercy and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

I thank God, whom I worship <sup>[or, “serve”]</sup> with a clear conscience, as my forefathers did, that I can remember you <sup>[i.e., your commitment]</sup> continually in my prayers night and day.
Remembering how you cried <sup>[Note: This was probably when Paul and Timothy last parted]</sup>, I long to see you <sup>[again]</sup>, so that I can be filled with joy.
I <sup>[also]</sup> remember the sincere faith you have in your heart, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice and, I am convinced, lives in you <sup>[too]</sup>.

The reason I am reminding you <sup>[of these things]</sup> is so that you will rekindle the gift from God, which is in your life through the laying on my hands. <sup>[Note: This “gift” was probably his ordination to serve as an evangelist (See 4:1-5), and in addition, may have included a supernatural “spiritual gift” bestowed by the apostle Paul]</sup>.
For God did not give us a spirit <sup>[i.e., a disposition]</sup> that makes us cowards but one that gives us power and love and self-control.

So, do not be ashamed to testify for our Lord, or ashamed of <sup>[being associated with]</sup> me, His prisoner, but <sup>[be willing to]</sup> suffer hardship with me for the sake of the Gospel, relying on God’s power <sup>[to sustain you]</sup>.

<sup>[It is]</sup> God who saved us and called us to live holy lives <sup>[or, “to be dedicated people”]</sup>, not as a result of our <sup>[good]</sup> deeds, but in harmony with His own purpose and unearned favor. <sup>[This was]</sup> given to us through <sup>[the atoning work of]</sup> Christ Jesus, before the beginning of time. <sup>[Note: Salvation through Christ was planned before time began. See Titus 1:2; Rom. 16:25; Eph. 1:4]</sup>.

But, it has <sup>[only]</sup> now been made known to us by the appearance of our Savior Christ Jesus, who has put an end to death and brought <sup>[never ending]</sup> life and incorruption <sup>[i.e., immortality]</sup> to light through the Gospel <sup>[message]</sup>,
of which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher.

For I am suffering these things <sup>[See verse 8]</sup> for this reason <sup>[i.e., because of being a Gospel preacher]</sup>. Yet I am not ashamed because I know whom I have believed in <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup>, and I am convinced that He is able to guard what I have entrusted to Him <sup>[i.e., Paul’s spiritual well-being]</sup> until that day <sup>[i.e., the Day of Judgment]</sup>.
You should hold on firmly to the pattern of sound teaching that you have heard from me, while <sup>[maintaining your]</sup> faith and love in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> Christ Jesus.
<sup>[Also]</sup> guard that precious treasure <sup>[i.e., the Gospel message]</sup> which was entrusted to you by the Holy Spirit, who lives in us.
You know that all those people in <sup>[the province of]</sup> Asia deserted me <sup>[i.e., those who could have helped me]</sup>, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
<sup>[But]</sup>, may the Lord grant mercy to Onesiphorus’ family, for he cheered me up many times and was not ashamed of my <sup>[being in]</sup> chains.
But, when he was in Rome, he looked for me diligently until he found me.
May the Lord grant that he will find mercy on that day <sup>[i.e., the Day of Judgment]</sup>. And you know very well the many things he did to serve <sup>[me while I was]</sup> at Ephesus.
So, may you, my son, be strengthened through the unearned favor that comes from <sup>[your fellowship with]</sup> Christ Jesus.
And the things that you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses, you should pass on to trustworthy men who will be able to teach other people also.
<sup>[Be willing to]</sup> suffer hardship with me like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
No soldier on active duty, gets himself involved in the affairs of civilian life, so that he can please the person who enlisted him <sup>[i.e., his commanding officer]</sup>.
And if a person competes as an athlete, he is not awarded the victor’s crown unless he competes according to the rules.
<sup>[And]</sup> the farmer who works hard should be the first one to receive a share of the crops.
Consider what I am saying, for the Lord will help you to understand everything.
<sup>[In the face of trouble]</sup> remember Jesus Christ, a descendant of King David, who was raised from the dead according to my Gospel,
for which I am suffering difficulty <sup>[here]</sup> in prison, as <sup>[though I were]</sup> a criminal. But God’s message is not “in prison.”
So, I endure everything for the elect’s sake <sup>[i.e., God’s people]</sup>, so that they can obtain the salvation which comes through Christ Jesus, with <sup>[its]</sup> never ending splendor.
This saying is trustworthy: For if we have died with Christ <sup>[i.e., stopped the practice of sin, See Rom. 6:1-11]</sup>, we will also live with Him <sup>[i.e., both here and hereafter]</sup>.

If we endure <sup>[i.e., remain faithful through suffering]</sup>, we will also rule with Him <sup>[i.e., both here and hereafter]</sup>. If we deny Him, He will also deny us. <sup>[See Matt. 10:33]</sup>.

If we prove to be unfaithful <sup>[i.e., to our commitment to Christ]</sup>, He <sup>[still]</sup> remains faithful <sup>[to His word]</sup>, for He cannot deny Himself. <sup>[Note: This verse refers to Christ remaining true to His promise of either accepting or rejecting us. See Matt. 10:32-33]</sup>.

Remind them <sup>[Note: This probably refers to the “trustworthy men” there at Ephesus. See 2:2]</sup> of these things <sup>[i.e., the truths just mentioned. Verses 8-13]</sup>, solemnly urging them, in the presence of the Lord, not to argue over words, <sup>[for]</sup> it is useless and brings <sup>[only]</sup> ruin to those who listen to it.

Do your best to present yourself as a worker who is approved by God <sup>[and]</sup> does not have to be ashamed, <sup>[because you are]</sup> accurately handling <sup>[i.e., explaining]</sup> the message of truth.
But avoid worldly, empty chatter, for those who practice it will become more and more ungodly,
and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among these people are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
who have strayed from the truth <sup>[by]</sup> saying that the resurrection <sup>[from the dead]</sup> has already happened and are undermining the faith of some people.

However, the solid foundation of God stands firm. <sup>[Note: This probably refers to the church. See I Tim. 3:15]</sup>. It is sealed with these words <sup>[i.e., certifying God’s ownership and a commitment to Him]</sup>: “The Lord knows those who are His people,” <sup>[See Num. 16:5]</sup>, and “Everyone who confesses the name of the Lord should stop practicing wickedness.” <sup>[See Isa. 26:13]</sup>.

Now in a large house there are utensils made, not only of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay. And some are for honorable uses <sup>[i.e., for special occasions]</sup> and some are for common, ordinary use.

If a person rids himself of the common ones <sup>[i.e., the influences of evil people and qualities]</sup>, he will be an instrument for <sup>[God’s]</sup> honor, dedicated, suitable for use by the Master, <sup>[and]</sup> prepared for <sup>[doing]</sup> every good deed.

But run from the evil desires of youth and pursue <sup>[the practice of]</sup> right living, faith, love and peace, along with those people who call on the Lord <sup>[i.e., in prayer]</sup> from a pure heart.
But refuse <sup>[to participate in]</sup> foolish and stupid arguments, knowing that they produce quarreling.
For the Lord’s slave must not quarrel, but be kind toward everyone, capable of teaching, <sup>[and]</sup> patient <sup>[with difficult people and situations]</sup>.

<sup>[He must]</sup> gently correct those people who oppose <sup>[him]</sup>, in hope that God will give them <sup>[opportunity for]</sup> repentance, leading to a <sup>[full]</sup> knowledge of the truth,
and that they will come to their senses and escape from being trapped by the devil, who had captured them to do what he wanted.
But you should know this: There will be terrible times in the last days <sup>[of the Christian age]</sup>.
For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, abusive talkers, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
lacking in natural affection, unwilling to be reconciled <sup>[to their enemies]</sup>, slanderers, lacking self-control, brutal, haters of what is good,
treacherous <sup>[i.e., betrayers]</sup>, reckless, conceited, <sup>[and]</sup> lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God.
<sup>[These people]</sup> maintain an appearance of being godly, but they have denied its power; <sup>[you should]</sup> turn away from such people.
They are the kind of people who enter homes and influence <sup>[morally]</sup> weak-willed women, loaded down with sins, <sup>[and]</sup> led on by all kinds of evil desires.
They are constantly learning and <sup>[yet]</sup> never able to arrive at a <sup>[full]</sup> knowledge of the truth.
These people oppose the truth just like Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. They have corrupt minds and are worthless as far as the faith is concerned.
But they will not advance any farther <sup>[in their sin]</sup> because their foolish ways will become obvious to everyone, just as those of Jannes and Jambres were.
But you have followed <sup>[Note: These words mean “to observe closely, have a keen interest in”]</sup> my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, perseverance,

persecutions and sufferings. You know what kind of things happened to me at Antioch, Iconium and Lystra <sup>[See Acts 13:13-14:23]</sup>, and what <sup>[severe]</sup> persecutions I experienced <sup>[there]</sup>. But the Lord rescued me from all of them.
In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in <sup>[the service of]</sup> Christ will be persecuted.
But evil people and impostors will go from bad to worse <sup>[in their behavior]</sup>, deceiving people and being deceived by them.

But you should continue <sup>[to believe and practice]</sup> what you have learned and been convinced of, knowing those from whom you learned it. <sup>[Note: These persons would include Lois and Eunice (See I Tim. 1:5), as well as Paul himself]</sup>.
And <sup>[remember]</sup> that, from childhood, you have known the sacred Scriptures which are able to make you wise <sup>[enough]</sup> to be saved through faith in Christ Jesus.

All Scripture is inspired by God <sup>[Note: Literally, this word means “breathed out by God”]</sup> and is useful for teaching <sup>[truth]</sup>, rebuking <sup>[wrongdoing]</sup>, correcting <sup>[error]</sup> and for providing instruction <sup>[i.e., training]</sup> on how to live right,
so that the man of God will be thoroughly equipped for <sup>[doing]</sup> every good deed.

In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who will judge <sup>[both]</sup> those still alive and those who have already died, and in view of His appearing <sup>[i.e., at His second coming]</sup> and His kingdom <sup>[i.e., when it is revealed in its eternal splendor]</sup>, I solemnly urge you:
