For if anyone is <sup>[only]</sup> a listener to the word and does not practice it, he is like a person who sees his natural face in a mirror,
and <sup>[then after]</sup> he sees himself, <sup>[he]</sup> goes away and immediately forgets what he looks like.

But the person who looks closely into the perfect law that brings freedom, and continues to do so, is not a listener who forgets <sup>[what he hears]</sup>, but someone who does the work <sup>[it requires]</sup>. This person will be blessed for doing so.
If anyone considers himself to be religious and yet does not keep a tight rein on his tongue, this person is self-deceived and his religion is worthless.
Religion that is pure and uncorrupted in the eyes of <sup>[our]</sup> God and Father involves taking care of orphans and widows in their distress and keeping oneself unstained by the world.

My brothers, do not hold <sup>[views regarding]</sup> the faith of our glorious Lord Jesus Christ <sup>[i.e., as tenets of Christianity]</sup> which show favoritism toward <sup>[certain]</sup> persons.
For, suppose a person comes into your assembly wearing a gold ring and dressed in fine clothing and <sup>[then another]</sup> person comes in who is poor and dressed in shabby clothing.

Now if you people show special attention to the one wearing the fine clothing and say <sup>[to him]</sup>, “You can sit here in this good seat,” and to the poor person, “You stand over there,” or “Sit on the floor by my feet,”
are you not making class distinctions among yourselves and judging <sup>[people]</sup> from evil motives?
Listen, my dearly loved brothers; did not God choose those who are poor in the eyes of the world to be wealthy in faith and to inherit the kingdom which He promised to those who love Him?
But you people have dishonored the poor person. Are not rich people the ones who oppress you and drag you to court?
Do they not speak against the honorable name by which you are called? <sup>[i.e., the name “Christ” or “Christian”]</sup>.
However, you are doing well if you fulfill <sup>[i.e., obey]</sup> the royal law found in the Scriptures <sup>[Lev. 19:18]</sup>, “You should love your neighbor the same way that you love yourself.”
But if you show favoritism toward <sup>[certain]</sup> persons, you are committing a sin and are convicted by the law <sup>[of God]</sup> as lawbreakers.
For, whoever observes the entire law <sup>[of God]</sup> and yet breaks one requirement <sup>[of it]</sup>, becomes guilty of <sup>[breaking]</sup> all of it.

For God, who said <sup>[through Moses, Ex. 20:13f]</sup>, “You must not be sexually unfaithful to your mate,” also said, “You must not murder.” Now if you are sexually faithful to your mate and yet murder <sup>[someone]</sup>, you have become a lawbreaker.
So, you should speak and act as people who will be judged by a law that brings freedom.
For the person who has shown no mercy will be judged without mercy. <sup>[For]</sup> mercy wins out over condemnation.
What good is it, my brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup>, if a person says he has faith but does not have <sup>[good]</sup> deeds? Can such faith save him?
If a brother or sister is without <sup>[adequate]</sup> clothing and needs daily food,
and one of you says to him, “Go <sup>[and be]</sup> at peace; keep warm and eat well,” and yet you do not give him anything for his physical needs, what good is that?
In the same way, if <sup>[your]</sup> faith does not have <sup>[good]</sup> deeds <sup>[to accompany it]</sup>, it is dead, <sup>[because it stands]</sup> alone.

But someone may say, “You have faith and I have <sup>[good]</sup> deeds.” <sup>[I reply]</sup>, show me your faith without your <sup>[good]</sup> deeds, and I will show you my faith by my <sup>[good]</sup> deeds.
You believe that there is <sup>[only]</sup> one God; that is good! Even the demons believe <sup>[that]</sup>, and they shudder <sup>[with fear at the thought]</sup>.
But will you acknowledge, you foolish person, that faith without <sup>[good]</sup> deeds is useless?
Was not our forefather Abraham made right with God by deeds <sup>[of obedience]</sup> when he offered his son Isaac <sup>[as a sacrifice]</sup> on the Altar?
<sup>[Do you not]</sup> see that faith cooperated together with his deeds, and faith was made complete by <sup>[his]</sup> deeds?
So, the Scripture was fulfilled, which says <sup>[Gen. 15:6]</sup>, “Abraham believed God and <sup>[so]</sup> God considered him righteous,” and he was called God’s friend.
<sup>[So]</sup>, you see, it is by deeds that a person is made right with God, and not by faith alone.

And in the same way, was not the prostitute Rahab made right with God by <sup>[her]</sup> deeds when she welcomed <sup>[and protected]</sup> the messengers, and <sup>[then]</sup> sent them away by a different road?
For as the body without the spirit is dead, so also faith without <sup>[good]</sup> deeds is dead.
Not many of you should be teachers, my brothers, because you <sup>[should]</sup> know that we <sup>[teachers]</sup> will be judged more strictly.
For we all make mistakes in many areas <sup>[of life]</sup>. If anyone does not make a mistake in what he says, he is a perfect person, capable of keeping a tight rein on his whole life also.
Now if we put bits in the mouths of horses so that they will obey us, we can govern the direction of their entire bodies as well.
Look, even though ships are huge and are driven by strong winds, still their direction can be governed by a very small rudder, wherever the pilot desires to sail.
So, the tongue is a small part <sup>[of the body]</sup>, yet it boasts of great accomplishments. Look at how large a forest is burned up by even a tiny spark.

And the tongue is <sup>[like]</sup> a fire <sup>[i.e., like that spark]</sup>. <sup>[For]</sup> the tongue, as a part of the body, is <sup>[like]</sup> the world of evil. It corrupts the entire life <sup>[of a person]</sup> and inflames the course of nature itself <sup>[i.e., it affects a person throughout his whole earthly existence]</sup>, and is ignited by hell itself <sup>[i.e., the evil produced by the fiery tongue has hell as its source and destiny]</sup>.
For all kinds of animals and birds and reptiles and ocean creatures are being subdued and have been subdued by mankind,
but no one can subdue the tongue. It is an uncontrollable <sup>[source of]</sup> evil and full of deadly poison.
We praise the Lord and Father with it, and <sup>[also]</sup> curse men who are created in God’s likeness with it.
Praise and cursing come out of the same mouth! My brothers, these things should not be happening.
Does a spring produce <sup>[both]</sup> fresh water and salt water from the same opening?
My brothers, can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine <sup>[produce]</sup> figs? Neither can a salt water <sup>[spring]</sup> produce fresh water.
Who <sup>[then]</sup> is the wise and understanding person among you? He should show by his good conduct <sup>[that he has]</sup> deeds of gentleness <sup>[stemming]</sup> from wisdom.
But if you people have bitter jealousy and rivalry in your hearts, do not be arrogant <sup>[over it]</sup> and deny the truth.
This “wisdom” is not the kind that comes from <sup>[God]</sup> above, but is earthly, unspiritual and demonic <sup>[i.e., it is motivated by an evil spirit and not the Holy Spirit]</sup>.
For when jealousy and rivalry exist, there is <sup>[also]</sup> disorder and every <sup>[kind of]</sup> evil practice.

But the <sup>[true]</sup> wisdom, which is from <sup>[God]</sup> above, is first of all pure, then peace-loving, patient and easily persuaded. <sup>[It is]</sup> full of mercy and produces a crop of good deeds; <sup>[it is]</sup> impartial <sup>[or, unwavering]</sup> and sincere.
<sup>[People who are]</sup> peacemakers sow <sup>[seeds of]</sup> peace and it produces a crop of righteousness <sup>[in the lives of others]</sup>.
Where do conflicts and fights among you come from? Do they not come from your <sup>[desire for]</sup> pleasures which produce inner conflict?

You people desire something and yet do not have it, <sup>[so]</sup> you murder <sup>[to get it]</sup>. You are envious <sup>[of what someone has]</sup> and <sup>[yet]</sup> are not able to obtain it, <sup>[so]</sup> you fight and quarrel <sup>[over it]</sup>. You do not have <sup>[what you want]</sup> because you do not ask <sup>[God for it]</sup>.
<sup>[When]</sup> you do ask, you do not receive it, because you ask from wrong motives, <sup>[just]</sup> so you can satisfy your own pleasures.

You people who have become unfaithful to God, do you not know that being <sup>[intimate]</sup> friends with the world <sup>[of sin]</sup> means hostility toward God? Therefore, whoever determines to be an <sup>[intimate]</sup> friend of the world becomes an enemy of God.

Or, do you think that the Scripture says <sup>[this]</sup> for nothing? <sup>[Note: No specific passage is here cited, but the idea occurs in several Old Testament passages]</sup>. “The Holy Spirit, whom God has made to live in us, longs jealously <sup>[for us to be faithful to God]</sup>.”

But He gives <sup>[us]</sup> more unearned favor <sup>[i.e., to overcome the world]</sup>. Therefore, the Scripture says <sup>[Prov. 3:34]</sup>, “God opposes proud people, but gives unearned favor to those who are humble.”
So, you should submit yourselves to God, but oppose the devil, and he will flee from you.

Come close to God and He will come close to you. Wash your hands, you sinful people <sup>[i.e., this is an exhortation to repent of moral and spiritual corruption]</sup>, and purify your hearts, you double-minded people.
Be distressed and grieve and cry <sup>[over your sins]</sup>; your laughter should turn into grieving and your joy <sup>[should turn]</sup> into sorrow.
Humble yourselves in the presence of the Lord and He will exalt you people.

Do not slander one another, brothers, <sup>[for]</sup> the person who speaks against his brother, or judges him, is <sup>[guilty of]</sup> speaking against the law <sup>[of God]</sup>. But when you judge the law, you are not obeying it but acting as its judge.
There is only One Lawgiver and Judge <sup>[i.e., God or Christ]</sup>. It is He who is able to save and destroy <sup>[people]</sup>. So, who <sup>[do you think]</sup> you are, judging your neighbor?
Come on now, you people who are saying, “Today or tomorrow we will go to this <sup>[or that]</sup> city, spend a year there, do business and make some money.”
<sup>[For actually]</sup> you do not know what your life will be like tomorrow. What is your life? For you are <sup>[like]</sup> a mist that appears briefly and then vanishes.
Instead, you should be saying, “If it is what the Lord wants, we will live and do this or that.”
But, as it is, you people are boasting in your arrogance <sup>[and]</sup> all such boasting is evil.
So, the person who knows the good <sup>[he ought]</sup> to be doing, but is not doing it, is sinning.
Come on now, you rich people, cry and wail over the miseries that are coming upon you.
Your wealth has rotted and moths have eaten your clothing.

Your gold and silver have tarnished and their tarnish will be a testimony against you <sup>[i.e., you have hoarded your wealth instead of using it to benefit others]</sup>, and they will devour your flesh as fire <sup>[i.e., your riches will ultimately consume you]</sup>. You have hoarded up your riches in these last days.

Look, the wages you have fraudulently refused to pay the workers who mowed your fields are crying out <sup>[against you]</sup>. And the cries of these harvest hands have reached the ears of the Lord of <sup>[heavenly]</sup> armies <sup>[i.e., Almighty God]</sup>.
You people have basked in luxury <sup>[here]</sup> on earth and lived lives devoted to pleasure. You have fattened yourselves <sup>[as sacrificial oxen]</sup> for the day of slaughter.
You have condemned and murdered the righteous man <sup>[i.e., the innocent person, See verse 4]</sup> and he does not <sup>[even]</sup> resist you.
So, be patient, brothers, until the Lord comes. Look, the farmer waits for his land to produce the valuable crop and is patient with it until it receives the fall and spring rains.

<sup>[So]</sup>, you people should be patient also; strengthen your hearts because the Lord’s coming is near <sup>[Note: This may refer to the destruction of Jerusalem in AD 70, or to Christ’s final coming and judgment]</sup>.

Do not complain against one another, brothers, so that you will not <sup>[have to]</sup> be judged <sup>[and condemned for it]</sup>. Look, the Judge <sup>[i.e., Christ]</sup> is standing at the door! <sup>[See verse 8]</sup>.
Brothers, take the <sup>[Old Testament]</sup> prophets, who spoke in the name <sup>[i.e., by the authority]</sup> of the Lord, as your examples of suffering and patience.

Notice that we consider those who endured <sup>[suffering with patience]</sup> to be blessed <sup>[by God]</sup>. You people have heard of Job’s patience and have seen what the Lord finally did <sup>[for him]</sup>. The Lord is very compassionate and merciful.

But above everything else, my brothers, do not take oaths, not “by the heaven,” nor “by the earth,” nor by any other oath. But your “yes” should be <sup>[simply]</sup> “yes,” and your “no,” <sup>[simply]</sup> “no,” so you do not fall under condemnation.
Is there anyone among you who is suffering? <sup>[If so]</sup>, he should pray. Is anyone happy? <sup>[Then]</sup> he should sing a psalm.

Is anyone among you sick? <sup>[Then]</sup> he should call for the elders of the church and they should pray for him, after pouring <sup>[olive]</sup> oil on him in the name <sup>[i.e., by the authority]</sup> of the Lord.

And if their prayer is <sup>[offered]</sup> by faith, <sup>[God]</sup> will heal the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up <sup>[from his sickbed]</sup>. And if he had sinned, he will be forgiven for them <sup>[Note: This may imply that the man’s sickness was the result of some sin. In any event, his forgiveness would follow his repentance]</sup>.

So, confess your sins to one another, and pray for one another to be healed <sup>[from sin sickness]</sup>. The special request of a righteous person is empowered with <sup>[great]</sup> effectiveness.
Elijah was a man with a similar nature as ours, and he prayed earnestly that it would not rain <sup>[I Kings 17:1; 18:1]</sup>, and it did not rain on the land for three and a half years.
Then he prayed again and the sky poured rain and the ground produced its crop.
My brothers, if anyone among you strays away from the truth <sup>[of God’s inspired message]</sup>, and someone brings him back <sup>[to it]</sup>,

that person should know that whoever brings a sinner back from the error of his way will save that soul from <sup>[spiritual]</sup> death and will cover over a large number of sins <sup>[i.e., the many sins of that person will become forgiven]</sup>.

<sup>[This is being written by]</sup> Jesus’ apostle Peter, to God’s people who are scattered throughout the countries of Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia and Bithynia. <sup>[Note: These places are located in present-day Turkey]</sup>.

These people have been <sup>[specially]</sup> selected, and <sup>[their acceptance was]</sup> pre-planned by God the Father, and they were set apart through <sup>[the work of]</sup> the Holy Spirit. This resulted in their obedience and sprinkling <sup>[Note: This is a figurative reference to the Old Testament practice of cleansing, See Exodus 24:7-8]</sup> by the blood of Jesus Christ. May God’s unearned favor and peace be continually upon you.

God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, is to be praised for being so merciful as to have given us a new birth, which provides for us a hope of never ending life through the means of Jesus Christ’s return from the dead.
Through Him we have an inheritance that cannot be corrupted or contaminated, and will never disappear; it is reserved in heaven for you.
Through God’s power you are protected by <sup>[your]</sup> faith, so that you can expect to be saved in the end.
Because of this you can be very joyous, even though it is necessary for you to have suffered many trials for a little while.

But these will only test your faith, which is more valuable than gold which, though refined by fire, eventually perishes. Your faith, <sup>[however]</sup>, will <sup>[endure and eventually]</sup> prove to be praiseworthy and honorable when Jesus returns.
Although you have not seen Jesus, you <sup>[still]</sup> love Him and believe in Him, and this causes you to have an indescribable, glorious joy.
Your faith will <sup>[finally]</sup> bring about the salvation of your souls.
The prophets <sup>[of old]</sup>, who spoke of this salvation, asked about and searched diligently concerning the unearned favor <sup>[God provided]</sup> for you people.

They studied <sup>[their own prophecies]</sup> to learn what, or which <sup>[person or]</sup> time the Holy Spirit in them pointed to, in predicting the sufferings of Christ and the glories yet to come.

It was revealed to these prophets that you people, not they, were the ones being ministered to in the matters now being announced to you by those who preached the Gospel to you by the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven. <sup>[This message contained]</sup> things which angels would like to look into.
So, gather your thoughts together for action, take control of yourselves and focus your hope completely on God’s unearned favor, which will be brought to you by Jesus Christ when He returns.
As obedient children, do not pattern your lives after the evil desires you formerly had when you were ignorant,
but according to the Holy One, who called you <sup>[i.e., God]</sup>, you people also should become holy <sup>[i.e., separated for God’s service]</sup> in all your conduct,
because it is written <sup>[Lev. 11:44f]</sup>, “You shall be holy <sup>[i.e., separated from sin]</sup> even as I am holy.”

And if you call upon <sup>[God as your]</sup> Father, who judges each person’s work impartially, <sup>[then]</sup> conduct yourselves during the time you spend in this life showing reverence <sup>[to God]</sup>.
You <sup>[should]</sup> know that you were bought back <sup>[from Satan]</sup>, from your useless way of life, inherited from your forefathers, not with such perishable things as silver and gold,
but with <sup>[the]</sup> precious blood of Christ, who represents an unblemished lamb, without defect.
For Christ was previously known <sup>[by God, even]</sup> before the world was created, yet was not fully revealed for your benefit until <sup>[these]</sup> final times.
<sup>[It is]</sup> through Christ, whom God raised from the dead and honored, that you people have become believers in God, so that your faith and hope could rest in God.

<sup>[Since]</sup> you have purified your souls by <sup>[your]</sup> obedience to the truth <sup>[i.e., the Gospel message]</sup>, which produces a genuine love for your brothers, <sup>[now]</sup> you should have this kind of fervent, heartfelt love for one another.
You have experienced new birth, not from seed that can decay, but from seed that cannot decay, through the living and enduring word of God.

For all forms of flesh are similar to grass, and all the splendor of <sup>[a person’s]</sup> flesh is like the flower <sup>[which blossoms]</sup> from the grass. <sup>[But as with]</sup> grass, <sup>[a person’s flesh]</sup> withers, and its flower <sup>[i.e., his splendor]</sup> falls off.
But the word of the Lord continues to live on and on <sup>[forever]</sup>. And this word is the good news that was preached to you people.
Therefore, you should put out of your lives all malice <sup>[i.e., bad attitudes]</sup>, and all deceit and hypocrisy, and all envy and unwholesome talk.
<sup>[So]</sup>, like newly born babies, you should desire the genuine, spiritual milk <sup>[i.e., God’s word. See Heb. 5:12-13]</sup>, so that by it you may grow toward <sup>[final]</sup> salvation,
since you have experienced the goodness of the Lord <sup>[i.e., that the Lord is good]</sup>.
You should come to Him, who is a living stone, rejected by people, but chosen as precious in God’s sight.
And you yourselves are also like living stones, being constructed into a spiritual house. For you are like holy priests who offer spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.

For it says in the Scripture <sup>[Isa. 28:16]</sup>, “Look, I am placing a stone in <sup>[the city of]</sup> Zion <sup>[i.e., Jerusalem]</sup>. It is a specially selected, very valuable cornerstone. And the person who trusts in the Him <sup>[i.e., the One represented by this stone]</sup> will never have to suffer shame <sup>[i.e., because of unforgiven sin being brought up against him]</sup>.”

To those of you who believe in Him then, this “Stone” is <sup>[very]</sup> precious. But to those who do not believe <sup>[Psa. 118:22 says]</sup>, “The stone which those in charge of the building project have rejected has been made the principal stone for aligning the whole structure.”

<sup>[And it also says, Isa. 8:14, that He would be]</sup>, “A stone that people would stumble over and a rock over which they would trip and fall.” The way they stumble is by disobeying the Gospel message. They were destined <sup>[though not forced]</sup> to do this.

But you people are especially chosen <sup>[by God]</sup>; you are a royal body of priests; you are a nation especially set apart for God’s use; you are a people who belong exclusively to God, that you may express the virtues of the One who called you out of darkness <sup>[i.e., of sin and error]</sup>, and into His marvelous light <sup>[i.e., righteousness and truth]</sup>.

At one time you <sup>[Gentiles]</sup> were not a <sup>[chosen]</sup> people, but now you are God’s <sup>[special]</sup> people. Once you had not received <sup>[His]</sup> mercy, but now you have received it.
My dearly loved ones, I urge you, as a people who are foreigners and strangers <sup>[in this world]</sup>, do not give in to sinful desires, which wage war against the soul.

You should live such good lives before <sup>[unconverted]</sup> Gentiles, so that even though they accuse you of being evildoers, by seeing your good deeds, they will <sup>[be led to]</sup> honor God <sup>[for you]</sup> on the day He visits us again <sup>[i.e., to bring judgment]</sup>.
Submit yourselves to every authority set up by people for the Lord’s sake. <sup>[Submit to]</sup> a king as the <sup>[highest]</sup> authority,
or to governors who are appointed by him to punish wrongdoers, and to commend those who do right.
For it is God’s will that, by doing good, you should put to silence the ignorant <sup>[talk and actions]</sup> of foolish people.
As servants of God, you should live as free people, but do not use this freedom to cover up your wrongdoing.
Show <sup>[proper]</sup> respect for everyone. Love your <sup>[Christian]</sup> brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup>. Have an awe-inspiring respect for God. Show honor to the king.
Slaves, submit yourselves to your masters and show them respect, not just the ones who are good <sup>[to you]</sup> and treat you well, but also to those who are harsh toward you.
For those of you who put up with such suffering unjustly, because of your close relationship to God, are to be commended.
But it is no credit to you if you put up with a beating you deserve. But if you patiently tolerate suffering for doing good <sup>[things]</sup>, this is pleasing to God.
<sup>[After all]</sup>, this is what you were called to do, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you would follow in His steps.
<sup>[For]</sup> He neither sinned nor used deceitful words.

When He was spoken to with harsh insults, He did not reply in the same way. When He was made to suffer, He did not threaten <sup>[His tormentors]</sup>, but instead entrusted Himself to God, <sup>[whom He knew]</sup> would make a righteous judgment <sup>[in the matter]</sup>.

Jesus took upon Himself our sins <sup>[when]</sup> His body was placed upon the cross so that we, <sup>[through]</sup> dying to <sup>[i.e., giving up]</sup> the sinful life, would live for righteousness. You were healed <sup>[from your sins]</sup> by His being brutally treated.
For you people were wandering away like sheep, but have now returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

In the same way <sup>[that Jesus was submissive]</sup>, you wives should submit yourselves to your own husbands so that, even though they may not obey the <sup>[Gospel]</sup> message <sup>[they have heard]</sup> they may, without your talking <sup>[to them about it]</sup>,
be won by observing your godly and respectful behavior.

Do not let your <sup>[adornment]</sup> be <sup>[the decorations]</sup> of the physical body, such as elaborate hair-dos, the wearing of golden <sup>[jewelry]</sup> and the wearing of <sup>[stunning]</sup> outfits.
But <sup>[the kind of adornment you should display is]</sup> that of the inner person, with its enduring qualities of a meek and calm spirit, which are extremely valuable in God’s eyes.
For this is the way holy women in ancient times decorated themselves. Their hope was in God and they were known for being in subjection to their own husbands.

Sarah obeyed her husband Abraham, even referring to him <sup>[in respectful terms such]</sup> as “sir.” You <sup>[wives]</sup> are her <sup>[spiritual]</sup> descendants if you also do what is right and are not fearful <sup>[that things in life will turn out badly]</sup>.

<sup>[You]</sup> husbands, in the same way, should live with your wives in an understanding way, recognizing their feminine nature as being physically weaker than yours, and showing special honor to them because of it. <sup>[Realize]</sup> that sharing your lives together is a gracious gift <sup>[from God]</sup>. <sup>[If you have the right relationship with them]</sup> it will help avoid problems in your prayer lives.

Now to summarize: All of you should think alike. Be sympathetically understanding <sup>[toward one another]</sup>. Be loving toward <sup>[your]</sup> brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup>. Be tender-hearted and humble-minded.

Do not repay a wrong action with <sup>[another]</sup> wrong action, nor abusive <sup>[language]</sup> with <sup>[additional]</sup> abuse, but rather be a blessing <sup>[to one another]</sup>. For the purpose of your being called <sup>[by God]</sup> was so that you could receive a blessing <sup>[from Him eventually]</sup>.
For <sup>[Psalm 39:12 says]</sup>, “The person who loves <sup>[his]</sup> life and wants to have happy days, should avoid saying anything bad or deceptive.
He should turn away from wrong living and do what is good. He should desire peace and do whatever it takes to maintain it.
For the Lord’s eyes see what the righteous person does and He hears their <sup>[earnest]</sup> prayers, but the Lord looks with disapproval on the person who does wrong.”
And who do you think would <sup>[try to]</sup> hurt you if you become enthusiastic for doing what is right?
But even if you have to suffer for doing what was right, you still have <sup>[God’s]</sup> blessing. So, do not be afraid of the threats <sup>[of your persecutors]</sup>, and do not let it worry you.

But instead, set aside a place in your hearts where Christ is <sup>[in full control as]</sup> Lord, and be prepared always to give a suitable reply to every person who asks you for a reason why you have an inner hope. But <sup>[be sure you do it]</sup> in a meek and respectful way, however.

May your conscience be able to so approve of your conduct that, even if you are ridiculed, those doing it will <sup>[eventually]</sup> have to be ashamed <sup>[of themselves]</sup> for criticizing your Christian behavior.
For, if it is God’s will that you should suffer, it is better <sup>[for you]</sup> that it be on account of doing right than doing wrong.

For Christ suffered once for people’s sins <sup>[to be forgiven]</sup>. <sup>[It was the case of]</sup> a righteous Man suffering for unrighteous people, so that He could bring you <sup>[or, “us”]</sup> to God <sup>[for salvation]</sup>. <sup>[Though]</sup> He <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup> was put to death physically, He was brought back to life by the Holy Spirit <sup>[Note: Some translations say “in spirit”]</sup>.
It was by the Holy Spirit that He went to preach <sup>[See Eph. 2:17]</sup> to the <sup>[now departed]</sup> spirits <sup>[of pre-Flood people who are now]</sup> in prison <sup>[i.e., Hades]</sup>.

These people had disobeyed <sup>[Noah’s preaching, See II Pet. 2:5]</sup> back when God’s patience waited <sup>[for them to repent, See Gen. 6:3]</sup>, during the days of Noah, when the ship was being constructed. It was by that means <sup>[i.e., the ship floating them to safety through the Flood waters]</sup> that a few persons, eight of them, were saved <sup>[from destruction]</sup> through water.

This water prefigured immersion <sup>[into Christ]</sup>, which now also saves you, not by removing dirt from the <sup>[physical]</sup> body, but <sup>[by serving]</sup> as an appeal <sup>[or, “pledge”]</sup> to God for a clear conscience through the raising of Jesus Christ from the dead.
He has <sup>[now]</sup> gone to heaven, and is <sup>[seated]</sup> at the right side of God, where the angels and <sup>[other]</sup> authorities and powers have been subjected to Him.

Therefore, since Christ has suffered physically, you people should fortify yourselves with the same attitude He had. For the person who has suffered physically <sup>[i.e., in standing for Christ]</sup> has quit sinning.
So then, you should no longer live the rest of your lives <sup>[to satisfy]</sup> the sinful desires of your body, but to do what God wants.

For the time you have <sup>[already]</sup> spent in the past <sup>[i.e., as Gentile unbelievers]</sup> was long enough to have practiced unrestrained, indecent conduct, improper sexual cravings, riotous living, wild parties, drunken orgies and disgusting idol worship.
Now, those people you used to run around with cannot understand why you have stopped living such wasteful lives, so they just “badmouth” you.
But, they will have to answer to God, who will judge both those who are now alive and those who have already died, <sup>[for the way they have lived]</sup>.

This is the purpose that the Gospel was preached to those who have died physically <sup>[for the faith]</sup>: It was so that, even though they had been judged physically <sup>[i.e., by suffering death at the hands of men]</sup>, they could still live spiritually in the presence of God.
But the end of everything <sup>[as far as your lives are concerned]</sup> is near, so be sensible, and sober <sup>[so as to be]</sup> prayerful.
Above everything else, have a warm love for one another, for such a love keeps many sins from occurring <sup>[between you]</sup>.
Extend hospitality to one another without complaining <sup>[that you have to]</sup>.

As each of you has received a gift <sup>[from God]</sup>, you should make use of it <sup>[in the body]</sup>, so that you will be <sup>[good]</sup> caretakers of the many unearned ways that God favors you.

If someone’s gift is speaking, he should <sup>[be sure to]</sup> say only what God wants him to. If a person’s gift is serving, he should do so by the strength provided by God <sup>[who supplies all such gifts]</sup>. This is so that, in using all gifts, God may be honored through Jesus Christ, to whom belongs honor and power forever and ever. May it be so.

Loved ones, do not be surprised that you people are undergoing extremely difficult trials, as though something strange were happening to you, for it is <sup>[only]</sup> a test <sup>[of your faith]</sup>.
You should rejoice that you are sharing some of what Christ has suffered, so that you will get to rejoice all the more when He returns in His splendor.
If you are spoken against for being Christians, you have God’s blessing, because His glorious Holy Spirit rests upon you <sup>[to provide comfort, See John 14:16]</sup>.
<sup>[However]</sup>, you should not have to suffer for murdering, stealing, doing evil or interfering in the affairs of others.

But if a person should have to suffer because he is a Christian, he should not be ashamed <sup>[of it]</sup>, but instead, he should bring honor to God by <sup>[wearing]</sup> the name <sup>[of Christ]</sup>.

For the time has <sup>[now]</sup> come for the house of God <sup>[the church, See I Tim. 3:15]</sup> to begin receiving judgment <sup>[i.e., by undergoing persecution]</sup>, and if it starts with us <sup>[Christians]</sup>, what will be the <sup>[ultimate]</sup> end of people who do not obey the Gospel of God?

And if people who are trying to do right <sup>[i.e., Christians]</sup> are saved through <sup>[suffering]</sup> such difficulties, where will the ungodly and sinful person end up, <sup>[if not in hell]</sup>?

Therefore, those who <sup>[are allowed to]</sup> suffer in harmony with God’s <sup>[permissive]</sup> will, should commit their souls to a trustworthy Creator, <sup>[who will take everything into account]</sup>, while they go on <sup>[trying]</sup> to do what is right.

So, the elders in the various places where you live, are to receive the following exhortation. (I too, am an elder and have witnessed the sufferings of Christ, and am also assured of partaking of the glorious <sup>[salvation, 1:5]</sup> that will someday be revealed).

You should serve as shepherds of God’s flock <sup>[i.e., the church]</sup>, among whom you minister, providing careful oversight <sup>[of the people]</sup>, not because you feel forced to, but because you want to, as this is in accordance with the way God wants it to be. Do not <sup>[serve as elders]</sup> simply out of a desire for the income you receive, but because your heart is in it.
Do not act as dictators over the people you are assigned to care for, but <sup>[rather]</sup> serve as examples of how they ought to live.

And when the Supreme Shepherd <sup>[i.e., Jesus]</sup> comes back, you will receive <sup>[for your effort]</sup> a glorious crown <sup>[of reward]</sup> that will never fade away <sup>[Note: The idea of “fading away” is suggested by the wilting of the laurel wreath bestowed on the winner of an athletic contest]</sup>.

And in the same way, you younger <sup>[Christians]</sup> should be <sup>[respectfully]</sup> submissive to the older ones. And all of you should <sup>[be willing to]</sup> humbly serve one another, for God stands opposed to <sup>[the efforts of]</sup> proud people, but extends His unearned favor to those <sup>[who serve]</sup> with humility.
Therefore, be humble <sup>[in your actions]</sup> before God’s powerful authority, so that you may be exalted <sup>[by Him]</sup> at the proper time.
Place all your anxious cares upon God <sup>[in prayer]</sup>, because He genuinely cares about you.
Be sensible and alert; your enemy the devil is prowling around like a roaring <sup>[i.e., hungry]</sup> lion looking for someone to <sup>[kill and]</sup> eat.

You should withstand his efforts by maintaining a strong faith <sup>[in God]</sup>, remembering that your <sup>[Christian]</sup> brothers in <sup>[the rest of]</sup> the world are experiencing <sup>[and enduring]</sup> suffering similar to yours.

And the God who bestows every unearned favor <sup>[upon His people]</sup>, who invited you to partake of His eternal splendor through Christ Jesus, will restore, confirm, strengthen and stabilize you people <sup>[in the faith]</sup>, after you have gone through a brief time of suffering.
May God have total authority over everything <sup>[including your circumstances]</sup> forever. May it be so.

This brief message is being inscribed <sup>[or delivered]</sup> by Silvanus, whom I consider to be a faithful brother. <sup>[In it]</sup> I am exhorting you and giving testimony that it is a message about God’s genuine unearned favor upon you. So, continue to follow <sup>[and obey]</sup> what it says.
Your sister <sup>[church]</sup> here in Babylon, <sup>[also]</sup> chosen <sup>[by God]</sup>, as you have been, sends her greetings along with my <sup>[spiritual]</sup> son Mark.

Greet one another lovingly, <sup>[expressing it]</sup> with a kiss <sup>[of brotherly affection]</sup>. May there be peace <sup>[of heart]</sup> to all those <sup>[there]</sup> who are in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> Christ.

<sup>[This letter is being written by]</sup> Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ, to those who have received a faith just as precious as ours, through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ.
<sup>[May God’s]</sup> unearned favor and peace be yours in abundance, through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.

God’s divine power has given us everything <sup>[necessary]</sup> for <sup>[abundant]</sup> life and godly living, through the knowledge of Him <sup>[i.e., God]</sup>, who called us by His own splendor and goodness.

By these <sup>[blessings]</sup> He has given to us the precious and tremendous promises, so that you people may share in His divine nature, <sup>[after]</sup> having escaped the world’s corruption, caused by evil desires.
And for this very reason, you people should make every effort to develop goodness along with your faith, and knowledge along with your goodness,
and self-control along with your knowledge, and endurance along with your self-control, and godliness along with your endurance,
and brotherly kindness along with your godliness, and with your brotherly kindness, develop love.
For if you people have a lot of these <sup>[qualities]</sup> they will keep you from being unproductive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
But, anyone who lacks these qualities is <sup>[spiritually]</sup> blind and shortsighted, and has forgotten that he has been cleansed <sup>[i.e., forgiven]</sup> of his past sins.

So, brothers, be diligent to make sure of your calling and election <sup>[i.e., of your status as being selected by God]</sup>, for when you do, you will never stumble <sup>[and fall away from God]</sup>.
For in this way, you will be provided with a rich welcome into the never ending kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ <sup>[i.e., in heaven]</sup>.

Therefore, I intend to constantly remind you people about these things <sup>[i.e., those mentioned in verses 5-11]</sup>, even though you <sup>[already]</sup> know them, and have been established in the truth you now possess.
But I consider it the right thing to do, as long as I am in this “tent” <sup>[Note: This is an allusion to his still being alive]</sup>, to stir up your memory <sup>[of these things]</sup>,
knowing that <sup>[I will]</sup> soon put off my “tent” <sup>[i.e., I will soon die]</sup>, even as our Lord Jesus Christ made clear to me.
And I will make every effort to see that, after I leave <sup>[i.e., die]</sup>, you will always be able to recall the things <sup>[I said to you]</sup>.

For we did not use cleverly conceived stories when we told you about the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, because we were eyewitnesses of His Majesty <sup>[i.e., the apostles had personally seen the miraculous power of Jesus]</sup>.
For Jesus had received honor and glory from God, the Father, when <sup>[God’s]</sup> voice came to Him from the Majestic Glory saying, “This is my dearly loved Son, and I am very pleased with Him.”
And we heard this voice coming from heaven when we were with Him in the holy mountain.

And we have the message of the prophets <sup>[confirmed to us]</sup> as more certain <sup>[than ever]</sup>. So, you will do well to pay careful attention to it, as <sup>[you would]</sup> to a lamp in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts <sup>[Note: This probably refers to the beginning of the Gospel age, when people would become enlightened to its message]</sup>.

Above all, <sup>[you should realize]</sup> that no prophecy of Scripture originates from a person’s own initiative <sup>[Note: This passage is discussing the source of Scripture, not the understanding of it]</sup>.
For prophecy was never produced by the will of man, but people spoke from God, as they were carried along <sup>[i.e., inspired]</sup> by the Holy Spirit.

But just as there were false prophets among the people before, there will also be false teachers among you <sup>[Christians]</sup>. They will privately teach harmful doctrines that destroy people <sup>[spiritually]</sup>, by even denying <sup>[that Jesus is]</sup> the Master, <sup>[and thereby]</sup> destroying themselves quickly.
And many people will follow their unrestrained, indecent conduct, which will result in the true way <sup>[of God]</sup> being spoken against.

And through greedy desires and deceptive words they will exploit you <sup>[for evil purposes]</sup>. But the judgment that they have had coming for a long time will not delay, but will eventually awaken <sup>[i.e., arrive]</sup> to bring destruction upon them.
For if God did not spare sinning angels, but cast them in chains into deep, dark dungeons to await judgment;
and if He did not spare the ancient world of Noah’s day, when He brought a flood on those ungodly people (though Noah, a preacher of righteousness, and seven other people, were saved from it);

and if God also condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah by reducing them to ashes, thereby condemning them to catastrophic destruction, and making them an example to other ungodly people in the future;
and if He rescued the righteous man Lot, who had become very disturbed by unrestrained wickedness <sup>[of the people there in Sodom]</sup>
(For, being a righteous man living among them, Lot’s righteous spirit was very disturbed every day by the wickedness he heard and saw);

then the Lord <sup>[surely]</sup> knows how to rescue godly people from trying situations, and to keep unrighteous people under <sup>[a sentence of]</sup> punishment <sup>[to be received]</sup> on the Judgment Day.

<sup>[This fate will come upon]</sup> those people especially who corrupt themselves with physicaldesires and despise being ruled by others. They are daring, self-willed, and are not afraid to speak against glorious beings <sup>[i.e., angels]</sup>,
while <sup>[those]</sup> angels, who are greater in strength and power than they, refuse to speak against such authorities in condemnation before the Lord.

However, these people are like unreasonable, ignorant creatures of instinct, born as animals which are <sup>[to be]</sup> caught and destroyed. They speak out against things about which they are ignorant. In <sup>[the process of]</sup> destroying others, they will certainly be destroyed themselves.

They suffer wrong themselves for doing wrong to other people. They consider it <sup>[especially]</sup> pleasurable to carry on their drunken reveling, <sup>[even]</sup> during the daytime. They become blots and blemishes <sup>[i.e., like spoiled food]</sup> in your fellowship meal, as they revel in their deceitful behavior.

Their eyes are filled with sexual lust, and they are never satisfied when it comes to sinning. They entice unstable people and have hearts trained to be greedy <sup>[i.e., they continually desire the possessions of others]</sup>. <sup>[In this condition]</sup> they are under God’s curse.
They strayed away <sup>[from God]</sup> by taking the wrong road like Balaam, the son of Bosor did, who loved the reward for doing wrong.
But Balaam was rebuked for his own wrongdoing by a speechless donkey, which spoke out in a man’s voice and prevented the prophet from continuing his foolishness.
Such people are like springs that give no water and clouds driven by the winds. <sup>[Only]</sup> the blackest darkness awaits them.

<sup>[Although]</sup> their talk is big, what they say is worthless, and by <sup>[offering people]</sup> fleshly desires <sup>[i.e., sexual gratification]</sup>, they lead people astray, who are barely escaping from the error of the world.
<sup>[Although]</sup> these people promise “liberty,” they themselves are slaves to corrupting influences, for people are slaves to whatever controls them.

For if people who have escaped from the defiling practices of the world by coming to know our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, become tangled up in them again, and are overcome by them, these people are worse off than before they were converted.
It would have been better for them if they had not known the right way <sup>[to God]</sup> than, after knowing it, to turn back from the sacred message that had been presented to them.

It has happened to them like the true proverb <sup>[Prov. 26:11 says]</sup>, “The dog <sup>[throws up, and then]</sup> goes back and eats its own vomit,” and “The hog that was all cleaned up <sup>[goes back]</sup> to wallowing in the mud.”
This is now, my dearly loved ones, the second letter I am writing to you; in both of them, as a reminder, I want to stir up your sincere thoughts
to remember the words spoken to you in the past by the holy prophets, and what the Lord and Savior commanded you through your apostles.
First of all, you should understand that, in the final days, mockers with evil desires will come <sup>[to you]</sup> saying, mockingly,
“Why has the promise of a coming Messiah <sup>[not been fulfilled yet]</sup>? After all, since the time our ancestors died, everything has continued the same, even since the beginning of creation!”
But, these people intentionally forget that the heavens, which existed long ago, and the earth, which was formed out of water and by water, <sup>[were created]</sup> by the word of God.
<sup>[And]</sup> it was by water that the world, which existed at that time, perished in a great flood <sup>[of Noah’s day]</sup>!

But, by means of the same word <sup>[of God]</sup>, the present heavens and earth have been reserved for fire, awaiting the judgment and destruction <sup>[by God]</sup>, along with <sup>[its]</sup> ungodly people.
But do not forget this one thing, my dearly loved ones, that as the Lord <sup>[counts time]</sup>, one day is like a thousand years, and a thousand years are like one day.

The Lord is not slow about keeping His promise, like some people are, but is <sup>[simply]</sup> being patient with you, since He does not want anyone to be destroyed, but He wants everyone to repent <sup>[i.e., change their hearts and lives]</sup>.

But the day of the Lord will come as a thief <sup>[i.e., unexpectedly]</sup>. At that time the heavens will pass away with a loud noise and the elements <sup>[Note: This refers either to the heavenly bodies or to the material components of the universe]</sup>, will be dissolved with intense heat. And the earth and its works <sup>[i.e., its activities]</sup> will be burned up. <sup>[Note: Some translations render this phrase as it is in some ancient manuscripts, “will be disclosed,” or “will be laid bare”]</sup>.
Since all these things are <sup>[certain]</sup> to be destroyed, what kind of persons should you be, <sup>[if not like those]</sup> living holy lives, and in a godly manner,

<sup>[while]</sup> looking forward to, and earnestly desiring the day of God to come, when the heavens will be set ablaze and dissolved by fire, and the elements <sup>[See verse 10]</sup> will melt with intense heat.
But, since God has promised it, we look forward to new heavens and a new earth, where righteousness will prevail.
So, my dearly loved ones, while you are waiting for these things <sup>[to happen]</sup>, do your best to be found at peace with God, pure and blameless in His sight.

And consider <sup>[that]</sup> the patience of the Lord means <sup>[further opportunities for]</sup> salvation, <sup>[just]</sup> as our dearly loved brother Paul also wrote to you, using the wisdom given to him by God.

He spoke about these things in all of his letters, which contain some parts that are difficult to understand, and <sup>[because of this]</sup>, ignorant and unstable people twist <sup>[his writings]</sup>, just as they also do to the other Scriptures, to their own destruction.

Therefore, dearly loved ones, since you know about these things ahead of time, be on your guard so that you are not led astray by the error of lawless people, and <sup>[thus]</sup> fall from your own stability <sup>[i.e., your safe or secure position in Christ]</sup>.
But grow in the unearned favor and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. May He be honored both now and forever. May it be so.

We <sup>[apostles]</sup> proclaim to you <sup>[the truth about Jesus]</sup>, who existed from the beginning <sup>[of time]</sup>. We have heard <sup>[Him]</sup> and seen <sup>[Him]</sup> with our <sup>[own]</sup> eyes. We looked at Him, and <sup>[even]</sup> touched Him with our hands. <sup>[See John 20:20]</sup>. <sup>[So, we proclaim that]</sup> He is the word of life <sup>[i.e., the life-giving Word]</sup>!

Truly, this “Life” was made known to us and we have <sup>[actually]</sup> seen it <sup>[i.e., Him]</sup>, and so we testify and proclaim to you <sup>[that He is]</sup> “the Eternal Life,” who was with the Father and was made known to us <sup>[apostles]</sup>.

We declare to you what we have seen and heard <sup>[concerning Christ]</sup>, so that you also may share a relationship with us. And indeed, our fellowship is with the Father and with His Son Jesus Christ.
We are writing to you about these things, so that <sup>[together]</sup> our joy may be <sup>[completely]</sup> full.

And this is the message we heard from Him, and <sup>[now want to]</sup> announce to you: God is light <sup>[i.e. righteousness]</sup> and in His <sup>[very nature]</sup> there is no trace of darkness <sup>[i.e., evil]</sup>.
If we claim to share a relationship with God and yet live in darkness <sup>[i.e., in a sinful way]</sup>, we are lying and are not living a truthful life.

But, if we live in the light <sup>[of righteousness]</sup> just like God <sup>[constantly]</sup> lives in the light <sup>[of righteousness]</sup>, we can truly share a relationship with one another and <sup>[can know that]</sup> the blood of Jesus, God’s Son, continues to cleanse <sup>[our spirits]</sup> from all of our sins.
If we claim to have no sinful practices <sup>[in our lives]</sup>, we are self-deceived and are not being truthful about the matter.

<sup>[But]</sup> if we confess our sins <sup>[to God]</sup>, He is faithful <sup>[to His promise]</sup> and righteous <sup>[in His judgment]</sup>, and will forgive us of those sins and cleanse us from all of our wrongdoing.

If we claim that we have not committed sins, we are <sup>[in effect]</sup> calling God a liar <sup>[i.e., because He says we have]</sup> and His word <sup>[i.e., the real truth about the matter]</sup> is not in our hearts.

My little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, I am writing this to you so that you will not sin anymore. But if anyone does sin, we have Someone to go to the Father on our behalf; it is Jesus Christ, the <sup>[only]</sup> righteous One.

And He is the One who provides a “covering over” <sup>[thereby the forgiveness]</sup> of our sins <sup>[See Heb. 2:17; Dan. 9:24; II Chron. 29:24]</sup>, and not of ours <sup>[who are already Christians]</sup> only, but also of the sins of the people of the whole world.
And here is how we can be assured that we know Jesus <sup>[i.e., that we are saved]</sup>: <sup>[It is]</sup> by obeying what He commands us.
The person who says, “I have come to know Him,” but does not obey what He commands, is lying <sup>[by such a profession]</sup>, and the truth <sup>[about the matter]</sup> is not in his heart.

But the person who obeys what His teaching requires, truly in this person love for God has been made complete. This is <sup>[another way]</sup> to be sure that we are in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Him:
The person who claims he is continuing to live in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Jesus should also live the way He did.

My dearly loved ones, I am not writing to you about a new commandment, but about an old one. <sup>[It is the one]</sup> you had learned about from the beginning. <sup>[Note: This is the commandment to love one another (3:1; II John 5) which Jesus had taught his disciples during His earthly ministry. See Mark 12:31]</sup>. This old commandment is the word <sup>[of God]</sup> which you heard <sup>[previously]</sup>.

Yet, I am <sup>[also]</sup> writing to you about a new commandment <sup>[Note: This commandment was to love one another as Jesus loved them. See John 13:34]</sup>; its truth is <sup>[being demonstrated]</sup> by Christ <sup>[perfectly]</sup> and <sup>[also]</sup> by you people <sup>[in a lesser measure]</sup>, because the darkness <sup>[of sin]</sup> is passing away, and the real light <sup>[of righteousness]</sup> is already shining <sup>[in people’s lives]</sup>.

The person who claims to be living in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> the light <sup>[i.e., by obeying God’s truth]</sup>, and <sup>[yet]</sup> hates his brother is still <sup>[living]</sup> in the darkness <sup>[of sin]</sup>.

<sup>[However]</sup>, the person who loves his brother continues to live in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> the light <sup>[of God’s truth]</sup>, and such a person is not the cause of others stumbling <sup>[over his example]</sup>.

But the person who hates his brother is in the darkness <sup>[of sin]</sup> and he lives in <sup>[harmony with]</sup> that darkness, and he does not know where he is heading, because the darkness <sup>[of sin]</sup> has blinded his eyes <sup>[to his spiritual condition]</sup>.
I am writing to you, little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, because you have been forgiven of your sins for <sup>[the sake of]</sup> Christ’s name.

I am writing to you fathers, because you have known Him <sup>[i.e., Christ]</sup>, <sup>[who has existed]</sup> from the beginning <sup>[See John 1:1]</sup>. I am writing to you young men, because you have overcome the <sup>[power of the]</sup> evil one <sup>[i.e., through faith they had gained victory over Satan, See 5:4]</sup>.

I have written to you little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, because you have known the Father <sup>[as your God]</sup>. I have written to you fathers, because you have known Him <sup>[i.e., Christ]</sup>, <sup>[who has existed]</sup> from the beginning. <sup>[See verse 13]</sup>. I have written to you young men, because you are <sup>[spiritually]</sup> strong and the word of God continues to live in your hearts, and you have overcome the <sup>[power of the]</sup> evil one <sup>[See verse 13]</sup>.

You should not love the <sup>[godless]</sup> world, nor the things <sup>[i.e., the materialistic values]</sup> that are part of this <sup>[present]</sup> age. If any person loves the world, a <sup>[genuine]</sup> love for the Father is not in his heart.

For all that makes up the world, namely, the improper desires of the physical body, the improper desires for what is seen, and the <sup>[boastful]</sup> pride over life’s possessions; these are not from the Father, but from the <sup>[godless]</sup> world.
And the world, with its <sup>[physical and material]</sup> desires, is passing away, but the person who does what God wants will continue to live forever.

Dear children, it is <sup>[now]</sup> the last hour <sup>[i.e., the final period of God’s dealing with people on earth]</sup>, and since you have heard that an antichrist <sup>[i.e., a person who is against Christ]</sup> is coming, even now <sup>[you should be aware that]</sup> many antichrists have <sup>[already]</sup> come. This is <sup>[one way]</sup> we know that this is the last hour.

These antichrists left our group <sup>[i.e., withdrew their fellowship from us]</sup>, but they were not <sup>[really a part]</sup> of us, for if they had been, they would have continued <sup>[to participate]</sup> with us. But, by their leaving us, it proved that none of them were <sup>[really]</sup> a part of our group <sup>[after all]</sup>.

But, you people have a “special gift” from the Holy One <sup>[Note: The Greek word for “special gift” is “charisma” and probably refers to the Holy Spirit (See Acts 2:38); “Holy One” probably refers to Jesus, (See Acts 2:27; 3:14)] </sup>, and all of you know <sup>[the truth]</sup>.
I did not write to you people because you are ignorant of the truth <sup>[about these matters]</sup>, but because you do know it, and because nothing false belongs to the true teaching.

Who is the liar? It is the person who denies that Jesus <sup>[truly]</sup> is the Christ <sup>[i.e., God’s specially chosen One]</sup>. This person is the antichrist <sup>[See verse 18]</sup>; he is the one who denies <sup>[the truth about]</sup> the Father and the Son.

Every person who denies <sup>[the truth about]</sup> the Son <sup>[See 4:2-3]</sup>, does not have the Father <sup>[living in his heart]</sup>; but, the person who confesses <sup>[the truth about]</sup> the Son has the Father <sup>[living in his heart]</sup> also.

You people should continue <sup>[to practice]</sup> what you heard from the beginning <sup>[i.e., to love one another, See verses 7- 11]</sup>. If you do, then you also will continue in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> the Son, and with the Father.

And this is the promise which Christ <sup>[or, “God”]</sup> has given to us: <sup>[It is]</sup> never ending life. <sup>[Note: This passage may mean that God promised us “the Eternal Life,” that is, Christ, See John 3:16]</sup>.
I have written these things to you people concerning those who are trying to lead you astray <sup>[from the truth]</sup>.

And this is important to you: The “special gift,” which you received from God <sup>[See verse 20]</sup>, continues to live in your hearts, <sup>[so that]</sup> you do not need anyone to teach you <sup>[anything different]</sup>. But, since this same “special gift” teaches you concerning everything that is true, and <sup>[what it teaches]</sup> is not false, you should continue to live in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ.

And now, little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, continue to live in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ, so that when He appears, we may have boldness and not have <sup>[any reason]</sup> to be ashamed in front of Him at His <sup>[second]</sup> coming.

If you know that Christ is <sup>[truly]</sup> righteous <sup>[verse 1]</sup>, then you should also know that everyone who does what is right has been <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived <sup>[and eventually born]</sup> by God <sup>[See 5:18]</sup>.

Look at how much love the Father has shown to us <sup>[Christians]</sup>, that we should be called His children! But, that is what we <sup>[really]</sup> are! The reason the people of the world do not know who we are is because they do not know who God <sup>[really]</sup> is.

Dearly loved ones, we are God’s children now, but it has not yet been revealed <sup>[to us]</sup> what we will be <sup>[in the future life, Luke 20:34-36]</sup>. But, we do know this, however, that when Jesus is revealed <sup>[i.e., at His second coming]</sup>, we will be like Him <sup>[I Cor. 15:49]</sup>, for we will see what He really looks like!
And every person who has set his hope on this <sup>[i.e., of being like Jesus]</sup>, will live <sup>[as]</sup> purely <sup>[as possible]</sup>, just as He <sup>[i.e., Jesus Himself]</sup> is pure.
Every person who practices a life of sin is also breaking <sup>[God’s]</sup> law, for sin is law-breaking.
But, you <sup>[should]</sup> know that Christ appeared <sup>[on earth]</sup> to take away <sup>[our]</sup> sins, and that there is no sin in His life <sup>[See II Cor. 5:21]</sup>.

No person who lives in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> Christ will continue living a sinful life; the one who keeps on sinning <sup>[however]</sup>, has not seen Him or known Him <sup>[as his personal Savior]</sup>.

Little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, do not allow anyone to lead you astray <sup>[from the truth]</sup>; the person who continues to do what is right is <sup>[considered by God as]</sup> righteous, even as Christ is righteous.

<sup>[But]</sup> the person who continues to live a sinful life belongs to the devil, because the devil has continued to sin since the beginning <sup>[See Gen. 3]</sup>. For this is the reason that the Son of God came <sup>[into the world]</sup>; it was to destroy the works <sup>[i.e., the influence of]</sup> the devil <sup>[in people’s lives]</sup>.

No one who has been <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived <sup>[and eventually born]</sup> by God continues to live a sinful life, because God’s seed <sup>[i.e., His word, See Luke 8:11]</sup> continues to live in his heart, so he cannot <sup>[continue a life of]</sup> sin, because he has been conceived by God.

Here is how the children of God and the children of the devil can be recognized: All those who do not continue to do what is right do not belong to God. Also, the person who does not continue to love his brother <sup>[or sister does not belong to God]</sup>.
<sup>[Now]</sup> this is the message that you people heard from the beginning <sup>[of Christ’s ministry]</sup>, that we should love one another <sup>[See Mark 12:31]</sup>,

not <sup>[hate each other]</sup> the way Cain did, who belonged to the evil one <sup>[i.e., Satan]</sup>, when he murdered his brother <sup>[Abel]</sup>. And why did he murder him? It was because his <sup>[own]</sup> actions were evil and his brother’s were righteous <sup>[See Heb. 11:4]</sup>.
So, do not be surprised, brothers, if the people of the world hate you.

<sup>[For]</sup> we <sup>[can]</sup> know that we have passed out of <sup>[the realm of spiritual]</sup> death and into <sup>[spiritual]</sup> life, because we <sup>[show]</sup> love for the brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup>. The person who does not love <sup>[them]</sup> continues to be <sup>[spiritually]</sup> dead.
Every person who hates his brother is a murderer <sup>[in God’s sight]</sup>; and you <sup>[surely]</sup> know that no murderer has <sup>[the hope of]</sup> never ending life remaining in his heart.

Here is how we can know what <sup>[God’s]</sup> genuine love really is: It was <sup>[demonstrated by]</sup> Jesus laying down His life <sup>[on the cross]</sup> for us. And <sup>[because of that]</sup> we <sup>[too]</sup> should <sup>[be willing to]</sup> lay down our lives for <sup>[the sake of helping]</sup> our brothers <sup>[and sisters]</sup>.

Now whoever has <sup>[a sufficient amount of]</sup> life’s possessions and <sup>[then]</sup> observes one of his <sup>[Christian]</sup> brothers who does not have enough to get by on, but does not feel sorry for him <sup>[i.e., enough to help him out]</sup>, surely a love for God does not continue to live in that person’s heart.

Little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, we should not <sup>[merely]</sup> claim to love <sup>[people]</sup>, or <sup>[even just]</sup> talk about it, but <sup>[we should love]</sup> by what we do <sup>[for them]</sup> in a genuine way.
And this is how we will know that we are following <sup>[God’s]</sup> truth, and will have assurance <sup>[of it]</sup> in our hearts and before God <sup>[as well]</sup>.
But, if our hearts condemn us <sup>[for not demonstrating God’s love for others]</sup>, surely God, who is greater than our hearts <sup>[will also condemn us]</sup>, because He knows all things.
Loved ones, if our hearts do not condemn us <sup>[i.e., because we are showing love by helping the needy]</sup>, we can have boldness before God <sup>[in prayer for our own needs]</sup>;

and we can be assured of receiving from God whatever we ask Him for, because we are obeying His commands <sup>[i.e., especially the one about loving others]</sup> and are practicing what is pleasing to Him.
And this is His commandment: We should believe in the name of His Son Jesus Christ <sup>[i.e., the person of Christ]</sup>, and love one another, just as He has commanded us to do.

And God lives in the heart of the person who <sup>[continually]</sup> obeys His commands, and he lives in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God. And here is <sup>[another]</sup> way we know that God lives in our hearts: It is by <sup>[means of]</sup> the Holy Spirit, whom God gave to us. <sup>[See Rom. 8:9, 11; James 4:5; I Cor. 6:19]</sup>.

Loved ones, do not believe <sup>[the message of]</sup> every spirit <sup>[i.e., every person claiming to be a teacher]</sup>, but test the spirits <sup>[i.e., require proof that they and their message are genuine]</sup> to see whether they represent God <sup>[or not]</sup>, because many false prophets have begun circulating throughout the world.

Here is how to know <sup>[who is speaking from]</sup> the Holy Spirit of God: Every spirit <sup>[i.e., person]</sup> who proclaims that Jesus Christ has come <sup>[to earth]</sup> in a physical body is <sup>[speaking]</sup> from God. <sup>[See I Cor. 12:3]</sup>.

But every spirit who does not proclaim <sup>[this truth about]</sup> Jesus is not <sup>[speaking]</sup> from God. And this <sup>[person]</sup> represents the spirit of the antichrist <sup>[See 2:18-22; II John 7]</sup>, whom you have heard would come. Well, now he is already here!

<sup>[But]</sup>, you people are <sup>[followers]</sup> of God, little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, and you have overcome them <sup>[i.e., these false teachers]</sup> because He who is in your lives <sup>[i.e., the Holy Spirit, See 3:24]</sup> is greater <sup>[i.e., more powerful]</sup> than he who is in <sup>[i.e., dominates]</sup> the world <sup>[i.e., Satan. See II Cor. 4:4]</sup>.
These <sup>[false teachers]</sup> are part of the world, so <sup>[naturally]</sup> they speak <sup>[a message endorsed]</sup> by the world and the world listens to them.

We <sup>[who preach the true Gospel]</sup> represent God; the person who knows God listens to our message; the one who does not belong to God does not listen to us. This is how we can know <sup>[the difference between those speaking by]</sup> the Holy Spirit of truth and <sup>[those speaking by]</sup> the spirit of <sup>[Satan’s]</sup> error.

Loved ones, we should have love for one another, because love comes from <sup>[the very nature of]</sup> God, and every person who <sup>[continually]</sup> loves <sup>[others]</sup> has been <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived by God <sup>[See 2:29; 3:9]</sup>, and knows <sup>[he is in fellowship with]</sup> God.
The person who does not love <sup>[others]</sup> does not know God <sup>[as Savior]</sup>, for <sup>[the very nature of]</sup> God is love.
Here is the way God’s love for us was demonstrated; He sent His one and only Son into the world so that we could have <sup>[spiritual]</sup> life through Him.

Here is <sup>[the essence of]</sup> love: <sup>[It is]</sup> not that we loved God <sup>[first]</sup>, but that He loved us <sup>[See Rom. 5:6-8]</sup> and sent His Son to be a “covering over” <sup>[see 2:2]</sup> of our sins.
Loved ones, if God loved us that much, we should also love one another.
No person has gazed upon God <sup>[at any time]</sup>; <sup>[but]</sup> if we love one another, God <sup>[continually]</sup> lives in our hearts, and His love is made complete within us.

This is <sup>[one way]</sup> to know we live in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God and that He lives in our hearts: <sup>[It is]</sup> because He has given us His Holy Spirit <sup>[to live within us, See 3:24]</sup>.
And we <sup>[apostles]</sup> have seen and are <sup>[reliable]</sup> witnesses that the Father has sent the Son to be the Savior of the world <sup>[See 1:2; 2:2]</sup>.
Whoever proclaims that Jesus is the Son of God, God <sup>[continually]</sup> lives in that person’s heart, and he lives in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God <sup>[See 2:23]</sup>.

And we have known <sup>[with certainty]</sup> and have believed <sup>[with assurance]</sup> that God has truly loved us. God’s <sup>[very nature]</sup> is love and the person who <sup>[continually]</sup> loves <sup>[Him and others]</sup> lives in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> God <sup>[continually]</sup>, and God <sup>[continually]</sup> lives in that person’s heart.

Here is what a complete love will do for us: It will give us boldness in <sup>[facing]</sup> the judgment day, because the way Jesus acts <sup>[regarding love]</sup> is the way we should act <sup>[while]</sup> in this world.

There is no <sup>[reason for]</sup> fear if one <sup>[really]</sup> loves, but a complete love removes fear <sup>[from us]</sup> because fear produces punishment <sup>[i.e., mental anxiety]</sup>, and the person who is <sup>[always]</sup> afraid does not have a complete love.
We love <sup>[God and others]</sup> because God has first loved us.

If a person says, “I love God,” yet <sup>[in reality]</sup> hates his brother, he is a liar. For the person who does not love his brother, whom he has seen, cannot <sup>[possibly]</sup> love God, whom he has never seen.

And this is the <sup>[greatest]</sup> commandment <sup>[See Matt. 2:37-38]</sup> we have received from Him: <sup>[It requires]</sup> that the person who <sup>[claims to]</sup> love God should also love his brother.

Every person who believes that Jesus is the Christ <sup>[i.e., God’s specially chosen one]</sup> has been <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived <sup>[and eventually born]</sup> by God. And everyone who <sup>[continually]</sup> loves God, who did the conceiving, also <sup>[must continually]</sup> love the person whom God has <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived.
This is <sup>[one]</sup> way we know that we love God’s <sup>[spiritually reborn]</sup> children: <sup>[It is]</sup> when we love God and obey what He commands us to do.
For this is <sup>[real evidence of]</sup> our love for God: <sup>[It is]</sup> obeying what He commands us to do. And obeying His commands does not cause us any grief <sup>[or, unpleasantness]</sup>.

For whoever <sup>[Note: The Greek says “whatever”]</sup> is <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived <sup>[and eventually born]</sup> by God will overcome <sup>[the evil influence of]</sup> the world. And this is <sup>[how]</sup> that victory can be gained over the world: <sup>[It is through]</sup> our faith <sup>[in God]</sup>.
And who is the person who can overcome <sup>[the evil influence of]</sup> the world? <sup>[It is]</sup> the one who <sup>[truly]</sup> believes that Jesus is the Son of God.

This Jesus is the one whose coming <sup>[to earth was introduced]</sup> by water <sup>[at His immersion. See Matt. 3:13]</sup> and <sup>[completed]</sup> by blood <sup>[at His crucifixion, John 19:17-37]</sup>. He did not come by the water only, but by the water and by the blood. And it is the Holy Spirit who gives testimony <sup>[at both of these events]</sup>, because the Holy Spirit is <sup>[always]</sup> the truth. <sup>[See John 16:13]</sup>.
For there are three who give their testimony <sup>[about Jesus]</sup>:

The Holy Spirit <sup>[through His Word]</sup>, and the water <sup>[through His immersion]</sup> and the blood <sup>[through His crucifixion]</sup>; and these three agree together <sup>[in giving testimony about Him]</sup>.

If we <sup>[are willing to]</sup> accept the testimony of people about matters, <sup>[surely we ought to accept that]</sup> the testimony of God is more reliable; because the testimony of God is that He has presented <sup>[confirming]</sup> testimony concerning <sup>[the identity and mission of]</sup> His Son.

The person who <sup>[sincerely]</sup> believes in God’s Son has the evidence <sup>[of it]</sup> within his heart, <sup>[confirming God’s testimony]</sup>. The person who does not believe God <sup>[i.e., what He says about Jesus]</sup>, accuses Him of lying, because he has not accepted the <sup>[irrefutable]</sup> testimony of God concerning <sup>[the identity and mission of]</sup> His Son.
And this is the testimony <sup>[of God]</sup>: He gave to us <sup>[the hope of]</sup> never ending life, and this life is <sup>[available through fellowship]</sup> in His Son.
The person who has the Son <sup>[in his heart]</sup> possesses this “life;” the one who does not have the Son of God does not possess this “life.”

I have written these things to you people, who believe in the name <sup>[i.e., in the person and work]</sup> of the Son of God, so that you can have assurance that you have <sup>[the hope of]</sup> never ending life. <sup>[See Titus 1:2; 3:7; Mark 10:30]</sup>.

And this is <sup>[how we can express]</sup> boldness in our relationship with God: Whatever we ask <sup>[of God, in prayer]</sup>, so long as it is in harmony with His will for us, <sup>[we know]</sup> He hears our request.
And if we can be sure that He hears our request for whatever we ask <sup>[of Him]</sup>, we can <sup>[also]</sup> be certain that we <sup>[will]</sup> have whatever we have requested from Him.

If anyone <sup>[of you]</sup> observes his brother <sup>[or sister]</sup> sinning <sup>[in a way that would not lead]</sup> to <sup>[spiritual]</sup> death, he should ask <sup>[God, in prayer, to help that person]</sup>, and God will give him <sup>[spiritual]</sup> life, <sup>[that is]</sup>, to such ones, provided their sin is not leading to <sup>[spiritual]</sup> death. There is such a sin that leads to <sup>[spiritual]</sup> death. <sup>[See Heb. 6:4-6; I Tim. 5:6; Heb. 10:26-27; II Pet. 2:20- 22]</sup>. I am not suggesting that you should ask <sup>[God, in prayer]</sup> about such a situation.
All wrongdoing is sinful, and <sup>[yet]</sup> there is a situation where <sup>[a person’s]</sup> sin does not lead <sup>[him]</sup> to <sup>[spiritual]</sup> death.

We know that every person who has been <sup>[spiritually]</sup> conceived by God <sup>[and eventually born]</sup> does not continue in <sup>[a life of]</sup> sin, but God’s only conceived One <sup>[i.e., His Son]</sup> keeps him <sup>[from continuing to sin]</sup>, and the evil one <sup>[i.e., Satan]</sup> does not continue to touch him <sup>[i.e., continue to control his life]</sup>.
We <sup>[also]</sup> know that we are <sup>[children]</sup> of God and that the people of the whole world lie under <sup>[the control of]</sup> the evil one <sup>[i.e., Satan]</sup>.

And <sup>[in addition]</sup>, we know that the Son of God has come <sup>[in a physical body, See 4:2]</sup> and has given us understanding, so that we can know the True One <sup>[i.e., God]</sup>, and that we are in <sup>[fellowship with]</sup> the True One, even His Son Jesus Christ. This Son is the true God and eternal life.
Little children <sup>[i.e., dear ones]</sup>, protect yourselves from <sup>[worshiping]</sup> idols.

<sup>[This is being written by]</sup> the elder to the lady chosen <sup>[by God]</sup>, and to her children <sup>[Note: The terms “lady” and “children” here refer to a congregation and the Christians who belong to it. See I Peter 5:13; II John 13]</sup>, whom I love in the truth. <sup>[Note: This phrase probably means “whom I truly love”]</sup>. And I am not the only one <sup>[to love you]</sup>, but all those <sup>[other congregations]</sup> who know the truth <sup>[of God]</sup>, also love you.
<sup>[We all love you]</sup> because of the truth which continues to live in our hearts and will be with us forever.
Unearned favor, mercy and peace will be with us from God, the Father, and from Jesus Christ, the Father’s Son, in truth and love <sup>[i.e., truth and love will accompany the other blessings]</sup>.

I was very happy when I learned that my children <sup>[i.e., members of the congregation there]</sup> are living in <sup>[harmony with the]</sup> truth, just as we have been commanded to do by the Father.

And now I ask you, lady <sup>[See verse 1]</sup>, not as though writing a new commandment to you, but <sup>[I wanted to remind you of]</sup> one that we had <sup>[heard]</sup> from the beginning <sup>[of Christ’s ministry]</sup>: <sup>[It is]</sup> that we should love one another.

And this is <sup>[the true meaning of]</sup> love: That we should live in obedience to God’s commandments. And this is the commandment, just as you heard from the beginning, that you should live in it <sup>[i.e., live a life of love]</sup>.

For many deceivers have begun circulating in the world; they are those who do not confess that Jesus Christ has come <sup>[to earth]</sup> in a physical body. Such a person is the deceiver and the antichrist <sup>[See I John 2:18f]</sup>.

Pay close attention to yourselves, so that you do not lose what we <sup>[Note: Some Greek manuscripts say “you”]</sup> worked for <sup>[i.e., the spiritual blessings you have acquired]</sup>, but <sup>[continue faithful]</sup> so that you will receive a full reward. <sup>[See Luke 12:47-48; James 3:1; Matt. 20:1-15]</sup>.

Every person who does not continue to follow the teaching of Christ, but goes beyond it, does not have God <sup>[in his life]</sup>. <sup>[But]</sup>, the person who continues to follow in the teaching <sup>[of Christ]</sup> has both the Father and the Son <sup>[in his life]</sup>.

If anyone comes to you <sup>[i.e., to your meetings]</sup> who does not bring this teaching <sup>[about Christ]</sup>, you should not welcome him into your home or give him <sup>[any]</sup> greetings <sup>[i.e., suggesting approval or endorsement]</sup>.
For the person who greets him <sup>[with approval]</sup> participates in his evil deeds <sup>[i.e., in his false teaching]</sup>.

I have many <sup>[more]</sup> things to write to you about, but will not do so with paper and ink, since I hope to visit you <sup>[soon]</sup> and speak to you in person, so that our joy may be complete.
The children <sup>[i.e., members]</sup> of your chosen sister <sup>[i.e., church]</sup> send you their greetings. <sup>[See verse 1]</sup>.
<sup>[This is being written by]</sup> the elder to my dear <sup>[friend]</sup> Gaius, whom I love in the truth. <sup>[Note: This probably means “whom I truly love”]</sup>.

<sup>[My]</sup> dearly loved <sup>[friend]</sup>, I am praying that you will do well in everything <sup>[materially (?)]</sup>, and that you will have <sup>[good]</sup> health, just as your soul <sup>[i.e., your spiritual life]</sup> is doing well.

For I was very happy when some of the brothers came and told me that you are being <sup>[faithful]</sup> in the truth <sup>[i.e., the word of God]</sup>, and that you are living according to it, <sup>[as well]</sup>.
I do not have any greater joy than to hear that my <sup>[spiritual]</sup> children are living according to the truth.

Dear <sup>[friend]</sup>, you are accomplishing a faithful work whenever you do something for the brothers <sup>[traveling in the Lord’s work]</sup>, especially when they are strangers <sup>[to you]</sup>.

These people testify about your love <sup>[for them]</sup> before the <sup>[assembled]</sup> church. You will be doing a good thing to send them on their journey in a way that is pleasing to God <sup>[i.e., by furnishing them with travel expenses for their ministry]</sup>.
For they went out, <sup>[ministering]</sup> for the sake of <sup>[Jesus’]</sup> name, without taking <sup>[any money]</sup> from Gentiles <sup>[i.e., non-Christians]</sup>.
Therefore, we should show hospitality to such men, so that we can be fellow-workers <sup>[with them]</sup> in <sup>[the cause of]</sup> the truth.

I wrote a letter to the church <sup>[there]</sup>, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first <sup>[in importance]</sup> among the members <sup>[of the body there]</sup>, does not receive us <sup>[i.e., he did not listen to or acknowledge the authority of us apostles]</sup>.

So, if I come <sup>[to visit you]</sup>, I will remind you of what he is doing, <sup>[such as]</sup> using wicked words to talk nonsense against us <sup>[apostles]</sup>.
